1.text: [1a]Blessed is the manWho does not walkIn the counsel of the wicked,Nor stand on the path of sinners,Nor [b]sit in the seat of mockers;
Rather his [a]delight is in the [1]law of Jehovah,And in His law he [b]meditates by day and by night.
And he will be like a [a]treeTransplanted beside streams of water,Which yields its fruit in its season,And whose foliage does not wither;And everything he does [1]prospers.
The wicked are not so,But are like [a]chaff,Which the wind drives away.
Therefore the wicked will not [a]stand in the judgment;Nor the sinners, in the assembly of the righteous.
For Jehovah [a]knows the way of the righteous,But the way of the wicked will perish.

2.text: [1a]Why are the [2b]nations in an uproar,And why do the [2]peoples contemplate a vain thing?
The kings of the earth take their stand,And the rulers sit in [a]counsel together,Against Jehovah and against His [1b]Anointed:
Let us break apart their [a]bondsAnd cast their ropes away from us.
He who sits in the heavens [a]laughs;The [1]Lord has them in derision.
Then He will speak to them in His anger,And in His burning wrath He will terrify them:
But I have installed My [1]KingUpon [2a]Zion, My holy mountain.
I will recount the decree of Jehovah;He said to Me: [1a]You are My Son;[b]Today I have begotten You.
Ask of Me,And I will give the nations as Your inheritanceAnd the [a]limits of the earth as Your possession.
You will break them with an [a]iron rod;You will shatter them like a potter’s [b]vessel.
[1]Now therefore, O kings, be prudent;Take the admonition, O judges of the earth.
Serve Jehovah with [a]fear,And rejoice with trembling.

[1a]Kiss the SonLest He be angry and you perish from the way;For His anger may suddenly be kindled.[2b]Blessed are all those who [1c]take refuge in Him.Psa 3:Title A [a]Psalm of David, when he [b]fled from Absalom his son

3.text: [1]Jehovah, how many are my adversaries!Many are those who rise up against me!
Many are those who say of my soul,There is no salvation for him in God! [1]Selah
But You, O Jehovah, are a [a]shield around me,My [b]glory and the One who lifts up my head.
With my voice I call out to Jehovah,And He answers me from His [a]holy mountain. Selah
I lay down and slept;I awoke, for Jehovah sustains me.
I will not fear the myriads of the peopleWho have set themselves against me all around.
Arise, O Jehovah;Save me, O my God!Oh that you would [1]strike all my enemies on the cheek;And break the teeth of the wicked.
[a]Salvation is of Jehovah:Your blessing be upon Your people. SelahPsa 4:Title To the choir director: on the stringed instruments. A [a]Psalm of David

4.text: When I call out, answer me,O God of my [1]righteousness;Make room for me when I am in straits;Be gracious to me and hear my prayer.
O sons of men, how long will my glory be made a reproach?How long will you love vanity, will you seek a lie? Selah
But know that Jehovah [1]sets apart the faithful man for Himself.Jehovah hears when I call out to Him.
[a]Be angry, yet do not sin;Consider in your heart upon your bed,And be silent. Selah
Offer sacrifices of righteousness,And trust in Jehovah.
Many are those who say, Who will show us any good?Lift up upon us the [a]light of Your countenance, O Jehovah.
You have put joy in my heart,More than when the [1]grain and new wine of others abound.
In peace I will both lie down and sleep,For You alone, O Jehovah, cause me to dwell in safety.Psa 5:Title To the choir director: for the wind instruments. A [a]Psalm of David

5.text: To my words give ear, O Jehovah;Consider my moaning.
Give heed to the sound of my cry,My [a]King and my God;For to You do I pray.
O Jehovah, in the morningYou will hear my voice;In the morning I will set forth my words in order to You,And I will watch.
[1]For You are not a God who delights in wickedness;Evil will not sojourn with You.
Those who boast will not standBefore Your eyes;You hate all workers of iniquity.
You will destroy those who speak lies.Jehovah abhors a man of bloodshed and deceit.
But as for me, in the abundance of Your lovingkindnessI will come into Your [a]house;I will bow down toward Your [b]holy templeIn fear of You.
O Jehovah, lead me in Your righteousnessOn account of those who lie in wait for me;Make Your way straight before me.
For there is no truth in their mouth;Their inward part is corruption itself;[a]Their throat is an open [b]grave;They [c]flatter with their tongue.
[1]Hold them guilty, O God;Let them fall by their own counsels;Because of the multitude of their transgressions thrust them out,For they have rebelled against You.
But let all who [a]take refuge in You rejoice;Let them shout for joy forever;And may You spread a cover over them;And those who love Your nameWill exult in You.
For You will bless the righteous man, O Jehovah;You will surround him with favor as with a shield.Psa 6:Title To the choir director: on the stringed instruments, on the octave. A [a]Psalm of David

6.text: [a]O Jehovah, do not rebuke me in Your anger,Nor [1]chasten me in Your burning wrath.
Be gracious to me, O Jehovah, for I am languishing;Heal me, O Jehovah, for my bones are shaken,
And my soul is greatly shaken.But as for You, O Jehovah, how long?
Return, O Jehovah; deliver my soul;Save me for Your lovingkindness’ sake.
For in [a]death there is no remembrance of You;In [1]Sheol who can give thanks to You?
I am weary with my groaning;Every night I cause my bed to swim;With my tears I dissolve my couch.
My eye is wasted because of grief;It has aged because of all those who vex me.
Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity,For Jehovah has heard the sound of my weeping.
Jehovah has heard my supplication;Jehovah receives my prayer.

All my enemies will be ashamed and greatly dismayed;They will turn back; they will be put to shame suddenly.Psa 7:Title A [*]Shiggaion [a]of David, which he sang to Jehovah on account of Cush the [b]Benjaminite

7.text: O Jehovah my God, in You do I [a]take refuge.Save me from all who pursue me, and deliver me;
Lest he tear my soul like a lion,Tearing it apart without anyone to deliver it.
O Jehovah my God, if I have done this,If there is [1]unrighteousness in my hands,
If I have recompensed with evil him who was at peace with me[1](Rather, I have delivered him who vexed me without cause);
Let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake me,And let him trample my life to the groundAnd lay my glory down in the dust. Selah
Arise, O Jehovah, in Your anger;Lift Yourself up against the overflowing wrath of those who vex me,And awake for me: You have commanded judgment.
And let the assembly of the peoples surround You,And over it return on high.
Jehovah will execute [a]judgment on the peoples;Judge me, O Jehovah, according to my righteousnessAnd according to my integrity that is with me.
Oh, let the evil of the wicked come to an end,But establish the righteous man.For the righteous God[a]Tries the hearts and the inward parts.
My shield is with God,Who saves the upright in heart.
God is a righteous [a]JudgeAnd a God who has indignation every day.
If one does not turn, He will whet His sword.He bends His bow and makes it ready.
And He prepares instruments of death against him;He has made His arrows fiery shafts.
Behold, the evil man travails with iniquity;He conceives mischief and brings forth falsehood.
He digs a pit and hollows it out,Then he falls into the hole that he has made.
His mischief returns upon his own head,And upon the crown of his own head his violence comes down.
I will give thanks to Jehovah according to His righteousness,And I will sing psalms to the name of Jehovah the Most High.Psa 8:Title To the choir director: on the [*]gittith. A [a]Psalm of David

8.text: [1]O Jehovah our Lord,How [2]excellent is Your [a]nameIn all the earth,You who have set Your glory over the heavens!
[a]Out of the mouths of [1b]babes and [1]sucklingsYou have [2]established strengthBecause of Your [3]adversaries,To stop the [3]enemy and the [3]avenger.
When I see Your [1]heavens, the works of Your fingers,[a]The moon and the stars, which You have ordained,
[a]What is [1]mortal man, that You [2]remember him,And the son of man, that You [2]visit him?
You have made [1]Him a little lower than [2]angelsAnd have [3]crowned Him with glory and honor.
For You have caused Him to [1a]rule over the works of Your hands;You have put [b]all things [c]under His feet:
All sheep and oxen,As well as the beasts of the field,
The birds of heaven and the fish of the sea,Whatever passes through the paths of the seas.
[1]O Jehovah our Lord,How excellent is Your nameIn all the earth!Psa 9:Title To the choir director: [*]according to muth-labben. A [a]Psalm of David

9.text: [1]I will give thanks to You, O Jehovah, with all my heart;I will tell out all Your wonders.
I will rejoice and exult in You;I will sing psalms to Your name, O Most High.
When my [1]enemies turn back,They stumble and perish at Your presence.
For You have upheld my right and my cause;You sit on the throne, [1]judging righteously.
You have rebuked the nations; You have destroyed the wicked;You have blotted out their name forever and ever.
The enemies are finished; they are in perpetual ruins;And You have uprooted their cities; the very memory of them has perished.
But Jehovah sits enthroned forever;He has established His throne for judgment.
And it is He who [a]judges the world with righteousness;He executes judgment on the peoples with equity.
And Jehovah is a [a]high retreat for the oppressed,A high retreat in times of distress.
And those who know Your name put their trust in You,For You have not forsaken those who seek You, O Jehovah.
Sing psalms to Jehovah, the [a]Inhabitant of [b]Zion;Declare His deeds among the peoples,
For He who avenges bloodshed remembers those who were slain;He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
Be gracious to me, O Jehovah; look on my affliction from those who hate me,You who raise me from the gates of death,
That I may tell out all Your praisesAnd, in the gates of the [a]daughter of Zion, [b]exult in Your [c]salvation.
The nations have sunk in the pit that they have made;In the net that they hid, their own foot is caught.
Jehovah has made Himself known; He has executed [1]justice;The wicked have been snared in the work of their hands. [2]Higgaion. Selah
The wicked will be turned back to Sheol,All the nations who forget God.
For not always will the needy be forgotten,Nor will the hope of the afflicted perish forever.
Arise, O Jehovah! Let not mortal man prevail;Let the nations be judged in Your sight.
Put them in fear, O Jehovah;Let the nations know that they are mortal men. Selah

10.text: [1]Why, O Jehovah, do You stand [a]far off?Why do You hide Yourself in times of distress?
Arrogantly the wicked hotly pursue the poor—May they be caught in the plots that they devise!
For the wicked man boasts of the desire of his soul,And the rapacious man curses, even despises, Jehovah.
The wicked man, according to the haughtiness of his countenance,Says, He will not require it;All his thoughts are this: [a]There is no God!
His ways succeed at all times;Your judgments are on high, out of his sight;As for all his adversaries, he snorts at them.
He has said in his heart, I will not be shaken;I will be in no trouble [1]forever.
His [a]mouth is full of curses and of deceit and oppression;Under his tongue are mischief and iniquity.
He sits in the ambushes of the open villages;In hidden places he murders the innocent;His eyes secretly watch for the unfortunate.
He lurks in secret like a lion in its covert;He lurks ready to seize the poor;He seizes the poor, drawing them up in his net.
He stoops, he crouches,And the unfortunate fall by his mighty claws.
He has said in his heart, God has forgotten;He has hidden His face; He will never [a]see this.
Arise, O Jehovah; O God, lift up Your hand.Do not forget the poor.
Why does the wicked man despise GodAnd say in his heart, You will not require it?
You do see! For You observe mischief and vexation,To requite it with Your own hand.To You the unfortunate one [a]commits his cause;You have been the help of the orphan.
Break the arm of the wicked man and the evil doer;Seek out his wickedness until You find no more.
Jehovah is [a]King forever and ever;The nations have perished from His land.
You have heard the desire of the lowly, O Jehovah;You will establish their heart; You will cause Your ears to listen,
In order to execute justice for the orphan and the oppressed one,That the mortal man of the earth may terrorize no longer.Psa 11:Title To the choir director. [a]Of David

11.text: In Jehovah have I [a]taken refuge. How can you say to my soul,Flee to [1]the mountains like a bird?
For behold, the wicked bend the bow;They ready their arrow on the stringTo shoot in the dark at the upright in heart.
When the foundations are destroyed,What will the righteous man do?
Jehovah is in His holy [a]temple;Jehovah—His [b]throne is in heaven.His eyes behold;His [1]eyelids try the sons of men.
Jehovah tries the righteous man;But the wicked man and him who loves violence, His soul hates.
He will rain down snares upon the wicked;[a]Fire and brimstone and a scorching wind will be the portion of their cup.
For Jehovah is righteous; He loves righteous deeds.The upright man will behold His face.Psa 12:Title To the choir director: on the octave. A [a]Psalm of David

12.text: Save, O Jehovah; for the faithful man is no more,For the trustworthy have vanished from among the sons of men.
Each man speaks falsehood with his neighbor;With flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
May Jehovah cut off all the flattering lips,The [a]tongue speaking great things,
Those who said, With our tongue we will prevail;Our lips are our own: Who is our lord?
Because of the devastation of the poor, because of the sighing of the needy,I will now arise, says Jehovah;I will set him in the safety that he longs for.
The words of Jehovah are [a]pure words,Silver refined in a furnace on the earth,Purified seven times.
You, O Jehovah, will keep them;You will guard them from this generation forever.
The wicked go about all around,While vileness is exalted among the sons of men.Psa 13:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

13.text: How long, O Jehovah? Will You forget me continually?How long will You hide Your face from me?
How long will I bear concern in my soulAnd sorrow in my heart day after day?How long will my enemy be exalted over me?
Consider and answer me, O Jehovah my God;Enlighten my eyes lest I [a]sleep the sleep of death,
Lest my enemy say, I have prevailed against him,And my adversaries exult when I am shaken.
But I trust in Your lovingkindness;My heart will exult in Your [a]salvation;
I will sing to Jehovah,For He has dealt bountifully with me.Psa 14:Title To the choir director. [a]Of David

14.text: [a]The fool has said in his heart,[b]There is no God.They are corrupt; they commit abominable deeds;[c]There is none who does good.
[a]Jehovah looked down from heavenUpon the sons of men[b]To see if there was anyone who had insight,Who seeks after God.
They have all turned aside;They are together perverse.There is none who does good;There is not even one.
Have they no knowledge, all the workers of iniquity,Who eat up my people as they would eat up breadAnd do not call upon Jehovah?
There they were in great fear,For God is among the generation of the righteous.
You put the counsel of the afflicted to shame,But Jehovah is their [a]refuge.
Oh that the [a]salvation of Israel might come forth from [b]Zion!When Jehovah [c]turns the captivity of His people,Jacob will exult, Israel will rejoice.Psa 15:Title A [a]Psalm of David

15.text: [1]O Jehovah, who may sojourn in Your [2]tent?[a]Who may dwell on Your [b]holy mountain?
[1]He who walks in integrityAnd does righteousnessAnd speaks truth from his heart.
He does not slander with his tongue;He does not do evil to his friend,Nor does he take up a reproach against his neighbor.
In his eyes a reprobate is despised,But he honors those who fear Jehovah.Should he swear to his harm,He does not change.
He does not lend his money on interest,Nor accept a bribe against the innocent.He who does these things will not be shaken forever.Psa 16:Title A [*]Michtam [a]of David

16.text: [1]Preserve me, O God, for I [2a]take refuge in You.
[1]I say to Jehovah, [2]You are my Lord;[a]No good have I beyond You;
As for the [1]saints who are on the earth, they are the excellent;All my [a]delight is in them.
The sorrows of them who bartered for some other [1]god will be multiplied;Their drink offerings of blood I will not offer,Nor will I take up their names upon my lips.
Jehovah is the [1a]portion of my inheritance and of my [b]cup;You maintain my lot.
The [1a]measuring lines have fallen on pleasant places for me;Indeed the inheritance is beautiful to me.
I will bless Jehovah, who [1]counsels me;Indeed in the nights my [a]inward parts instruct me.
[a]I have [1]set Jehovah before me continually;Because He is at my right hand, I shall not be shaken.
Therefore my heart rejoices and my [1a]glory exults;Even my flesh [2]dwells securely.
For You will not abandon my [1]soul to [a]Sheol,Nor let Your [b]Holy One see the [2c]pit.
You will make known to me the [1a]path of [b]life;In Your [2c]presence is fullness of joy;At Your [2]right hand there are [d]pleasures forever.Psa 17:Title A Prayer [a]of David

17.text: [1]Hear, O Jehovah, what is righteous;Give heed to my cry;Give ear to my prayer,Which is not made with lips of deceit.
May a judgment for me come forth from Your presence;May Your eyes regard equity.
You have [a]examined my heart; You have visited me by night;You have [b]tried me: You have found nothing.I have resolved that my mouth will not transgress.
Regarding the works of man, by the word of Your lips,I have kept myself from the paths of the violent man.
My steps have held fast to Your [a]tracks;My footsteps have not slipped.
I call on You, for You will answer me, O God;Incline Your ear to me; hear my speaking.
Wondrously display Your [1]lovingkindness,You who save by Your right handThose who take [a]refuge in You from them who rise up against them.
Guard me like the [a]pupil of Your eye;In the [b]shadow of Your wings hide me
From the presence of the wicked who oppress me,From my deadly enemies, who close in upon me.
They have shut up their fat hearts;With their mouths they speak arrogantly.
Now they have surrounded [1]us in our steps;They have fixed their eyes on casting us down to the ground.
He is like a lion that is eager to tear,And like a young lion lurking in hidden places.
Arise, O Jehovah, [1]confront him; bring him down.With Your sword rescue my soul from the wicked man,

From mortal men with Your hand, O Jehovah,From mortal men of the world, whose portion is in this life,And whose belly You fill with Your treasure;They are satisfied with children,And they leave their abundance for their little ones.

As for me, in [1]righteousness I will behold Your face;When I [a]awake, I will be satisfied with Your [1b]likeness.Psa 18:Title To the choir director. [a]Of David, the servant of Jehovah, [b]who [*]spoke to Jehovah the words of this song on the day when Jehovah delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul, and he said:

18.text: I love You, O Jehovah, my [1a]strength.
[a]Jehovah is my crag and my [b]fortress and my Deliverer;My God, my [c]rock, in whom I [d]take refuge;My [e]shield and the [f]horn of my salvation, my [g]high retreat.
I called upon Jehovah, who is worthy of praise,And from my enemies I was saved.
The cords of death encompassed me;And the torrents of destruction assailed me.
The cords of Sheol surrounded me;The snares of death confronted me.
In my distress I [1]called upon JehovahAnd cried out to my God.He heard my voice from His [a]temple,And my cry before Him came to His ears.
Then the earth shook and quaked,And the foundations of the mountains trembled and shook about,For He was furious.
Smoke went up from His nostrils,And fire from His mouth devoured;Coals blazed forth from Him.
He [a]bowed the heavens and descended,And deep darkness was under His feet.
He rode upon a [a]cherub and did fly;He darted upon the [b]wings of the wind.
He made darkness His hiding place, His pavilion around Him:Dark waterclouds, thick clouds of the skies.
Out of the brightness before Him there broke through His clouds[a]Hail and fiery coals.
Then Jehovah thundered in heaven,And the Most High uttered His voice[1].
And He sent forth His arrows and scattered [1]my enemies;And He shot forth lightning bolts and discomfited them.
The channels of the [1a]sea were seen,And the foundations of the habitable land were laid bareAt Your rebuke, O Jehovah,At the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
He reached forth from on high; He took me;He drew me out of great waters.
He delivered me from my strong enemyAnd from those who hate me, for they were too mighty for me.
They confronted me in the day of my calamity,But Jehovah became my support.
He brought me forth to a place broad and free;He rescued me, for He took delight in me.
Jehovah has [1]recompensed me according to my righteousness;According to the cleanness of my hands He has [1]repaid me.
For I have kept the ways of JehovahAnd have not acted wickedly by turning away from my God.
For all His judgments were before me,And I did not turn His statutes away from me.
I was perfect toward Him,And I kept myself from my iniquity.
Therefore Jehovah recompensed me according to my righteousness,According to the cleanness of my hands in His sight.
[a]With the faithful You show Yourself faithful,With a perfect man You show Yourself perfect,
With the pure You show Yourself pure,And with the perverse You show Yourself contrary.
For it is You who save the afflicted people,But the haughty eyes You bring down;
For it is You who light my [a]lamp;Jehovah my God lights up my darkness;
For by You I can run up against a troop;Indeed by my God I can [a]leap over a wall.
As for God—His way is perfect;The word of Jehovah is [a]tried.He is a [b]shield to all who [c]take refuge in Him.
For who is God other than Jehovah,And who is a [a]rock but our God?
The God who girds me with strengthAnd makes my way perfect,
Who makes my feet like [a]hinds’ feetAnd sets me on my [b]high places,
Who [a]teaches my hands to wage warSo that my arms may bend a bronze bow.
And You have given me the shield of Your salvation,And Your right hand supports me;And Your condescending gentleness has made me great.
You have broadened the places of my steps under me,And my feet have not slipped.
I pursued my enemies and overtook them,And I did not turn back until they were consumed.
I shattered them, so that they were not able to rise up;They fell under my feet.
And You girded me with strength for war;You brought down under me those who rose up against me.
You also made my enemies turn their back to me,And I annihilated those who hated me.
They cried out, but there was no one to save them;They cried out to Jehovah, but He did not answer them.
Then I beat them like dust before the wind;Like the mud of the streets I [1]pounded them.
You rescued me from the strivings of the people;You have made me the [a]head of the nations;A people whom I had [b]not known serves me.
At the mere hearing of a report, they obey me;Foreigners come [a]cringing to me;
Foreigners are discouragedAnd come [a]quaking out of their fortresses.
Jehovah lives; and blessed be my [a]rock,And exalted be the [b]God of my salvation,
The God who executes vengeance for meAnd subdues peoples under me,
Who rescues me from my enemies.Indeed You exalted me above those who rise up against me;From the violent man You delivered me.
[a]Therefore I give thanks to You among the nations, O Jehovah;And I will [b]sing psalms to Your name.
It is He who magnifies salvation to His kingAnd executes lovingkindness to His anointed,To David and to his [1]seed [a]forever.Psa 19:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

19.text: The [a]heavens [b]declare the glory of God,And the [c]expanse proclaims the work of His hands.
Day to day pours forth speech,And night to night tells out knowledge.
There is no speech and there are no words;Their voice is not heard.
[a]In all the earth their line has gone forth,And their words to the end of the world.In [1]the heavens He has made a tent for the sun,
And he, like a [a]bridegroom, goes forth from his canopy;He rejoices like a mighty man running the course.
His going forth is from the end of the heavens,And his circuit is unto their ends;And there is nothing hidden from his heat.
The [1]law of Jehovah is [a]perfect,Restoring the soul;The [1]testimony of Jehovah is faithful,[b]Making the simple wise;
The precepts of Jehovah are right,Making the heart [a]joyous;The commandment of Jehovah is clear,[b]Enlightening the eyes;
The fear of Jehovah is pure,Enduring forever;The [a]judgments of Jehovah are truthAnd altogether righteous.
More to be desired are they than [a]gold,Even much refined gold;[b]Sweeter also than honeyAnd the drippings of the honeycomb.
Moreover by them Your servant is warned;In keeping them there is much reward.
Who can discern his errors?[1]Clear me of my secret faults.
Also keep back Your servant from presumptuous sins;Do not let them have dominion over me;Then I will be blameless and clearedOf great transgression.
May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heartBe acceptable before You,O Jehovah, my [a]rock and my [b]Redeemer.Psa 20:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

20.text: [1]May Jehovah answer you in the day of trouble;May the name of the God of Jacob set you on high.
May He send you help from the [a]sanctuaryAnd support you from [b]Zion.
May He remember all your meal offeringsAnd accept your burnt offering. Selah
May He give you according to your heart’s desireAnd fulfill all your intention.
May we shout victoriously in Your salvationAnd raise the [a]banner in the name of our God.May Jehovah fulfill all your petitions.
Now I knowThat Jehovah saves His [a]anointed;He will answer him from His holy heavenWith mighty acts of salvation from His right hand.
Some boast in [a]chariots, and some in horses;But we boast in the [b]name of Jehovah our God.
They are humbled and fallen,But we have risen and stand upright.
[1a]Save, O Jehovah!May the king answer us when we call.Psa 21:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

21.text: O Jehovah, in Your strength the king rejoices,And in Your salvation how greatly does he exult.
You have given him his heart’s desireAnd have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah
For You meet him with blessings of goodness;You have set a crown of refined gold on his head.
He asked [a]life of You; You granted it to him:Length of days forever and ever.
His glory is great in Your salvation;You have laid majesty and splendor upon him;
For You have made him to be blessings forever;You have made him glad with rejoicing in Your presence.
For the king trusts in Jehovah;And because of the lovingkindness of the Most High, he is not shaken.
Your hand finds all Your enemies;Your right hand finds those who hate You.
You make them like a furnace of fireWhen Your presence appears.Jehovah swallows them in His anger,And fire devours them.
You destroy their fruit from the earth,And their seed from among the sons of men.
Although they intend evil against You,And they think up a scheme, they will not prevail.
For You make them turn their back;You aim with Your bowstrings at their faces.
Be [1]exalted, O Jehovah, in Your strength;We will sing and psalm Your might.Psa 22:Title To the choir director: according to the [*]hind of the dawn. A [a]Psalm of David

22.text: [1a]My God, my God, why have You [2]forsaken me?[3]Why are You so far from saving me,From the words of my groaning?
O my God, I call out in the day, but You do not answer;And in the night, but there is no rest for me.
But You are holy, You who sit enthronedUpon the praises of Israel.
In You our fathers [a]trusted;They trusted and You rescued them.
To You they cried out and were delivered;In You they trusted and were not put to shame.
[1]But I am a [a]worm and not a man,A reproach of men and one [b]despised by the people.
All who see me [a]deride me;They sneer at me; they [b]shake their heads, saying,
[1a]He [b]committed himself to Jehovah; let Him rescue him;Let Him deliver him since He takes pleasure in him.
[1]But You are the One who drew me forth from the [a]womb,Who made me trust while at my mother’s breasts.
I was cast upon You from [a]birth;From my mother’s womb You have been my God.
Do not be far from me,For trouble is near,For there is none to help me.
[1]Many [a]bulls surround me;The mighty bulls of Bashan encompass me.
They open their mouth at me,Like a ravening and roaring [a]lion.
I am poured out like water,And all my [a]bones are out of joint.My heart is like wax;It is melted within [1]me.
My strength is [a]dried up like a shard,And my tongue is stuck to my jaws;[1]You have put me in the dust of death.
For [a]dogs surround me;A company of evildoers encloses me;[1]They [b]pierce my [c]hands and feet.
I count all my [a]bones;They look, they [b]stare at me.
They divide my garments to themselves,And for my clothing they [a]cast lots.
[1]But You, O Jehovah, be not far off;O my help, hasten to aid me.
Deliver my soul from the sword,[1a]My precious life from the power of the [b]dog;
Save me from the mouth of the [a]lion;Indeed [1]while I am in the horns of wild oxen, answer me.
[a]I will declare Your name to my [1b]brothers;In the midst of the [2]assembly I will praise [3]You.
You who fear Jehovah, [1]praise Him!All you seed of Jacob, glorify Him!And stand in awe of Him, all you seed of Israel!
For He has not despised nor detestedThe affliction of him who is afflicted;And He has not hidden His face from him;But when he cried out to Him, He heard.
From You comes my praise in the great assembly;My vows will I pay before those who fear Him.
The lowly will eat and be [a]satisfied;Those who seek after Jehovah will praise Him—May your hearts live forever!
All the [a]ends of the earthWill remember and return to Jehovah,And all families of the [1]nationsWill worship before You;
For the [a]kingdom is Jehovah’s,And [1]He rules among the nations.
All the flourishing of the earthWill eat and worship.All who go down into the dust will [a]bow down before Him,Even he who does not keep his soul alive.
A [1a]seed will serve Him;That which concerns the Lord will be told to a coming [b]generation.
They will come and declare His righteousnessTo a people yet to be born, that He has done this.Psa 23:Title A [a]Psalm of David

23.text: He makes me lie down in [1]green [a]pastures;He leads me beside [2b]waters of rest.
He [1a]restores my soul;He [b]guides me on the [2]paths of righteousnessFor His [3c]name’s sake.
Even though I walkThrough the [1]valley of the [a]shadow of death,I do not fear evil,For You are with me;Your [2b]rod and Your [2]staff,They comfort me.
You spread a [1a]table before meIn the presence of my adversaries;You [2b]anoint my head with oil;My [3c]cup runs over.
Surely [1]goodness and lovingkindness will follow meAll the days of my life,And I will dwell in the [1a]house of JehovahFor the length of my days.Psa 24:Title A [a]Psalm of David

24.text: [1]The [a]earth is Jehovah’s, and its [b]fullness,The habitable land and those who dwell in it.
For it is He who [a]founded it upon the seasAnd established it upon the streams.
[1a]Who may ascend the [2]mountain of Jehovah,And who may stand in His holy place?
He who has [a]clean hands and a [b]pure heart,Who has not lifted up his soul to falsehoodOr sworn deceitfully.
He will receive blessing from Jehovah,And righteousness from the God of his salvation.
This is the generation of those who seek Him,Those who [a]seek Your face, even Jacob. Selah
[1]Lift up your heads, O [2a]gates;And be lifted up, O [3]long enduring [2]doors;And the [4]King of [b]glory will come in.
Who is the King of glory?Jehovah [a]strong and mighty!Jehovah mighty in [b]battle!
Lift up your heads, O gates;And lift up, O long enduring doors;And the King of glory will come in.
Who is this King of glory?Jehovah of hosts—He is the King of glory! SelahPsa 25:Title [a]Of David

25.text: [1]To You, O Jehovah,I [a]lift up my soul.
O my God, I [a]trust in You;Do not let me be [b]ashamed;Do not let my enemies exult over me.
Indeed may all who wait on You not be ashamed;May those who deal treacherously without cause be ashamed.
[a]Make Your ways known to me, O Jehovah;Teach me Your paths.
Guide me in Your truth, and teach me;For You are the God of my salvation;I wait on You all the day long.
Remember Your compassions, O Jehovah,And Your acts of kindness, for they are from of old.
The [1]sins of my youth and my transgressions, do not remember;According to Your lovingkindness, remember me,For the sake of Your goodness, O Jehovah.
Jehovah is good and upright;Therefore He instructs sinners concerning the way.
He guides the lowly in justiceAnd teaches the lowly His way.
All the paths of Jehovah are lovingkindness and truthTo those who observe His covenant and His [1]testimonies.
For the sake of Your [a]name, O Jehovah,Pardon my iniquity, for it is great.
Who then is the man who fears Jehovah?Him will He instruct concerning the way that he should choose.
His soul will dwell in [1]prosperity,And his seed will possess the [a]land.
The [a]intimate [1]counsel of Jehovah is to those who fear Him,And His [1]covenant will He make known to them.
My eyes are continually turned to Jehovah,For He will bring my feet out of the net.
Turn toward me, and be gracious to me,For I am alone and afflicted.
[1]Relieve the troubles of my heart,And bring me out of my distresses.
Look on my affliction and my suffering,And forgive all my sins.
Look on my enemies, for they are many;Indeed they hate me with a violent hatred.
Keep my soul, and deliver me;Do not let me be put to shame,For I [a]take refuge in You.
May [1]integrity and [1]uprightness preserve me,For I wait on You.
Redeem Israel, O God,Out of all his troubles.Psa 26:Title [a]Of David

26.text: Judge me, O Jehovah;For I have walked in my [1]integrity,And in Jehovah I have [a]trusted;I will not waver.
Examine me, O Jehovah, and [a]try me;Test my [b]inward parts and my heart.
For Your lovingkindness is before my eyes,And I have [a]walked in Your truth.
I do not [a]sit with men of falsehood,Nor will I associate with hypocrites.
I hate the assembly of evildoers,Nor will I sit with the wicked.
I [a]wash my hands in innocenceAnd go around Your altar, O Jehovah,
Making myself heard with the voice of thanksgivingAnd telling out all Your wonderful deeds.
O Jehovah, I [a]love the habitation of Your house,And the place where Your glory [1b]abides.
Do not gather my soul with sinners,Nor my life with men of bloodshed,
In whose hand is a wicked scheme,And whose right hand is full of bribes.
But as for me, I walk in my integrity;Redeem me and be gracious to me.
My foot stands on level ground;In the assemblies I will bless Jehovah.Psa 27:Title [a]Of David

27.text: Jehovah is my [a]light and my [b]salvation;Whom shall I fear?Jehovah is the strength of my life;Whom shall I dread?
When evildoers come near meTo devour my flesh,It is they, my adversaries and my enemies,Who stumble and fall.
If an army camps against me,My heart does not fear;If a battle arises against me,In spite of this I am confident.
One thing I have asked from Jehovah;That do I seek:To dwell in the [1a]house of JehovahAll the days of my life,To [b]behold the [2c]beauty of Jehovah,And to inquire in His temple.
For He will conceal me in His shelterIn the day of trouble;He will hide me in the [a]hiding place of His tent;He will raise me up upon a rock.
And now my head is lifted upAbove my enemies who surround me.And I will offer in His tent[a]Sacrifices of shouts of joy:I will sing and [b]psalm to Jehovah.
Hear, O Jehovah, when I call with my voice,And be gracious to me and answer me.
When You say, [a]Seek My face,To You my heart says, Your face, O Jehovah, will I seek.
Do not hide Your face from me;Do not turn Your servant away in Your anger;You have been my help.Do not abandon me or forsake me,O God of my salvation.
If my father and my mother forsake me,Jehovah will take me up.
Teach me, O Jehovah, Your way,And lead me on a level pathBecause of those who lie in wait for me.
Do not give me overTo the desire of my adversaries;For [a]false witnesses have risen up against me,And those who breathe out threats.
[1]I have believedThat I would see the goodness of JehovahIn the land of the living.
Wait for Jehovah;Be strong, and let your heart be encouraged.Yes, wait for Jehovah.Psa 28:Title [a]Of David

28.text: To You, O Jehovah, do I call out;My [a]rock, do not be deaf to me,Lest, if You are silent to me,I become like those who go down into the pit.
Hear the voice of my supplicationsWhen I cry out to You,When I [a]lift up my handsUnto Your innermost sanctuary.
Do not drag me away with the wickedAnd with the workers of iniquity,Who speak peace to their neighborsWhile evil is in their heart.
[1]Repay them according to what they have doneAnd according to the evil of their deeds;[a]According to the work of their hands repay them;Return to them their due.
For they do not regard the acts of JehovahOr the work of His hands;He will tear them downAnd not rebuild them.
Blessed be Jehovah,For He has heard the voice of my supplications.
Jehovah is my strength and my [a]shield;My heart trusts in Him, and I am helped;Therefore my heart exults;And with my song do I give thanks to Him.
Jehovah is [1]their strength;And He is a stronghold of salvation to His anointed.
Save Your people,And bless Your inheritance;And [a]shepherd them and carry them forever.Psa 29:Title A [a]Psalm of David

29.text: [1a]Ascribe to Jehovah, O sons of the Mighty,Ascribe to Jehovah glory and strength;
Ascribe to Jehovah the glory of His name;Worship Jehovah in holy [1]splendor.
The voice of Jehovah is over the waters;The God of [a]glory [b]thunders,Jehovah over the great waters.
The voice of Jehovah is powerful;The voice of Jehovah is full of majesty.
The voice of Jehovah breaks the cedars;Indeed Jehovah breaks the cedars of Lebanon in pieces;
And He makes Lebanon [a]skip like a calf,And [1]Sirion like a young wild ox.
The voice of Jehovah cleaves out flames of fire.
The voice of Jehovah shakes the wilderness;Jehovah shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
The voice of Jehovah [1]causes the hinds to calveAnd strips the forests bare;And in His temple all say, Glory!
Jehovah sat enthroned at the Flood,And Jehovah sits as [a]King forever.
May Jehovah give strength to His people.May Jehovah bless His people with peace.Psa 30:Title A Psalm; a Song for the [a]Dedication of the House. [b]Of David

30.text: I extol You, O Jehovah, for You have drawn me upAnd have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
O Jehovah my God,I cried out to You,And You healed me.
O Jehovah, You have brought up my soul from Sheol;You have preserved me alive [1]that I would not go down into the pit.
Sing psalms to Jehovah, you who are His faithful ones;And [1a]praise His holy [2]memorial.
For His anger lasts but a moment,His favor, a lifetime;Weeping may linger in the evening,But a ringing shout goes up in the morning.
As for me, I said in my prosperity,I will never be shaken.
O Jehovah, by Your favor You had made my [1]mountain stand strong.You hid Your face; I was troubled.
To You, O Jehovah, I called out;And to [1]Jehovah I made my supplication.
What profit is there in my blood if I go down into the pit?Will the dust [a]praise You? Will it declare Your [1]faithfulness?
Hear, O Jehovah, and be gracious to me.O Jehovah, be my help.
You have turned for me my mourning into [a]dancing;You have loosened my sackclothAnd girded me with rejoicing,
That my [1a]glory might sing psalms to You and not be silent.O Jehovah my God, I will praise You forever.Psa 31:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

31.text: [a]In You, O Jehovah, do I [b]take refuge;Never let me be put to shame.[1]Rescue me in Your righteousness.
Incline Your ear to me;Deliver me speedily.Be to me a [1a]rock of protection,A fortress, to save me.
For You are my crag and my fortress;Therefore for Your name’s sakeLead me and guide me.
Bring me out of the netThat they have secretly laid for me,For You are my protection.
[1a]Into Your hand I commit my spirit;Redeem me, O Jehovah, God of truth.
I hate those who regard [1]vanities of falsehood,But I trust in Jehovah.
I will exult and rejoice in Your lovingkindness,Since You have seen my affliction;You know the distress of my soul.
And You have not delivered me into the hand of the enemy;You have made my feet stand in a place broad and free.
Be gracious to me, O Jehovah, for I am in [1]distress.My eye is wasted away with grief,Indeed my soul and body.
For my life has been consumed in [a]sorrow,And my years, in sighing.My strength fails because of my iniquity,And my bones have wasted away.
Because of all my adversaries,I have become a reproach,And especially so to my neighbors,And something dreaded to my acquaintances;Those who see me on the street flee from me.
I am forgotten, like a dead man out of mind;I am like a destroyed vessel.
For I hear the [a]slander of many;Terror is on every side.When they take [b]counsel together against me,They scheme to take my life.
But I [a]trust in You, O Jehovah;I say, You are my God.
My [a]times are in Your hand;Deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from those who pursue me.
Cause Your face to [a]shine upon Your servant;Save me in Your lovingkindness.
O Jehovah, do not let me be put to shame when I call out to You;Let the wicked be put to shame; let them be silent in Sheol.
Let the lips of falsehood be made dumb,Which speak arrogantly against the righteousWith pride and contempt.
How great is Your goodness,Which You have stored up for those who fear You,Which You have accomplished in the sight of the sons of menFor those who [a]take refuge in You!
You hide them in the [a]hiding place of Your presenceApart from the conspiracies of men;You lay them away secretly in a shelterApart from the contention of tongues.
Blessed be Jehovah,For He displayed His lovingkindness wondrouslyTo me in a fortified city.
I, on my part, had said in my alarm,I am cut off before Your eyes;But instead You heardThe voice of my supplicationsWhen I cried out to You.
[a]Love Jehovah, all you who are His faithful ones;Jehovah guards the trustworthy,And recompenses abundantlyHim who acts haughtily.
Be strong, and let your heart be encouraged,All you who [1]hope in Jehovah.Psa 32:Title [a]Of David. A [*]Maschil

32.text: [1a]Blessed is he whose transgression is [2b]forgiven;Whose sin is [c]covered.
Blessed is the man to whomJehovah does not impute iniquityAnd in whose spirit there is no deceit.
When I kept silent, my bones wasted awayThrough my groaning all day long.
For day and night Your [a]handWas heavy upon me;My life [b]sap was dried upAs in the drought of summer. Selah
I [a]acknowledged my sin to You,And I did not cover my iniquity.I said, I will confess my transgressions to Jehovah.Then You [b]forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah
Because of this let everyone who is faithfulPray to You at a time when You may be found;Surely when great waters overflow,They will not reach him.
You are my [a]hiding place;You preserve me from trouble;You surround me with the ringing shouts of deliverance. Selah
I will instruct you and teach you concerning the way you should go;I will counsel you; my eye is upon you.
Do not be like a horse or like a mule, without understanding;Whose trappings consist of [a]bit and bridle to restrain them,Else they do not come near you.
Many are the sorrows that the wicked have;But he who trusts in Jehovah is encompassed by lovingkindness.
Rejoice in Jehovah and exult, O [1]righteous ones;And give a ringing shout, all you who are [1]upright in heart.

33.text: Rejoice in Jehovah, O righteous ones.Praise is becoming to the upright.
Give thanks to Jehovah with the lyre;With the harp of ten strings sing psalms to Him.
Sing Him a [a]new song;Play skillfully with a joyous shout.
For the word of Jehovah is right,And all His work is done in faithfulness.
He loves righteousness and justice;The earth is [a]full of the lovingkindness of Jehovah.
By the [a]word of Jehovah the heavens were made,And all their host, by the breath of His mouth.
He gathers the water of the [a]sea like a [b]heap;He puts the deep waters into storage places.
Let all the earth fear Jehovah;Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
For He [a]spoke, and it was;He [b]commanded, and it stood.
Jehovah brings the [a]counsel of the nations to nothing;He frustrates the intentions of the peoples.
The [a]counsel of Jehovah stands forever;The intentions of His heart stand from generation to generation.
[a]Blessed is the nation whose God is Jehovah,The people whom He has [b]chosen as His own possession.
Jehovah looks from heaven;He sees all the sons of men.
From the site of His habitation He gazesAt all the inhabitants of the earth,
He who fashions the hearts of them all,He who discerns all their works.
No king is saved by a great army;A mighty man is not delivered by great strength.
A horse is a vain thing for salvation,And it cannot deliver by its great power.
Behold, the [a]eye of Jehovah is upon those who fear Him,Upon those who [1]hope in His lovingkindness,
To [a]deliver their soul from deathAnd to preserve them alive in famine.
Our soul waits on Jehovah;He is our help and our shield;
For in Him our heart rejoices,Because in His holy name we trust.
May Your lovingkindness, O Jehovah, be upon us,Even as we have hoped in You.Psa 34:Title [a]Of David, when he [b]disguised his sanity before [*]Abimelech, who drove him out, and he departed

34.text: [1]I will [2]bless Jehovah at all times;His praise will continually be in my mouth.
My soul makes its boast in Jehovah;The lowly hear and they rejoice.
[a]Magnify Jehovah with me,And let us exalt His name together.
I sought Jehovah, and He answered me;And He delivered me from all that terrified me.
They looked to Him and were radiant;And their faces will never be abashed.
This poor man called out, and Jehovah heard;And He [1]saved him out of all his troubles.
The [a]angel of Jehovah [b]encampsAround those who fear Him, and He delivers them.
[1a]Taste and see that Jehovah is good;[2b]Blessed is the man who takes refuge in Him.
Fear Jehovah, you who are His saints;For there is no lack with those who fear Him.
The young lions hunger and starve,But those who seek Jehovah will not lack any good thing.
Come, children; hear me.I will teach you the fear of Jehovah.
[a]Who is the man who desires life,Who loves having days in order to see good?
Guard your [a]tongue from evil,And your lips from speaking deceit.
[a]Turn away from evil and do good;Seek peace and pursue it.
The eyes of Jehovah are set toward the righteous,And His ears, toward their cry.
The face of Jehovah is against those who do evil,To cut off the memory of them from the earth.
The righteous cry out, and Jehovah hears;And He saves them out of all their troubles.
Jehovah is near to those who are broken in heart,And He saves those who are [a]contrite in spirit.
Many are the troubles of the righteous man,But Jehovah delivers him from all of them.
He keeps all his [a]bones;[1]Not one of them is broken.
Evil will slay the wicked,And those who hate the righteous man will be condemned.
Jehovah redeems the soul of His servants,And none who [a]take refuge in Him will be condemned.Psa 35:Title [a]Of David

35.text: [1]Strive, O Jehovah, with those who strive with me;[1]Battle against those who battle against me.
Take hold of buckler and shield,And rise up as my help.
Draw out also the spear, and close up the wayAgainst those who pursue me;Say to my soul,I am your salvation.
[a]Let those who seek my lifeBe put to shame and humiliated;Let those who devise evil for meBe turned back and confounded.
Let them be like chaff before the wind,With the [a]angel of Jehovah driving them on.
Let their way be darkness and slipperiness itself,With the angel of Jehovah pursuing them.
For without cause they hid their net for me;Without cause they dug a pit for me.
Let [a]destruction come upon him unawares,And let his net, which he hid, catch him;Let him fall into it, into destruction.
Then my soul will exult in Jehovah;It will rejoice in His salvation.
All my bones will say,Jehovah, who is like You,Who delivers the poor from him who is too strong for him,And the poor and needy from him who robs him?
Malicious [a]witnesses rise up;They ask me about things I know nothing of.
They repay me evil for good;I am bereaved in soul.
But I, when they were sick,Had sackcloth as my clothing;I afflicted my soul with fasting,And my prayer [a]returned to my own bosom.
I conducted myself as if it had been my friend or my brother;Like one mourning for his mother, I bowed down gloomily.
But at my stumbling they rejoice and gather together;The attackers, whom I did not knowGather together against me;They tear at me and do not cease.
As profane mocking parasites,They [a]gnash their teeth at me.
O Lord, how long will You look on?Rescue my soul from their destruction,My [1]precious life from the lions.
I will give You thanks in the great congregation;Among a mighty people I will praise You.
Do not let those who are wrongly my enemies rejoice over me;Do not let those who [a]hate me without cause narrow their eyes at me.
For they do not speak [a]peace,But devise words of deceitAgainst the quiet people of the land.
And they open their mouth wide against me;They say, Aha! Aha! Our eyes have seen it.
You have seen, O Jehovah; do not be silent.O Lord, do not be far from me.
Awake, and stir Yourself for my cause,For my claim, O my God and my Lord.
Vindicate me according to Your righteousness, O Jehovah my God;And do not let them rejoice over me.
Do not let them say in their heart,[a]Aha! [1]That is what we wanted.Do not let them say, We have swallowed him up.
May those who rejoice at my troubleBe ashamed and confounded together;May those who magnify themselves against meBe clothed with shame and humiliation.
May those who delight in the vindication of my righteousnessGive a ringing shout and rejoice,And may they say continually,Jehovah be [a]magnified,Who delights in the well-being of His servant.
And my tongue will declare Your righteousnessAnd Your praise all day long.Psa 36:Title To the choir director. Of the servant of Jehovah; [a]of David

36.text: Transgression speaks to the wickedWithin [1]his heart;[a]There is no [b]dread of GodBefore his eyes.
For he flatters himself in his own eyes,That his iniquity cannot be found out and hated.
The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit;He has ceased being wise and doing good.
He plans wickedness on his bed;He sets himself on a way that is not good;He does not reject evil.
[1]O Jehovah, [a]Your lovingkindness is in the heavens;Your faithfulness reaches unto the skies.
Your righteousness is like the mountains of God;Your judgments are like the great deep;You preserve man and beast, O Jehovah.
How precious is Your lovingkindness, O God!Thus the sons of men take [a]refuge in the shadow of Your [b]wings.
They are saturated with the [1a]fatness of Your [2]house,And You cause them to drink of the [b]river of Your pleasures.
For with You is the [a]fountain of [b]life;In Your [c]light we see light.
Continue Your lovingkindness to those who know You,And Your righteousness to those who are upright in heart.
Do not let the foot of the arrogant come upon me,Nor let the hand of the wicked drive me away.
There the workers of iniquity are fallen;They are cast down and unable to rise.Psa 37:Title [a]Of David

37.text: [1]Do not be incensed because of evildoers;Do not be envious of wrongdoers.
For they soon [a]wither like grassAnd fade away like green herbage.
Trust in Jehovah, and do good;Dwell in the land, and feed on His faithfulness.
Delight yourself in Jehovah,And He will give you the requests of your heart.
[a]Commit your way to Jehovah,And trust in Him; and He will act.
And He will cause your righteousness to go forth like [a]light,And your justness like noonday.
Be still before Jehovah, and wait for Him;Do not be incensed because of him who enjoys prosperity on his way,Because of the man who performs his evil schemes.
Cease from [a]anger and forsake wrath;Do not be incensed; it leads only to evil.
For the evildoers will be cut off;But those who wait on Jehovah,They will [a]inherit the land.
And in a little while longer there will be no wicked man;Indeed, you will look diligently for his place, and he will not be there.
But the [a]lowly will inherit the landAnd delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
The wicked man makes evil schemes against the righteous manAnd [a]gnashes his teeth at him.
The Lord will laugh at him,For He sees that his day will come.
The wicked have drawn out the swordAnd bent their bow,To cast the poor and the needy down,To slaughter those who are upright in their way.
Their sword will enter their own heart,And their bows will be broken.
Better is the [a]little that the righteous man hasThan the abundance of many wicked.
For the arms of the wicked will be broken,But Jehovah upholds the righteous.
Jehovah knows the days of the perfect,And their inheritance will be forever.
They will not be put to shame in a time of evil,And in the days of famine they will be satisfied.
But the wicked will perish,And the enemies of Jehovah will be like the glory of the pastures:They are consumed; in smoke they are consumed.
The wicked borrow but do not repay,But the righteous is gracious and gives.
For those [a]blessed by Him will [b]inherit the land,But those cursed by Him will be cut off.
A man’s [a]steps are established by Jehovah,And He delights in his way.
If he falls, he is not utterly cast down;For Jehovah upholds him by the hand.
I was once a young man; now I am old.Yet I have not seen the righteous man forsaken,Nor his seed seeking bread.
He is always gracious and [a]lends,And his seed becomes a blessing.
[a]Turn away from evil and do good,And abide forever.
For Jehovah loves justiceAnd does not abandon His faithful ones;They are kept forever;But the seed of the wicked is cut off.
The righteous will inherit the land,And they will abide in it forever.
The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom,And his tongue speaks justice.
The law of his God is in his heart;His steps do not slip.
The wicked man watches for the righteous manAnd seeks to slay him.
Jehovah does not leave him in his hand,Nor let him be condemned when he is judged.
Wait for Jehovah,And keep His way;And He will exalt you to inherit the land;When the wicked are cut off, you will see it.
I have seen the wicked man terrorizing,And spreading himself like a luxuriant native tree.
Then someone passes by, and behold, he is not there;I search for him, and he is not found.
Observe the perfect man, and watch the upright man;For there is a future for the man of [a]peace.
But the transgressors will be altogether destroyed;The future of the wicked will be cut off.
And the salvation of the righteous is from Jehovah;He is their stronghold in a time of distress.
And Jehovah helps them and rescues them;He rescues them from the wicked,And saves them, for they [a]take refuge in Him.Psa 38:Title A [a]Psalm of David, for remembrance

38.text: [1a]O Jehovah, do not rebuke me in Your indignation,Nor chasten me in Your burning wrath.
For Your arrows have sunk deep in me,And Your hand has come down heavy upon me.
There is no soundness in my flesh due to Your indignation;There is no wholeness in my bones due to my sin.
For my iniquities have passed over my head;Like a heavy burden, they are too heavy for me.
My wounds are odious and festeringDue to my foolishness.
I am bent down and bowed utterly;I go about all day long mourning.
For my loins are filled with burning,And there is no soundness in my flesh.
I am benumbed and crushed utterly;I cry out due to the [1]groaning of my heart.
O Lord, all my desire is before You,And my groaning is not hidden from You.
My heart throbs; my strength fails me;And as for the light of my eyes, neither is it with me.
Those who love me and those who are my friends stand aloof from myplague,And my relatives stand [a]far away.
And those who seek my soul lay traps for me,And those who pursue my misfortune speak of destruction,And they contemplate deceit all day long.
But I, like a deaf man, cannot hear;And I am like a dumb man, who cannot open his [a]mouth.
Indeed I am like a man who does not hearAnd in whose mouth there are no reproofs.
For in You, O Jehovah, do I hope;You will respond, O Lord my God.
For I said, May they never rejoice over me!When my foot slips, they magnify themselves over me.
For I am ready to stumble,And my pain is before me continually.
For I declare my iniquity;I am anxious because of my sin.
But my enemies are vigorous; they are strong;And those who wrongly hate me are many.
Those also who repay evil for goodAre my adversaries because I pursue what is good.
Do not abandon me, O Jehovah;O my God, do not be far away from me.
Hasten to help me,O Lord, my [a]salvation.Psa 39:Title To the choir director, to Jeduthun. A [a]Psalm of David

39.text: I said, I will keep my ways,So that I do not sin with my [a]tongue;I will keep a muzzle on my mouthWhen the wicked are before me.
I was dumb and silent;I held my peace, even from good;And my pain was stirred up.
My heart was hot within me;While I mused, the fire burned;I spoke with my tongue:
O Jehovah, cause me to know my end,And the measure of my [a]days, what it is.May I know how transient I am.
Behold, You have made my [a]days as mere handbreadths,And my lifetime is as nothing before You;Surely every man [1]at his best is altogether [2b]vanity. Selah
Surely man goes about as a semblance;Surely they bustle about in vain:He [a]heaps up riches yet knows not[b]Who will gather them.
And now what am I waiting for, O Lord?My hope—it is in You.
Deliver me from all my transgressions;Do not make me the fool’s reproach.
I was [a]dumb; I did not open my mouth;For You have done this.
Remove Your plague from me;By the hostility of Your hand I am consumed.
With rebukes You chasten a man because of iniquity,And cause what is desirous to him to disappear like a moth.Surely every man is [a]vanity. Selah
Hear my prayer, O Jehovah;And give ear to my cry;Do not hold Your peace at my tears.For I am a stranger with You,A sojourner, as all my fathers were.
Look away from me, that I may be cheerful,Before I go away and am no more.Psa 40:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

40.text: I waited patiently on Jehovah,And He inclined to meAnd heard my cry.
Then He brought me up out of a pit of destruction,Out of the miry clay;And He set my feet upon a rock,Establishing my steps.
Then He put a [a]new song in my mouth,A praise to our God.Many see it and fear,And they trust in Jehovah.
[1a]Blessed is the manWho makes Jehovah his trustAnd does not turn to the proudOr to those who turn aside after deception.

Many, O Jehovah my God, are the wonderful deeds which You have doneAnd Your [a]thoughts toward us;They cannot be set in order unto You.Were I to declare and speak of them,They would be too numerous to recount.
[1a]You do not delight in [2b]sacrifice and [2]offering;You have [3]prepared [c]ears for Me;You do not require burnt offering and sin offering.
Then I said,Behold, I have [1]come;In the [2]scroll of the bookIt is written concerning Me.
I delight in doing Your will, O My God;Indeed Your law is within My [a]inward parts.
I bear tidings of righteousnessIn the great [a]congregation.Behold, I will not withhold My lips;O Jehovah, You know.
I have not hidden Your righteousnessWithin My heart;I have declared Your faithfulness and Your salvation;I have not concealed Your lovingkindness and Your truth from the greatcongregation.
You, O Jehovah, do not withholdYour compassions from me;May Your lovingkindness and Your truthPreserve me continually.
For evils encompass me until they cannot be counted;My iniquities have so overtaken meThat I cannot see;They are more numerous than the hairs of my head;Thus my heart fails me.
[a]Be pleased, O Jehovah, to deliver me;O Jehovah, hasten to help me.
[a]May those who seek my life to destroy itBe put to shame and confounded together;May those who take delight in my misfortuneBe turned back and humiliated.
May those who say to me, [a]Aha! Aha!Be desolate because of their shame.
May all those who seek YouBe glad and rejoice in You;May those who love Your salvation say continually,May Jehovah be [a]magnified!
But I am poor and needy;May the Lord think about me.You are my help and my Deliverer;O my God, do not delay.Psa 41:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

41.text: Blessed is he who considers the [a]poor;In the day of evil Jehovah will deliver him.
May Jehovah keep him and preserve him alive;May he be [1]blessed in the land;And do not give him over to the desire of his enemies.
Jehovah will sustain him on his sickbed;You make all his bed in his illness.
I said, O Jehovah, be gracious to me;Heal my soul, for I have sinned against You.
My enemies speak evil against me:When will he die and his name perish?
And if he comes to see me, he speaks falsehood.His heart gathers iniquity to itself;When he goes out, he speaks it.
All who hate me whisper together against me;Against me they devise my misfortune.
[1]Some evil illness, they say, has been poured out on him;And when he lies down, he will not rise again.
[1]Even my familiar [a]friend, in whom I trusted,[b]Who [c]ate my bread, has lifted his heel against me.
But You, O Jehovah, be gracious to me and raise me upThat I may [1]recompense them.
By this I know that You delight in me,That my enemy has not shouted in triumph over me.
And as for me, You uphold me in my [1]integrity,And You set me in Your presence forever.
[a]Blessed be Jehovah the God of Israel,From eternity to eternity.[b]Amen and Amen.Psa 42:Title To the choir director. A [*]Maschil [a]of the sons of [b]Korah

42.text: [1]As the [a]hart pantsAfter the [b]streams of water,So my soul [2]pantsFor You, O [3]God.
My soul [a]thirsts for God,For the living God.When will I come and [b]appearBefore God?
My tears have been my foodDay and night,While they say to me all day long,Where is your God?

These things I [1]remember,And I pour out my soul within me:That I passed through with the throng;I led them to the [a]house of GodWith the voice of a joyous shout and [2]praise,The [b]festal multitude.
[a]Why are you cast down, O my soul?And why are you disquieted within me?Hope in God, for I will yet [1]praise HimFor the salvation of His [2b]countenance.
O my God, my soul is cast down within me;Therefore I [1]remember YouFrom the land of the Jordan and the [a]Hermons,From Mount Mizar.
Deep calls unto deepAt the sound of Your water spouts;All Your [a]waves and Your billowsPass over me.
By day Jehovah commandsHis lovingkindness,And by [a]night His song is with me,A prayer to the God of my life.
I say to God my [a]rock,Why have You forgotten me?Why do I go mourningBecause of the oppression of the enemy?
As with a shattering in my bones,My adversaries reproach me,While they say to me all day long,Where is your God?
[a]Why are you cast down, O my soul?And why are you disquieted within me?Hope in God, for I will yet praise Him,The [b]salvation of my countenance and my God.

43.text: [1]Render judgment for me, O God;And plead my causeBefore an unfaithful nation;From the deceitful and unjust manDeliver me.
For You are the God of my protection.Why have You rejected me?Why do I go about mourningBecause of the oppression of the enemy?
Send forth Your [1a]light and Your [b]truth;They will lead me;They will bring me to Your [c]holy mountainAnd to Your [d]tabernacles.
And I will go to the [1a]altar of God,To God my exceeding joy;And I will praise You with the harp,O God, my God.

[a]Why are you cast down, O my soul?And why are you disquieted within me?Hope in God, for I will yet praise Him,The salvation of my countenance and my God.Psa 44:Title To the choir director. [a]Of the sons of Korah. A [*]Maschil

44.text: [1]O God, we have heard with our ears;Our fathers have recounted it to us,The [a]work You did in their days,In the days of old.
You, by Your hand,Have [a]dispossessed nations, but You have [b]planted [1]our fathers;You afflicted peoples, but You spread [1]our fathers forth.
For they did not possess the land by their sword,Nor did their arm save them;Rather it was Your [a]right hand and Your arm,And the light of Your [b]countenance;For You delighted in them.
You are my [a]King, O God;Command salvation for Jacob.
Through You we thrust back our adversaries;Through Your name we trample those who rise up against us.
For I will not trust in my bow,Nor will my sword save me.
But You save us from our adversaries,And You put to shame those who hate us.
In God we [a]boast all day long,And we praise Your name forever. Selah
[1]Nevertheless You have rejected us and humiliated us,And You have not gone forth with our armies.
You have caused us to turn back from the adversary,And those who hate us take spoil for themselves.
You have given us over like [a]sheep to be eatenAnd have [b]scattered us among the nations.
You have [a]sold Your people for nothingAnd have not gone high on their price.
You have made us a reproach to our neighbors,A mocking and a derision to those around us.
You have made us a [a]proverb among the nations,Something to shake the head at among the peoples.
All day long my humiliation is before me,And the shame of my face covers me,
Because of the voice of him who reproaches and reviles,Because of the presence of the enemy and of him who avenges himself.
All this has come upon us; but we have [1]not forgotten You,Nor have we been false to Your covenant.
Our heart has not turned back,Nor have our steps deviated from Your way;
But You have crushed us in the place of jackalsAnd have covered us with the shadow of death.
If we have forgotten the name of our GodOr spread out our hands to a strange god,
Will God not search this out?For He [a]knows the hidden things of the heart.
But [a]for Your sake we are slain all day long;We are considered as [b]sheep for slaughter.
Awake! Why are You sleeping, O [1]Lord?Arise! Do not reject us forever.
Why do You hide Your faceAnd forget our affliction and our oppression?
For our soul bows down to the dust;Our body clings to the earth.
Rise up; be our help,And redeem us for Your lovingkindness’ sake.Psa 45:Title To the choir director: according to [*]Shoshannim. [a]Of the sons of Korah. A [**]Maschil; a song of [***]love

45.text: My heart [a]overflows with a good matter;I speak what I have composed concerning the [1b]King.My [2c]tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
You are [1a]fairer than the sons of men;[b]Grace is poured upon Your lips;Therefore God has [c]blessed You forever.
Gird Your sword upon Your thigh, O mighty One,In Your [1]majesty and Your [1]splendor.
And in Your splendor ride on [1]victoriouslyBecause of truth and [2a]meekness and righteousness;And let Your right hand [3]teach You [4]awesome deeds.
Your arrows are sharp:The peoples fall under You;The arrows are in the heart of the King’s enemies.
[a]Your [b]throne, O [1]God, is forever and ever;The [2]scepter of uprightness is the scepter of Your [3]kingdom.
You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness;Therefore God, [1]Your God, has [a]anointed YouWith the [2]oil of gladness above Your [2]companions.
All Your [1]garments smell of [a]myrrh and aloes, of cassia;From [2]palaces of ivory, harpstrings have made You glad.
[1]The [2]daughters of kings are among Your most prized;The [3]queen stands at Your right hand in the [4]gold of Ophir.
Hear, O [1]daughter, and see; and incline your ear;And [a]forget your people and your father’s house;
Thus the King will desire your [1]beauty.Because He is your [2]Lord,Worship Him.
And the [1]daughter of Tyre will come with a gift;The [1]rich among the people will entreat your favor.
The king’s [1]daughter is all glorious within the royal abode;Her [a]garment is a [2]woven work inwrought with gold.
She will be led to the King in [1]embroidered clothing;The [2a]virgins behind her, her [2]companions,Will be brought to You.
They will be led with rejoicing and exultation;They will [a]enter the King’s [1]palace.
In the place of Your [1]fathers will be Your [1]sons;You will make them [1a]princes in all the earth.

I will cause Your [1a]name to be remembered in all generations;Therefore the peoples will praise You forever and ever.Psa 46:Title To the choir director. [a]Of the sons of Korah. According to [*]alamoth. A Song

46.text: [1]God is our [a]refuge and strength;A help in distress, He is most readily found.
Therefore we will not fear,Though the earth change,And the [a]mountains slip into the heart of the seas;
Though the waters of the sea roar and foam;Though the mountains shake at its swelling. Selah
There is a [1a]river whose streams gladden the [2b]city of God,The holy place of the [c]tabernacles of the Most High.
God is in the [a]midst of her; she will [1]not be moved;God helps her at the dawn of the morning.
The nations rage; the kingdoms are moved.He utters His voice; the earth melts.
Jehovah of hosts is with us;The God of Jacob is our [a]high retreat. Selah
Come, see the works of Jehovah,How He has made desolations on earth.
He makes [a]wars cease unto the end of the earth;He breaks the bow and cuts the spear to pieces;He burns the war wagons with fire.
[1]Be [a]still and know that I am God.I will be exalted among the nations; I will be exalted on earth.
Jehovah of hosts is with us;The God of Jacob is our high retreat. SelahPsa 47:Title To the choir director. [a]Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm

47.text: Clap your hands, all you peoples;Shout to God with the voice of a triumphant sound.
For Jehovah Most High is awesome:A great [1a]King over all the earth.
He subdues peoples under us,And nations under our [a]feet.
He chose our inheritance for us,The excellency of Jacob, whom He loved. Selah
God ascends amidst shouting;Jehovah, at the sound of a trumpet.
Sing psalms to God, sing psalms;Sing psalms to our [a]King, sing psalms.
For God is the King of all the earth;Sing psalms with [a]understanding.
God reigns over the nations;God [a]sits upon His holy throne.
The princes of the peoples have been gatheredAs the people of the God of Abraham;For the shields of the earth belong to God;He is greatly exalted.Psa 48:Title A Song; a [a]Psalm of the sons of Korah

48.text: [1a]Great is Jehovah,And much to be praisedIn the [2b]city of our God,In His [c]holy [2]mountain.
[a]Beautiful in elevation,The joy of the whole earth,Is Mount [1b]Zion, the [c]sides of the north,The [d]city of the great King.
God has made Himself knownIn her [1]palaces as a [a]high retreat.
For, behold, the kings have assembled themselves;They passed by together.
They saw; therefore they were astounded.They were dismayed; they hurried off in alarm.
Trembling seized them there;Writhing, like that of a woman in labor;
With an east wind You shatteredThe ships of Tarshish.
As we have heard,So we have seenIn the city of Jehovah of hosts,In the city of our God.God will [a]establish her forever. Selah
We consider, O God, Your lovingkindnessIn the midst of Your temple.
As Your [a]name is, O God,So is Your praiseUnto the ends of the earth;Your right hand is full of righteousness.
Let Mount [a]Zion [b]rejoice;Let the daughters of Judah exultBecause of Your judgments.
Walk about [a]Zion, and go around her;Count her [1]towers.
Consider carefully her [a]bulwarks;Pass between her palaces;That you may recount it to the [b]generation following.
For this God is our God forever and ever.He will [1a]guide us even unto death.Psa 49:Title To the choir director. [a]Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm

49.text: [1]Hear this, all you peoples;Give ear, all you inhabitants of the world,
Both low and high,Rich and poor together;
My mouth will speak wisdom,And the [1]meditation of my heart will be that of understanding.
I will incline my ear to a parable;I will disclose my riddle upon the harp.
Why should I fear in the days of evil,When the iniquity of those who supplant me surrounds me,
Those who [a]trust in their [1]wealthAnd boast in the multitude of their [1]riches?
None can by any means redeem his brotherOr give to God [1]a ransom for him
(For the redemption of their [a]soul is costlyAnd must be given up forever),
That he would yet [a]live alwaysAnd not see [b]corruption.
For he will see that wise men die;The foolish one and the senseless one perish alikeAnd [a]leave their wealth to others.
Their inward thought is that their houses will remain forever,That their habitations will remain from generation to generation;They call lands after their own names.
But man in honor does not remain;He is like the beasts that perish.
Such is the way of those who are [1]self-confident,And of those after them who approve of their sayings. Selah
Like a flock, they are appointed for Sheol;Death will shepherd them.[1]And straight to the grave they will descend,And their form will be for Sheol to consumeThat there be no abode for them.
But God will redeem my soulFrom the power of Sheol,For He will receive me. Selah
Do not fear when a man becomes rich,When the glory of his house increases.
For when he dies, he will [a]take nothing;His glory will not descend after him.
Though he [a]blessed his soul while he lived—And men praise you when you do good for yourself—
[1]It will go to the generation of his fathers;They will never see light.
A man in honor but without understandingIs like the beasts that perish.Psa 50:Title A [a]Psalm of Asaph

50.text: The [a]Mighty One, God Jehovah,Speaks and summons the earthFrom the [b]rising of the sun to its setting.
Out of [1a]Zion, the perfection of [b]beauty,God [2c]shines forth.
Our God has come and will not be silent;[a]Fire devours before Him,And around Him it is exceedingly tempestuous.
He calls to the heavens above,And to the earth that He may [a]judge His people:
[a]Gather My saints to Me,Those who have made a [1b]covenant with Me by sacrifice.
Then the [a]heavens declare His righteousness,For God Himself is Judge. Selah
[a]Hear, O My people, and I will speak;O Israel, and I will [b]testify [1]against you:I am God, your God.
I will not reprove you because of your sacrifices,And your [a]burnt offerings are before Me continually.
I will not take a bull from your houseNor goats from your folds,
For every animal of the forest is Mine,The cattle upon a thousand hills.
I know every bird of the mountains,And I am aware of everything that moves in the field.
If I were hungry, I would not tell you;For the world is Mine and the [a]fullness thereof.
Shall I eat the flesh of bullsOr drink the blood of goats?
[a]Offer to God a sacrifice of [1]thanksgiving,And repay your [b]vows to the Most High;
And [1]call upon Me in the day of trouble:I will deliver you, and you will glorify Me.
But to the wicked God says,What right do you have to declare My statutesAnd take up My covenant in your mouth,
Seeing that you hate correctionAnd cast My words behind you?
When you see a thief, you take pleasure in him;And your portion is with adulterers.
You let your mouth loose for evil,And your tongue frames deceit.
You sit and speak against your brother;You defame the son of your mother.
These things you do, and I keep silent;You think that I am altogether like you.I will rebuke you and set forth the matter in order before your eyes.
Consider this then, you who forget God,Lest I tear you apart and there be none to deliver you.

Whoever offers a sacrifice of thanksgiving glorifies Me;And to him who sets his way right I will show the salvation of God.Psa 51:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David, when [*b]Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to [c]Bath-sheba

51.text: [1]Be gracious to me, O God, according to Your lovingkindness;According to the greatness of Your compassions [2a]blot out my transgressions.
[a]Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity,And from my sin cleanse me.
For I do know my transgressions,And my sin is before me continually.
Against You and You alone have I [a]sinned,And I have done what is evil in Your sight.Therefore You are [b]righteous when You speak;You are clear when You judge.
Behold, I was [1]brought forth in iniquity,And [a]in sin did my mother [b]conceive me.
Behold, You delight in truth in the [1a]inward parts;And in the [1b]hidden part You would make known wisdom to me.
Purge my sin with [1a]hyssop, and I will be clean;Wash me, and I will be [b]whiter than snow.
Let me hear gladness and joy,That the bones which You have broken may exult.
Hide Your face from my sins,And [a]blot out all my iniquities.
Create in me a [1a]clean heart, O God,And [2b]renew a steadfast spirit within me.
Do not cast me from Your [1a]presence,And do not take the [2b]Spirit of Your holiness away from me.
Restore to me the [a]gladness of Your salvation,And sustain me with a willing spirit.
I will teach transgressors Your ways,And sinners will turn back to You.
Deliver me from the guilt of [a]bloodshed, O God, O God of my salvation;And my tongue will ring forth Your righteousness.
O Lord, open my lips,And my mouth will declare Your praise.
For You do not delight in sacrifice;Otherwise I would offer it;You take no pleasure in [a]burnt offerings.
The sacrifices of God are a broken [a]spirit;A broken and a [b]contrite heart, O God, You will not despise.
Do [1]good in Your good pleasure unto [a]Zion;[b]Build the walls of Jerusalem.

Then You will delight in the sacrifices of righteousness,In burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings;Then they will offer bulls upon Your altar.Psa 52:Title To the choir director. A [*]Maschil [a]of David, when [b]Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul and said to him, David has come to the house of Ahimelech

52.text: [1]Why do you boast in evil, O mighty one?God’s lovingkindness endures continually.
Your tongue devises destruction;It is like a sharpened razor, O worker of deceit.
You have loved evil rather than good,Falsehood rather than speaking righteousness. Selah
You have loved all the devouring words,O deceitful tongue.
God will likewise [1]break you down forever;He will take you and pluck you up from your tent,And uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
And the righteous will see it and fear,And they will laugh at him, saying,
Behold, this is that mighty manWho did not make God his [1]strength,But [a]trusted in the multitude of his richesAnd strengthened himself in his desire.
But I, like a flourishing [a]olive treeIn the house of God,Trust in God’s lovingkindnessForever and ever.

I will give You thanks forever,For You have acted;And I will hope in Your name in the presence of Your faithful ones,For it is good.Psa 53:Title To the choir director: according to [*]mahalath. A [**]Maschil [a]of David

53.text: [a]The [1]fool has said in his heart,There is no God.They are corrupt and commit abominable deeds;[b]There is none who does good.
God looked down from heavenUpon the sons of menTo see if there was anyone who had insight,Who [a]seeks after God.
[a]All of them have turned back;They are together perverse.There is none who does good;There is not even one.
Have they no knowledge, the workers of iniquity,Who eat up my people as they would eat up breadAnd do not call upon God?
There they were in great fear,Where there was no fear.For God scattered the [a]bonesOf him who encamped against you.You put them to shameBecause God has rejected them.
