Oh that the [1]salvation of Israel might come forth from [a]Zion!When God [b]turns the captivity of His people,Jacob will exult, Israel will rejoice.Psa 54:Title To the choir director: on the stringed instruments. A [**]Maschil [a]of David, when the [b]Ziphites came and said to Saul, David is indeed hiding among us!

54.text: O God, [1]save me by Your name,And execute judgment for me by Your might.
O God, hear my prayer;Give ear to the words of my mouth.
For strangers have risen up against me,And those who terrorize seek my life;They do not [a]set God before them. Selah
Behold, God is my [a]Helper;The Lord is among those who sustain my soul.
He will return the evil to those who lie in wait for me.Annihilate them in Your [1]faithfulness.
I will offer a freewill offering to You;I will [1]praise Your name, O Jehovah, for it is good.
For He has delivered me from all distress,And my eye looks triumphantly upon my enemies.Psa 55:Title To the choir director: on the stringed instruments. A [*]Maschil [a]of David

55.text: [1a]Give ear, O God, to my prayer;And do not hide Yourself from my supplication.
Give heed to me, and answer me.I grow restless in my complaint and moan,
Because of the voice of the enemy,Because of the oppression of the wicked man;For they [a]drop iniquity upon me,And in anger they make me their enemy.
My heart writhes within me,And the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
Fear and trembling have come upon me,And shuddering overwhelms me.
And I say, Oh that I had [a]wings like a dove!Then I would fly away and be at rest.
Indeed I would wander far away;I would lodge in the wilderness. Selah
I would hasten my escapeFrom the storm wind and the tempest.
Confuse, O Lord, divide their tongue;For I have seen violence and contention in the city.
Day and night they go around it,Upon its walls;And iniquity and trouble are in the midst of it.
Utter ruin is in the midst of it,And oppression and deceitDo not depart from its streets.
For it is not an enemy who reproaches me;Otherwise I could bear it.It is not he who hates me who magnifies himself against me;Otherwise I could hide myself from him.
But it is you, [1]my equal,My close [a]friend and my acquaintance.
We were in sweet counsel together;We walked with the throng in the [a]house of God.
[1]Let death surprise them;May they go down into Sheol alive.For evil is in their dwelling, in the midst of them.
As for me, I call out to God,And Jehovah will save me.
Evening and morning and at noontimeI complain and moan,And He hears my voice.
He has redeemed my soul in peaceFrom the battle against me,For many are they who strive with me.
God hears and answers them—Even He who sits enthroned from of old—SelahBecause with them there is no change,And they do not fear God.
[1]He put forth his hands against those who were at peace with him;He profaned his covenant.
Smooth were the buttery words of his mouth,But his heart was war;More soothing than oil were his words,But they were in fact drawn swords.
[a]Cast your burden upon Jehovah,And He will sustain you;He will never allow the righteous to be moved.

But You, O God, will bring them downInto the pit of decay.Men of bloodshed and deceitWill not live out half their days;But I will trust in You.Psa 56:Title To the choir director: according to the [*]dove on the distant terebinths. [a]Of David. A [**]Michtam, when the [b]Philistines seized him in Gath

56.text: [1]Be gracious to me, O God; for man tramples me;All day long he, fighting, oppresses me.
Those who lie in wait for me trample me all day long,For many are those who arrogantly fight against me.
When I am afraid,I trust in You.
In God, whose word I praise,[a]In God do I trust, and I will not be [b]afraid.What can flesh do to me?
All day long they wrest my words;All their thoughts are against me for evil.
They gather themselves; they lurk;They [a]watch my steps,As they have waited to take my life.
Will there be deliverance for them in spite of their iniquity?In anger cast down the peoples, O God.
You have counted my wanderings.Put my tears into Your bottle.Are they not in Your [a]book?
Then my enemies will turn backWhen I call;This I know,That God is for me.
In God, whose word I praise,In Jehovah, whose word I praise,
[a]In God do I trust; I will not be afraid.What can man do to me?
Your vows, O God, are on me;I will render thank offerings to You.

[a]For You have delivered my soul from death,Indeed, my feet from stumbling,That I may walk before GodIn the [b]light of the living.Psa 57:Title To the choir director: [*]Do not destroy. [a]Of David. A [**]Michtam, when he fled from Saul, in the [b]cave

57.text: [1]Be gracious to me, O God, be gracious to me;For my soul [a]takes refuge in You;Indeed in the shadow of Your [b]wings I will take refuge,Until the destruction passes by.
I call out to God the [a]Most High,To the God who accomplishes all things for me.
He sends forth from heaven and saves me;He reproaches him who would trample me; SelahGod sends forthHis lovingkindness and His [1]faithfulness.
My soul is among lions;I lie down among those who blaze forth, the sons of men,Whose teeth are spears and arrows,And whose tongue is a sharp sword.
[a]Be exalted above the heavens, O God;Let Your glory be above all the earth.
They have prepared a net for my steps;My soul is bowed down.They have dug a pit before me;They have fallen into it themselves. Selah
My heart is steadfast, O God;[a]My heart is steadfast.I will sing, indeed I will sing psalms.
Awake, my [a]glory;Awake, O harp and lyre!I will awaken the dawn.
[a]I will give thanks to You among the peoples, O Lord;I will sing psalms to You among the nations.
For Your [a]lovingkindness is as great as the heavens;And Your faithfulness reaches unto the skies.
[a]Be exalted above the heavens, O God;Let Your glory be above all the earth.Psa 58:Title To the choir director: [*]Do not destroy. [a]Of David. A [**]Michtam

58.text: [1]Do you indeed, O [2]judges, speak righteousness?Do you judge in uprightness, O sons of men?
No, in heart you perform injustice;You weigh out the violence of your hands on earth.
The wicked are estranged from the womb;They err from their birth, speaking lies.
Their venom is like the venom of a serpent;They are like the deaf cobra that has shut its ear,
Which does not hear the sound of the charmersOr the wise spellbinder.
O God, break their teeth in their mouth;Break off the fangs of the young lions, O Jehovah.
Let them melt like water that runs off.When he shoots his arrows,Let them be as though they were cut off.
Let them be like a snail that melts and goes away,Like a woman’s miscarriage, which by no means sees the sun.
Before your pots can feel the fire of the thorns,Whether green or burning, He will sweep them away.
The righteous man will rejoice when he sees vengeance;He will wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.

And men will say, Indeed there is fruit for the righteous man;Indeed there is a God who [a]judges on earth.Psa 59:Title To the choir director: [*]Do not destroy. [a]Of David. A [**]Michtam, when Saul sent men, and they [b]watched the house in order to kill him

59.text: Deliver me from my enemies, O my God;Set me on high, secure from those who rise up against me.
Deliver me from the workers of iniquity,And save me from men of bloodshed.
For behold, they lie in wait for my soul;The strong gather themselves against me.Not because of any transgression of mine nor because of any sin ofmine, O Jehovah,
Not for any fault of mine, they run and ready themselves;Awake to meet me, and see.
Indeed You, O Jehovah, God of hosts, God of Israel,Arise to punish all the nations;Have no mercy upon any who treacherously commit iniquity. Selah
They return in the evening;They growl like a dogAnd go around the city.
Behold, they belch forth with their mouth;Swords are in their lips;For, Who, they say, hears?
But You, O Jehovah, [a]laugh at them;You have all the nations in derision.
O [1]my strength, I will watch for You;For God is my [a]high retreat.
My God in His lovingkindness will meet me;God will let me look triumphantly upon those who lie in wait for me.
Do not slay them, lest my people forget;Scatter them by Your power, and bring them down,O Lord our shield.
For the sin of their mouth,For the word of their lips,Let them even be taken in their arrogance,And because of the cursing and the lying that they speak.
Consume them in wrath; consume them so that they are no more;And men will know that God rules in JacobUnto the ends of the earth. Selah
They return in the evening;They growl like a dogAnd go around the city.
They wander about for food;And if they are not filled, they [1]linger.
[1]But I will sing of Your strength;And I will joyfully sing of Your [a]lovingkindness in the morning.For You have been my [b]high retreatAnd a [c]refuge in the day of my distress.

O my strength, unto You I will sing psalms,For God is my high retreat,My God of lovingkindness.Psa 60:Title To the choir director: according to the [*]lily of the testimony. A [**]Michtam [a]of David, for instruction, when he struggled with [b]Aram-naharaim and with Aram-zobah, and Joab returned and struck twelve thousand of Edom in the Valley of Salt

60.text: O God, You have rejected us; You have broken out upon us;You have been angry. Restore us.
You have caused the earth to quake; You have split it open.Heal the breaks in it, for it totters.
You have shown Your people hard things;You have made us drink wine to stagger us.
You have given a [a]banner to those who fear You,To be displayed because of the truth. Selah
[a]That Your beloved ones may be delivered,Save with Your right hand, and answer [1]us.
God has spoken in His [1a]holiness: I will exult;I will divide ShechemAnd measure out the Valley of Succoth.
Gilead is Mine, and Manasseh is Mine,And Ephraim is the helmet for My head;Judah is My [a]scepter.
[a]Moab is My washbowl;Upon [b]Edom I will cast My sandal;Philistia, shout aloud because of Me.
Who will bring me into the besieged city?Who will lead me to Edom?
Will not You, O God, who [a]rejected us?And will You not go forth, O God, with our armies?
Give us help from the adversary,For a salvation by man is vain.
In God we will do valiantly,And it is He who will [a]tread down our adversaries.Psa 61:Title To the choir director: on the stringed instruments. [a]Of David

61.text: Hear, O God, my cry;Give heed to my prayer.
From the end of the earthI will call out unto YouWhen my heart faints;Lead me to the [a]rock that is higher than I.
For You are a [a]refuge to me,A strong tower before the enemy.
Let me sojourn in Your [a]tent forever;Let me take [b]refuge in the covering of Your [c]wings. Selah
For You, O God, have heard my vows;You have given me the inheritance of those who fear Your name.
You will add [a]days to the days of the king;May his years be like generation after generation.
May he dwell forever before God;Appoint lovingkindness and [1]faithfulness, that they may preserve him.
So I will sing psalms to Your name forever,As I [1]repay my vows day by day.Psa 62:Title To the choir director, to Jeduthun. A [a]Psalm of David

62.text: My soul waits in silence for God alone;My salvation is from Him.
He alone is my [a]rock and my salvation,My [b]high retreat; I will not be greatly shaken.
How long will you assail a manSo that you may slay him, all of you,Like a leaning wall or a tottering fence?
They only make plans to thrust him down from his dignity;They take delight in lies;With their mouth they bless,But inwardly they curse. Selah
My soul, wait in silence for God alone,For my expectation is from Him.
He alone is my [a]rock and my salvation,My [b]high retreat; I will not be shaken.
On God my salvation and my glory depend;The [a]rock of my strength, my [b]refuge, is in God.
Trust in Him at all times, O people;[a]Pour out your heart before Him;God is a [b]refuge to us. Selah
Men of low degree are but a vapor;Men of high degree, a lie.In the balances they go up;Together they are less than a [a]vapor.
Do not trust in oppression,And do not vainly hope in robbery;If wealth increases,Do not set your heart on it.
Once has God spoken;Twice have I heard this,That strength belongs to God.
To You also, O Lord, belongs lovingkindness;For You [a]repay every man according to his work.Psa 63:Title A [a]Psalm of David, when he was in the [b]wilderness of Judah

63.text: O God, You are my God; I seek You diligently.My soul [a]thirsts for You,My flesh faints in longing for You,In a dry and weary land without water.
So I have looked upon You in the [a]sanctuary,Seeing Your power and Your glory.
Because Your lovingkindness is better than life,My lips praise You.
Thus I bless You while I live;I [a]lift up my hands in Your name.
My soul is [a]satisfied as with the rich fatness,And my mouth praises You with lips of joyous shouting,
When I remember You while on my bed,When I consider You in the night watches.
For You have been my help,And in the shadow of Your [a]wings I will shout for joy.
My soul pursues after You;Your right hand upholds me.
But those who seek my [1]life to destroy itWill go into the [a]lower parts of the earth;
They will be given over to the power of the sword;They will be the portion of foxes.
But the king will rejoice in God;Everyone who swears by Him will boast;For the mouth of those who speak falsehood will be stopped.Psa 64:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

64.text: Hear, O God, my voice in my complaint;Preserve my life from the enemy’s dread.
Hide me from the secret counsel of evildoers,From the throng of the workers of iniquity,
Who have sharpened their tongue like a sword,Who have aimed their arrow, their bitter word,
To shoot in secret places at the [1]perfect man.They shoot suddenly at him and do not fear.
They firm up for themselves an evil matter;They talk of laying snares secretly;They say, Who will see them?
They seek out injustices, saying:We have completed a carefully sought-out plan.Indeed each man’s inward thoughtAnd heart are deep.
But God will shoot an arrow at them;Suddenly they are wounded.
They are made to stumble over each other by their own tongue;All who see them shake their heads.
And all men will fearAnd declare what has been done by God,And they will ponder His work.
The righteous man will rejoice in Jehovah and [a]take refuge in Him,And all the upright in heart will boast.Psa 65:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David; a Song

65.text: Praise awaits You, O God, in [a]Zion;And to You is the vow paid.
O You who [a]hear prayer,To You all [b]flesh comes.
Iniquities prevail against me;As for our transgressions, You make [a]propitiation for them.
Blessed is he whom You have [a]chosen and bring [b]nearTo [c]dwell in Your courts.We will be [d]satisfied with the goodness of Your house,Of Your holy temple.
By awesome deeds You answer us in righteousness,O [a]God of our salvation,You who are the trust of all the [b]ends of the earthAnd of the remotest sea;
Who established the mountains in His strength,Girded with might;
Who [a]stilled the roaring of the seas,The roaring of their billows,And the tumult of the peoples.
And they who dwell in the uttermost partsAre afraid of Your signs;You make the exitways of the morning and the eveningShout for joy.
You visit the earth and [a]water it;You enrich it greatly:The [b]river of God is full of water.You provide [1]men grain, for so You have prepared [2]the earth.
Saturating its furrows, settling its ridges,You soften it with [a]showers;You bless its sprouting forth.
You place a crown on a year with Your goodness,And Your wagon tracks drop fatness;
The wilderness pastures overflow,And the hills gird themselves with exultation.
The meadows are clothed with the flock,And the valleys cover themselves in grain;They shout for joy; indeed they [a]sing.Psa 66:Title To the choir director. A Song; a Psalm

66.text: Shout joyfully to God, all the earth;
[1]Sing forth the glory of His name;Make glorious His praise.
Say to God, How awesome are Your deeds!Because of the greatness of Your strength, Your enemies come cringingto You.
All the earth will [a]worship YouAnd sing psalms to You;They will sing psalms to Your name. Selah
Come and see the works of God;He is awesome in His deeds toward the sons of men.
He turned the sea into [a]dry land;Through the river they crossed on foot.There we rejoiced in Him.
He rules by His [a]might forever;His eyes observe the nations:Let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah
Bless our God, O peoples;And make the voice of His praise heard,
Who has kept our soul in lifeAnd does not allow our feet to slip.
For You have [a]tried us, O God;You have [b]refined us as silver is refined.
You have brought us into the net;You have put a heavy burden on our loins.
You have made men ride over our heads;We have gone through [a]fire and water;But You have brought us forth into abundance.
I will come into Your house with burnt offerings;I will pay my vows to You,
Which my lips utteredAnd my mouth spoke when I was in distress.
I will offer to You burnt offerings of fatlingsWith the smoke of rams;I will offer cattle with goats. Selah
Come, hear, all you who fear God,And I will recount what He has done for my soul.
To Him I called out with my mouth,And [1]He was extolled with my tongue.
If I regarded iniquity in my heart,The Lord did [a]not hear.
But indeed God has heard;He has given heed to the voice of my prayer.
Blessed be God,Who has not turned my prayer awayNor His lovingkindness from me.Psa 67:Title To the choir director: on the stringed instruments. A Psalm; a Song

67.text: God be gracious to us and bless us;May He make His face [a]shine upon us; Selah
That Your way may be known on earth,And Your salvation among all the nations.
Let the peoples praise You, O God;Let the peoples praise You, all of them.
Let the nations rejoice and shout for joy,For You will judge the peoples equitablyAnd will guide the nations on the earth. Selah
Let the peoples praise You, O God;Let the peoples praise You, all of them.
The earth has yielded its increase;God, our God, blesses us.
God blesses us;And all the ends of the earth will fear Him.Psa 68:Title To the choir director. [a]Of David. A Psalm; a Song

68.text: [1]Let God [a]arise; let His [2]enemies be scattered;And let those who hate Him flee before Him.
As smoke is driven away, You will drive them away;As wax melts before fire,The wicked will perish [a]before God.
But the righteous will rejoice; they will exult before GodAnd be glad with rejoicing.
Sing to God; sing psalms to His name;[1]Triumph in Him who rides through the [a]deserts—For [2b]Jah is His name—and [1]exult before Him.
A Father to the [1]orphans and a Judge for the widowsIs God in His holy [a]habitation.
God causes the solitary to dwell in a household;He brings the prisoners forth into prosperity;But the rebellious dwell in a parched land.
[a]O God, when You went forth [b]before Your people,When You marched through the desert, Selah
The earth [a]trembled, the heavens also dripped,At the presence of God;Sinai there quakedAt the presence of God, the God of Israel.
You shed forth bountiful [a]rain, O God;You confirmed Your inheritance when it languished.
Your living flock dwelt in [1]the land;In Your goodness You provided for the poor, O God.
The Lord gives the command;The [1a]women who [2]bear the [b]glad tidings are a great host.
The [1]kings of the armies flee.They flee!And [2]she who [3]abides at home[a]Divides the [4]spoil.
Though you [1]lie among the sheepfolds,There are [2]dove wings covered with [a]silver,And its pinions, with greenish-yellow gold.
When the [1]Almighty scatteredThe [2a]kings in [3]the land,It snowed on Zalmon.
O [1]mighty mountain, O mountain of Bashan,O many-peaked mountain, O mountain of Bashan:
Why do you look with envy, O many-peaked mountains,At the [1a]mountain on which God desires to dwell?Indeed Jehovah will dwell there forever.
The [a]chariots of God are twenty thousand,Thousands upon thousands;The Lord is among them,As at [b]Sinai, in the sanctuary.
You have [1a]ascended on high; You have led captive [2]those taken captive;You have received [3]gifts among men,Even the rebellious ones also,That [4]Jehovah God may [5]dwell among them.
Blessed be the Lord, who day by day [1]loads us with [2]good;God is our salvation. Selah
God is to usA God of deliverance,And with Jehovah the LordAre the [1]goings forth even from [a]death.
Indeed God will [1]smashThe [a]head of His enemies,The hairy crownOf him who goes about in his guiltiness.
The Lord said, I will bring them again from Bashan;I will bring them again from the depths of the sea,
That you may [1]bathe your foot in blood,That the tongue of your dogs may have their portion from the enemies.
[1]They have seen Your [1]goings, O God,The goings of my God, my [a]King, in the [1]sanctuary:
Singers go before; players after;In the midst of [1]virgins sounding the tambourines.
Bless God in the congregations,Even [1]Jehovah, O you who are of the [a]fountain of Israel.
There are little [1a]Benjamin, who rules them,And the princes of [2b]Judah in their company,The princes of [3c]Zebulun, the princes of [4d]Naphtali.
Your God has commanded your strength;[1]Strengthen, O God, that which You have done for us.
Because of Your temple at Jerusalem,[1]Kings will bring a [a]gift to You.
Rebuke the [1]animals of the reeds,The herd of the bulls among the calves of the peoples,Trampling on those who lust after silver;Scatter the [2]peoples who take delight in war.
Nobles will come out of [a]Egypt;[1b]Cush will quickly stretch out its hands to God.
O [a]kingdoms of the [b]earth,Sing to God,Sing psalms to the Lord, Selah
To Him who rides upon the ancient [a]heaven of heavens.Behold, He utters His voice, a mighty voice.
Ascribe strength to God;His excellence is over Israel,And His strength is in the skies.
You are awesome, O God, from Your sanctuaries.The God of Israel, He gives strength and power to the people.Blessed be God!Psa 69:Title To the choir director: according to [*]Shoshannim. [a]Of David

69.text: [1]Save me, O God; for the watersThreaten my life.
I sink in deep mire,And there is no place to stand;I have come to the depths of water,And the flood flows over me.
I am weary of my crying;My throat is parched;My eyes failWhile I wait for my God.
More numerous than the hairs of my headAre those who [a]hate me without cause;Mighty are those who would destroy me,Those who are wrongly my enemies.What I did not steal,I must then restore.
O God, You know my foolishness,And my sins of guilt are not hidden from You.
Do not let those who wait on You be put to shame because of me,O Lord Jehovah of hosts;Do not let those who seek You be humiliated because of me,O God of Israel.
Because for Your sake I have borne [a]reproach;Humiliation has covered my face.
I have become a stranger to my [a]brothers,And a foreigner to my mother’s children.
For the [a]zeal of Your house has devoured me,And the [b]reproaches of those who reproach You have fallen on me.
When I [a]wept, when my soul was in fasting,That became a reproach to me.
When I made sackcloth my clothing,I became a [a]proverb to them.
Those who sit in the gate [a]talk about me,And I am the mocking song of those who drink liquor.
But as for me, my prayer is to You,O Jehovah, in an [a]acceptable time;O God, in the abundance of Your lovingkindness answer me,In the faithfulness of Your salvation.
Deliver me out of the mire, and let me not sink;May I be delivered from those who hate me,And out of the depths of water.
Do not let the flood of water flow over me,Nor let the deep swallow me,Nor let the pit close its mouth over me.
Answer me, O Jehovah; for Your lovingkindness is good;According to the abundance of Your compassions turn to me.
And do not hide Your face from Your servant,For I am in distress; hurry and answer me.
Draw near to my soul; redeem it;Ransom me because of my enemies.
You know my [a]reproachAnd my shame and my humiliation;All my adversaries are before You.
Reproach has broken my heart,And I am sick;I looked for pity, but there was none;And for comforters, but I found [a]none.
But they gave me [a]gall for my food,And for my [b]thirst they gave me [c]vinegar to drink.
[1a]May their table become a snare before themAnd a trap when they are at peace.
May their eyes be darkened so that they cannot [a]see;And make their loins shake continually.
Pour out Your indignation upon them;And may the burning fierceness of Your anger overtake them.
[a]May their camp be desolate;May there be no one to dwell in their tents.
For they persecute him whom You have [a]stricken,And they recount the pain of those whom You have wounded.
Add iniquity to their iniquity,And do not let them come into Your righteousness.
May they be blotted out of the [a]book of life,And may they not be written with the righteous.
But I am poor and pained;May Your salvation, O God, set me on high.
I will praise the name of God with a songAnd will [a]magnify Him with thanksgiving;
And it will be better to Jehovah than an oxOr a bull with horns and hooves.
The meek have seen it; they rejoice.You who seek God, let your heart revive.
For Jehovah listens to the needy,And He does not despise His prisoners.
[a]Let the heavens and the earth praise Him,The seas and everything that moves in them.
For God will save [a]ZionAnd build the cities of Judah;And they will abide there and possess it.
And the seed of His servants will inherit it,And those who love His name will dwell in it.Psa 70:Title To the choir director. [a]Of David, for remembrance

70.text: [a]O God, hasten to deliver me;O Jehovah, hasten to help me.
[a]May those who seek my lifeBe put to shame and confounded;May those who take delight in my misfortuneBe turned back and humiliated;
May those who say, Aha! Aha!Turn away because of their shame;
May all those who seek YouBe glad and rejoice in You;And may those who love Your salvationSay continually,May God be magnified!
But I am poor and needy;O God, hasten to me.You are my help and my Deliverer;O Jehovah, do not delay.

71.text: [a]In You, O Jehovah, do I [b]take refuge;Never let me be put to shame.
In Your righteousness deliver me and rescue me;Incline Your ear to me and save me.
Be to me a [a]rock of habitation for me to come to continually.You have given command to save me,For You are my rock and my [b]fortress.
My God, rescue me from the hand of the wicked,From the hand of the unjust and cruel;
For You are my hope, O Lord Jehovah,My trust from my youth.
On You I have been sustained since [a]birth;It was You who took me from my mother’s [b]womb;My praise is continually of You.
I am like a wonder to many,But You are my strong [a]refuge.
My mouth is filled with Your praise,With Your [1]glory, all day long.
Do not cast me off in the time of [a]old age;When my strength fails, do not forsake me.
For my enemies speak concerning me,And those who watch for my soul take [a]counsel together,
Saying, God has forsaken him;Pursue and lay hold of him, for there is no one to deliver him.
O God, do not be far away from me;My God, hasten to help me.
May those who are the adversaries to my soulBe put to shame and consumed;May those who seek my misfortuneWrap themselves in reproach and humiliation.
But I will hope continuallyAnd will add yet more to all Your praise.
My mouth will recount Your righteousness,Your salvation, all day long;For I do not know its full numbers.
I will come with the proof of the mighty acts of the Lord Jehovah;I will remind men of Your righteousness, Yours alone.
O God, You have taught me from my youth;And until now I have declared Your wondrous deeds.
So even into my [a]old age and grayheadedness,O God, do not forsake me,Until I have declared Your strengthUnto the next generation,Your might unto everyone who is to come.
And Your righteousness, O God, is so high,You who have done [a]great things.O God, [b]who is like You?
You who have shown [1]meMany great troublesWill [a]revive [1]me againAnd bring [1]me up againFrom the depths of the earth.
You will increase my greatnessAnd will turn and comfort me.
I will also praise You with the harp,I will praise Your [1]faithfulness, my God;I will sing psalms to You with the lyre,O Holy One of Israel.
My lips will give a ringing shoutWhen I sing psalms to You,And my soul, which You have redeemed.
My tongue also will declareYour righteousness all day long;For they are put to shame, for they are confounded,They who seek my misfortune.Psa 72:Title [a]Of Solomon

72.text: O God, give Your judgments to the [1]king,And Your righteousness to the son of the king.
He will judge Your people in [1a]righteousness,And Your poor with [1]justice.
The mountains will bear [a]peace to the people,And the hills, in righteousness.
He will judge the poor of the people;He will save the children of the needyAnd crush the oppressor.
They will fear You as long as the sun enduresAnd as long as the moon enduresThroughout the generations.
He will [1]drop like rain upon mown grass,Like abundant [a]showers dripping on the earth.
In His days the righteous will flourish,And there will be an abundance of peaceUntil the [a]moon is no more.
And He will have [a]dominion from sea to seaAnd from the [1b]River unto the ends of the earth.
The desert dwellers will bow down before Him,And His enemies will lick the dust.
The kings of Tarshish and of the [1]coastlandsWill pay tribute;The kings of Sheba and SebaWill present [a]gifts.
And all the [a]kings will bow down before Him;All the nations will serve Him.
For He will deliver the needy man when he cries,And the poor man who has no one to help him.
He will have pity on the weak and needy,And the souls of the needy He will save.
From oppression and violenceHe will redeem their soul,And their blood will be [a]precious in His sight.
So He shall live and to Him shall be givenThe [a]gold of Sheba;And prayer shall be made continually for Him;He shall be blessed all the day long.
There will be an abundance of grain on the earth,Even at the tops of the mountains.Its fruit will wave like the forests of Lebanon,And those of the city will blossom like the foliage of the earth.
His name will be forever;As long as the sun endures, His name will spread;And men will be [a]blessed in Him;All the nations will call Him [b]blessed.
[1a]Blessed be Jehovah God, the God of Israel,Who alone does wondrous deeds;
And blessed be His glorious name forever;And may His [a]glory fill the whole earth.[b]Amen and Amen.
The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.Psa 73:Title A [a]Psalm of Asaph

73.text: [1]Surely God is good to Israel,To those who are [2a]pure in heart.
But as for me, my feet were nearly [1]turned aside;My steps had almost slipped.
For I was envious of the arrogant,When I saw the [a]prosperity of the wicked.
For they have no pangs in their death,And their body is well nourished.
They do not find themselves in the hardship of men,Nor are they plagued like other men.
Therefore pride is a necklace for them,Violence covers them like a garment.
Their eyes bulge out from fatness;The imaginations of their heart overflow.
They mock and wickedly speak of oppression;They speak loftily.
They set their mouth against the heavens,And their [a]tongue walks about on the earth.
Therefore His people return here,And waters of abundance are drained by them;
And they say, How does God know?And is there knowledge with the Most High?
Behold, these are the wicked;And always at ease, they [a]heap up riches.
Surely I have purified my heart in [1]vain,And I have [a]washed my hands in innocence.
For I have been plagued all day longAnd chastened every morning.
If I had said, I will speak thus;Behold, I would have betrayed the generation of Your children.
When I considered this in order to understand it,It was a troublesome task in my sight,
Until I went into the [1a]sanctuary of God;Then I perceived their end.
Surely You set them in slippery places;You cast them down into ruins.
How they are made desolate in a moment!They are utterly consumed by terrors.
Like a dream, when one awakes, You, O Lord,Upon arising, will despise their image.
When my heart was embittered,And inwardly I was pricked;
I was brutish and knew nothing;I was like a [a]beast before You.
Nevertheless I am continually [a]with You;You have taken hold of my [b]right hand.
You will guide me with Your counsel,And afterward You will receive me in [a]glory.
[1]Whom do I have in heaven but You?And besides You there is nothing I [a]desire on earth.
My flesh and my heart fail,But God is the [a]rock of my heart and my [1b]portion forever.
For behold, those who are far from You will perish;You destroy all who go about as harlots away from You.
But as for me, [a]drawing near to God is good for me;I have made the Lord Jehovah my [b]refuge,That I may declare all Your works.Psa 74:Title A [*]Maschil [a]of Asaph

74.text: [1]Why, O God, have You [a]cast us off forever?Why does Your anger smoke against the [b]sheep of Your pasture?
Remember Your [1]assembly, which You have [a]purchased of old,Which You have redeemed as the tribe of Your inheritance,And Mount [1b]Zion, where You dwell.
Lift up Your steps unto the perpetual ruins;The enemy has damaged everything in the [a]sanctuary.
Your adversaries have roared in the midst of Your assembly place;They have set up their symbols as signs.
It looked like when men bring up axesOn the thicket of the trees.
And now they have struck down with hatchet and hammerIts [a]carved work altogether.
They have [a]set fire to Your sanctuary;They have defiled the dwelling place of Your name to the ground.
They said in their heart, Let us oppress them completely.They have burned down all God’s assembly places in the land.
We do not see our signs; there is no longer a [a]prophet,Nor is there anyone among us who knows how long.
How long, O God, will the adversary reproach?Will the enemy despise Your name forever?
Why do You withdraw Your hand, even Your [a]right hand?Bring it forth from the midst of Your bosom; destroy them.
[1]But God is my [a]King of old,Accomplishing salvation in the midst of the earth.
You [a]divided the sea by Your strength;You broke the heads of the sea serpents on the waters.
You crushed the heads of the [1]leviathan;You gave him as food to the creatures of the desert.
You broke open the [a]fountain and the torrent;You [b]dried up the ever-flowing streams.
The day is Yours; the night also is Yours;You prepared the moon and the sun.
You established all the [a]borders of the earth:You formed the summer and the winter.
Remember this: The enemy has reproached Jehovah,And a foolish people has despised Your name.
Do not give the soul of Your turtledove to the wild beast;Do not forget the life of Your poor forever.
Regard the [a]covenant,For the dark places of the earth are full of the habitations of violence.
Do not let him who is oppressed return ashamed.May the poor and needy praise Your name.
Arise, O God; contend for Your own cause;Remember how the foolish man reproaches You all day long.
Do not forget the voice of Your adversaries;The roar of those who rise up against You goes up continually.Psa 75:Title To the choir director: [*]Do not destroy. A [a]Psalm of Asaph; a Song

75.text: [1]We give thanks to You, O God; we give thanks,For Your name is near; men tell of Your wondrous deeds.
When I choose the appointed [1]time,It is I who [a]judge with equity.
The earth and all who dwell in it melt;I have established its pillars. Selah
I said to the boastful, Do not boast;And to the wicked, Do not lift up the horn.
Do not lift your horn on high;Do not speak arrogantly with a haughty neck.
For neither from the east nor from the west,And neither from the [1]south, does exaltation come;
For God is the Judge:He puts this one down and [a]exalts that one.
For there is a [a]cup in the hand of Jehovah,And the wine foams; it is full of mixture,And He pours from it; indeed its dregs will all the wicked of the earthDrain off and drink up.
But as for me, I will declare forever,I will sing psalms to the God of Jacob.
And all the horns of the wicked will I cut off,But the horns of the righteous man will be exalted.Psa 76:Title To the choir director: on the stringed instruments. A [a]Psalm of Asaph; a Song

76.text: [1]God is known in Judah;His name is great in Israel.
And in [1a]Salem is His tabernacle,And His dwelling place, in [2b]Zion.
[1]There He broke the flashing arrows of the [a]bow,The shield and the sword and the battle. Selah
More glorious are You,More excellent than the mountains of prey.
The stouthearted are despoiled;They slept their sleep,And none of the men of strengthHave found their hands.
At Your rebuke, O God of Jacob,Both chariot and horse fall into [a]deep sleep.
You, indeed You, are to be feared;And who may [a]stand before You once You are angry?
You caused judgment to be heard from the heavens;The earth feared and was still,
When God rose for judgment,To save all the meek of the earth. Selah
For the wrath of man will praise You;You will gird Yourself with the remainder of wrath.
Make vows and pay them to Jehovah your God;Let all who are around Him bring gifts to Him who is to be feared.
He will cut off the spirit of the princes;He is terrible to the kings of the earth.Psa 77:Title To the choir director, to Jeduthun. [a]Of Asaph. A Psalm

77.text: [1]My voice calls out to God, and I will cry out;My voice calls out to God, and He will give ear to me.
In the day of my distress I sought the Lord;My hand was stretched out in the night, and it did not drop.My soul refused to be comforted.
I remember God, and I moan;I complain, and my spirit faints. Selah
You hold open my eyelids;I am disturbed and cannot speak.
I consider the days of old,The years of ancient times;
I remember my [a]song in the night;I muse with my own heart, and my [b]spirit carefully searches.
Will the Lord [1a]cast us off forever,And will He never be favorable to us again?
Has His lovingkindness ceased forever?Has the promise come to an end for all generations?
Has God forgotten to be gracious?Or has He in anger shut up His compassions? Selah
Then I said, This is my infirmity;[1]But I recall the years of the right hand of the Most High.
I will bring to remembrance the deeds of [1]Jehovah,For I will remember Your wonders from of old.
[a]And I will meditate on all that You have doneAnd muse on Your doings.
O God, Your way is in the [1a]sanctuary;Who is so [b]great a god as God?
You are the God who does wonders;You have made known Your strength among the peoples.
You [a]redeemed Your people with Your arm,The sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
The [a]waters saw You, O God;The waters saw You; they were afraid;Indeed the depths trembled.
The clouds poured forth water;The skies sent forth a sound;Indeed Your arrows went here and there.
The sound of Your thunder was in the whirlwind;Lightning illuminated the world;The earth [a]trembled and quaked.
Your way was in the [a]sea,And Your paths in the great waters;And Your footsteps were not known.
You led Your people like a [a]flockBy the hand of Moses and Aaron.Psa 78:Title A [*]Maschil [a]of Asaph

78.text: [a]Give ear, O my people, to my [1]instruction;Incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
[a]I will open my mouth in a [b]parable;I will utter riddles from of old,
Which we have heard and known,And our fathers have related to us.
We will not hide them from their children,But will relate to the generation to comeThe praises of Jehovah and His strengthAnd His wondrous deeds that He has done.
Indeed He has established a testimony in JacobAnd appointed a law in Israel,Which He commanded our fathersThat they should make them known to their [a]children;
That the generation to come,The children to be born, may know them,Who should arise and relate them to their children;
That they might set their hope in God,And not forget the deeds of God,But observe His commandments;
That they might not be like their fathers,A stubborn and rebellious generation,A [a]generation that did not set their [b]heart right,And whose spirit was not faithful to God.
The children of Ephraim, armed archers with bows,Turned back in the day of battle.
They did not keep the covenant of God,And they refused to walk in His law.
And they forgot His doingsAnd His wondrous deeds that He had shown them.
He did something [a]wonderful in the sight of their fathersIn the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.
He [a]divided the sea and caused them to pass through,And He made the water stand as a [b]heap.
And He led them with the [a]cloud by dayAnd all the night with a light of fire.
He split [a]rocks in the wildernessAnd gave them abundant drink, as from the depths.
He also brought forth streams from the rockAnd made water run down like rivers.
Yet they still continued to sin against Him,To rebel against the Most High in the desert.
And they put God to the [a]test in their heart,By asking for food according to their desire.
They also spoke against God;They said, Can God prepare a [a]table in the [b]wilderness?
Behold, He struck the [a]rock,And water gushed out,And streams overflowed;Can He also give us bread,Or provide meat for His people?
Therefore Jehovah heard and was [a]angry.And a fire was kindled against Jacob,And also anger went up against Israel;
Because they did [a]not believe in God,And they did not trust in His salvation.
Yet He commanded the skies aboveAnd opened the doors of heaven;
And He rained down upon them [a]manna to eatAnd gave them the [b]food of heaven.
Man ate the bread of the mighty;He sent them full provision.
He made the east [a]wind blow in the heavensAnd guided the south wind by His strength.
And He rained upon them meat like dust,And winged [a]birds like the sand of the seas.
And He made it fall in the midst of their camp,Around their habitations.
So they ate and were well filled;Indeed what they desired He brought to them.
They were not estranged from what they desired;Their food was still in their mouths
When the [a]anger of God went up against themAnd slew some of their fattest onesAnd struck down the choice men of Israel.
In spite of all this they still sinnedAnd did [a]not believe in His wondrous deeds.
Therefore He consumed their days with vanity,And their years with terror.
When He slew them, they [a]inquired after Him,And they returned and diligently sought God.
And they remembered that God was their [a]rock,And the Most High God, their Redeemer.
But they flattered Him with their mouthAnd lied to Him with their tongue.
For their [a]heart was not steadfast toward Him,Nor were they faithful to His covenant.
But He, being [a]merciful, forgave their iniquityAnd did not destroy them;Indeed He turned back His anger many timesAnd did not stir up all His wrath.
And He remembered that they were [a]flesh,A [1b]wind that goes away and does not return.
How often they rebelled against Him in the wildernessAnd [a]grieved Him in the desert!
And they repeatedly put God to the [a]testAnd provoked the [b]Holy One of Israel.
They did not remember His hand,The day when He ransomed them from the adversary;
[a]When He set His signs in EgyptAnd His wonders in the field of Zoan;
And He turned their rivers into [a]blood,As well as their streams, so that they could not drink.
He sent among them a swarm of [a]flies, which devoured them;And [b]frogs, which destroyed them.
And He gave their increase to the consuming [a]locust,And their labor to the swarming locust.
And He killed their vines with [a]hail,And their sycamores with [1]ice stones.
He gave over their [a]cattle also to the hailAnd their herds to the lightning bolts.
He sent among them the burning fierceness of His anger,Overflowing wrath and indignation and trouble,A mission of angels of evils.
He leveled a path for His anger;He did not spare their soul from death,But gave their life over to the [a]pestilence.
And He [a]struck all the firstborn in Egypt,The firstfruits of their vigor in the tents of Ham.
But He set forth His people like sheepAnd led them like a [a]flock in the wilderness.
And He led them to safety, so that they did not fear;But the sea [a]covered their enemies.
And He brought them forth to His holy border,The [a]mountain, which His right hand had acquired.
And He [a]drove out the nations before themAnd allotted them as an [b]inheritance by line,And He made the tribes of Israel dwell there in their tents.
Yet they put Him to the test and rebelled against God the Most High,And did not keep His testimonies.
And they turned back and dealt treacherously, as their fathers had done;They turned aside like a deceitful bow.
Indeed they provoked Him to anger with their [a]high placesAnd made Him [b]jealous with their graven images.
God heard, and He was angered;And He greatly abhorred Israel.
Thus He forsook the tabernacle in [a]Shiloh,The tent in which He dwelt among men;
And He gave over His [1a]Ark into captivity,And His [2]glory into the hand of the adversary.
He also delivered His people to the swordAnd was angry with His inheritance.
Fire devoured their young men,And their virgins had no marriage song.
Their priests [a]fell by the sword,And their widows did not lament.
Then the Lord awoke as if from sleep,Like a mighty man who shouts because of wine.
And He struck His adversaries and sent them back;He put an everlasting reproach upon them.
And He rejected the tent of Joseph,And the tribe of Ephraim He did not choose.
Rather He [a]chose the tribe of Judah,Mount [b]Zion, which He loved.
And He built His sanctuary like the heights,Like the earth that He has founded forever.
Moreover He chose David His servantAnd took him from the [a]sheepfolds;
From following after the ewes He brought himTo [1a]shepherd Jacob His peopleAnd Israel His inheritance.
And he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart,And guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.Psa 79:Title A [a]Psalm of Asaph

79.text: O God, the [a]nations have come into Your inheritance;They have defiled Your holy temple;They have turned [b]Jerusalem into heaps.
They have given the [a]dead bodies of Your servantsAs food for the birds of heaven,The flesh of Your saints to the animals of the earth.
They have poured out their blood like water around Jerusalem,And there was no one to bury them.
We have become a reproach to our neighbors,A mocking and a derision to those around us.
How long, O Jehovah? Will You be angry forever?Will Your [a]jealousy burn like fire?
[a]Pour out Your wrath upon the nationsWho do [b]not know You,And upon the kingdomsWho do not call upon Your name.
For they have devoured JacobAnd have desolated his habitation.
Do not remember against us the iniquities of our forefathers;May Your compassions quickly meet us,For we have been brought very low.
Help us, O God of our salvation,For the glory of Your name;And deliver us and expiate our sins,For Your name’s sake.
Why should the nations say,Where is their God?May the [a]avenging of the blood of Your servants, which has been poured out,Be known among the nations in our sight.
May the sighing of the prisoner come before You.According to the greatness of Your power preserve those appointed to die,
And render to our neighbors sevenfold into their bosomTheir reproach, with which they have reproached You, O Lord.

And we, Your people and the [a]sheep of Your pasture,Will give You thanks forever;From generation to generation we will tell out Your praise.Psa 80:Title To the choir director: according to [*]shoshannim-eduth. [a]Of Asaph. A Psalm

80.text: O [a]Shepherd of Israel, give ear,You who lead Joseph like a [b]flock;You who [c]are enthroned between the cherubim, shine forth.
Before Ephraim and Benjamin and ManassehStir up Your might,And come to save us.
O God, restore us;And cause Your face to [a]shine, and we will be saved.
O Jehovah God of hosts,How long will You fumeAgainst the prayer of Your people?
You have fed them with the bread of tearsAnd have made them drink tears in large measure.
You have made us a cause of strife to our neighbors,And our enemies deride us among themselves.
O God of hosts, restore us;And cause Your face to [a]shine, and we will be saved.
You brought a [1a]vine out of Egypt;You [b]drove out nations and planted it;
You cleared the ground before it,And it took deep root and filled the land.
The mountains were covered with its shadow,And its boughs were like the cedars of God.
It put forth its branches unto the sea,And its shoots unto the [1a]River.
Why have You broken down its [a]hedges,So that all who pass by on the way pluck it?
The boar from the forest ravages it,And that which moves through the field feeds on it.
O God of hosts, turn, we beseech You;Look down from heaven and see,And [1]visit this vine,
Even the [a]stock which Your right hand has plantedAnd the [1]son whom You have strengthened for Yourself.
It is burned with fire; it is cut down;They perish at the rebuke of Your countenance.
Let Your [a]hand be upon the [1]man of Your right hand,Upon the [b]son of man whom You have strengthened for Yourself;
Then we will not turn back from You.Revive us, and we will [1a]call upon Your name.
O Jehovah God of hosts, restore us;Cause Your face to [a]shine, and we will be saved.Psa 81:Title To the choir director: on the [*]gittith. [a]Of Asaph

81.text: [1]Sing for joy to God our strength;Make a [a]joyful noise to the God of Jacob.
Lift up a song and sound the tambourine,The pleasant lyre with the harp.
Blow the [a]trumpet at the new moon,At the full moon, on our feast day.
For that is a [1]statute of Israel,An ordinance of the God of Jacob.
He appointed it as a testimony in JosephWhen He went out over the land of Egypt.I heard a language that I did not know:
I turned [1]your shoulder from the [a]burden;Your hands were freed from the basket.
You called in trouble, and I delivered you;I answered you from the secret place of thunder;I proved you at the waters of [a]Meribah. Selah
[a]Hear, O My people, and I will testify [1]against you:O Israel, if you would listen to Me!
There shall be no [a]strange god among you,Neither shall you worship any [b]foreign god.
I am [a]Jehovah your God,Who brought you up out of the land of Egypt;Open your [b]mouth wide, and I will fill it.
But My people did not listen to My voice,And Israel would not consent to Me.
So I [a]gave them over to the stubbornness of their heart:They walked after their own counsels.
Oh that My people had listened to Me,That Israel had walked in My ways!
I would have soon subdued their enemiesAnd turned My hand against their adversaries.
Those who hate Jehovah would have come cringing to Him,And their time would be forever.
And He would have fed them with the finest of [a]wheat;Indeed with [b]honey from the rock I would have satisfied you.Psa 82:Title A [a]Psalm of Asaph

82.text: God stands in the congregation of God;He [a]judges in the midst of the [1]gods.
How long will you judge unjustlyAnd respect the persons of the wicked? Selah
Judge the poor and the orphan;Execute justice for the afflicted and the destitute;
Rescue the poor and the needy;Deliver them from the hand of the wicked.
[1]You do not know, nor do you understand;You go about in darkness;All the foundations of the earth are shaken.
[a]I said, You are gods,And all of you are [b]sons of the Most High.
Nevertheless you will die like menAnd fall like one of the princes.
Arise, O God; [1a]judge the earth;For You will [1b]inherit all the nations.Psa 83:Title A Song; a [a]Psalm of Asaph

83.text: O God, do not keep silent;Do not be quiet, and do not be still, O God.
For, behold, Your enemies are in tumult,And those who hate You lift up their heads.
They [1]devise crafty counsel against Your people,And conspire against Your [2a]hidden ones.
They have said, Come and let us [a]destroy them from being a nation,That the name of Israel may no longer be remembered.
For they have [a]conspired together with one heart;It is against You that they have made an alliance:
The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites,Moab and the Hagarites,
Gebal and Ammon and Amalek,Philistia along with the inhabitants of Tyre;
Assyria is also joined with them;They have been a help to the children of Lot. Selah
Do with them as with [a]Midian,As with [b]Sisera and Jabin, at the brook Kishon:
They were destroyed at [a]Endor;They became dung for the earth.
Make their nobles like [a]Oreb and Zeeb,And all their princes like [b]Zebah and Zalmunna,
Who said, Let us possess for ourselvesThe habitations of God.
[1]O my God, make them like a tumbleweed,Like stubble before the wind.
Like fire that burns a forestAnd like a flame that sets mountains on fire,
So pursue them with Your tempest,And terrify them with Your storm.
Fill their faces with shame,That they may seek Your name, O Jehovah.
May they be ashamed and dismayed forever,And may they be confounded and perish;
That they may know that You alone, whose name is Jehovah,Are the [a]Most High over all the earth.Psa 84:Title To the choir director: on the [*]gittith. [a]Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm

84.text: My soul [1]longs, indeed even [1]faints,For the [a]courts of Jehovah;My heart and my flesh cry outTo the living God.
At Your [1]two altars even the [2a]sparrow has found a [3b]home;And the [2]swallow, a [3]nest for herself,Where she may lay her young,O Jehovah of hosts, my [c]King and my God.
Blessed are those who [a]dwell in Your [1]house;They will yet be praising You. Selah
Blessed is the man whose strength is in You,In whose heart are the [1]highways to Zion.
Passing through the valley of [1a]Baca,They make it a [2]spring;Indeed the [3]early rain covers it with blessings.
They go from strength to strength;Each [a]appears before God in [1b]Zion.
O Jehovah God of hosts, hear my prayer;Give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah
Behold our [1]shield, O God;And look upon the face of Your [1]anointed.
For a day in Your courts is better than a thousand;I would rather stand at the threshold of the house of my GodThan dwell in the tents of the wicked.
For Jehovah God is a [1a]sun and a [b]shield;Jehovah gives grace and glory;He does not withhold anything goodFrom [2]those who walk uprightly.
O Jehovah of hosts, [a]blessed is the [1]manWho trusts in You.Psa 85:Title To the choir director. [a]Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm

85.text: [1]You have been pleased, O Jehovah, with Your land;You have [a]turned the captivity of Jacob.
You have [a]forgiven the iniquity of Your people;You have covered all their sin. Selah
You have taken away all Your wrath;You have turned from the fierceness of Your anger.
[1]Restore us, O God of our salvation,And cause Your indignation toward us to cease.
Will You be angry with us forever?Will You extend Your anger from generation to generation?
Will You not again [a]revive us,That Your people may rejoice in You?
Show us, O Jehovah, Your lovingkindness,And grant us Your salvation.
I will hear what God Jehovah will speak,For He will speak [a]peaceTo His people and to His faithful ones;But let them not turn again unto folly.
Surely His salvation is near to those who fear Him,That glory may dwell in our land.
Lovingkindness and truth have met together;[a]Righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
Truth has sprung forth from the earth,And righteousness has looked down from heaven.
Indeed Jehovah will give what is [a]good,And our land will give its increase.
Righteousness will go before HimAnd make His footsteps a way to walk in.Psa 86:Title A Prayer [a]of David

86.text: [1]Incline Your ear, O Jehovah; answer me;For I am poor and needy.
Keep my soul; for I am faithful;Save Your servant who trusts in You, O You who are my God.
Be gracious to me, O Lord;For to You do I call out all day long.
Cause the soul of Your servant to rejoice,For unto You, O Lord, do I [a]lift up my soul.
For You, O Lord, are good and ready to forgiveAnd abundant in lovingkindness to all who call upon You.
[a]Give ear, O Jehovah, to my prayer,And give heed to the voice of my supplications.
In the day of my distress I call upon You,For You answer me.
There is none [a]like You among the gods, O Lord;Nor are there any works like Yours.
All the nations which You have made will comeAnd [a]worship before You, O Lord;And they will glorify Your name.
For You are great, and You do wondrous deeds;You alone are God.
Teach me, O Jehovah, Your way;I will walk in Your truth.Make my heart single in fearing Your name.
I will praise You, O Lord my God, with all my heart;And I will glorify Your name forever.
For Your lovingkindness is great toward me,And You have delivered my soul from the lowest Sheol.
O God, the proud have risen up against me,And a company of violent men seek my life;And they do not [a]set You before them.
But You, O Lord, are a [a]compassionate and gracious God,Long-suffering and abundant in lovingkindness and truth.
Turn to me and be gracious to me;Give Your strength to Your servant,And save the son of Your female servant.
Show me a token of goodness,That those who hate me may see it and be ashamed;For You, O Jehovah, have helped me and comforted me.Psa 87:Title [a]Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm; a Song

87.text: Jehovah [a]loves the [1b]gates of [2c]ZionMore than all the dwellings of Jacob.
Glorious things are spoken of you,O [a]city of God. Selah
I will make mention of [1]Rahab and Babylon as those who know Me;Behold, Philistia and Tyre along with [2]Cush:[3]This one was born there, they say.
But of [a]Zion it will be said,[1]This one and that one were born in her,And the Most High Himself will [b]establish her.
Jehovah will countWhen He records the peoples:This [1]One was born there. Selah

Then singing as well as dancing, they will say,All my [a]springs are in [1]you.Psa 88:Title A Song; a [a]Psalm of the sons of Korah. To the choir director: according to [*]mahalath leannoth. A [**]Maschil of Heman the Ezrahite.

88.text: O Jehovah, the God of my [1]salvation,I have cried out [a]by day and in the night before You.
May my prayer come before You;Incline Your ear to my cry.
For my soul is full of troubles,And my life draws near to Sheol.
I am accounted among those who go down into the pit;I am like a man without any [1]help,
Abandoned among the dead,Like the slain that lie in the grave,Whom You remember no more,And they are cut off from Your hand.
You have put me in the lowest pit,In the dark places, in the depths.
Your wrath lies heavily upon me,And You have afflicted me with all Your waves. Selah
You have put my acquaintances [a]far from me;You have made me an abomination to them.I am shut in and cannot go out.
My eye wastes away at the affliction;I have called out to You, O Jehovah, every day;I have spread out my hands to You.
Will You perform wonders for the dead?Will the [a]deceased rise up and praise You? Selah
Will Your lovingkindness be declared in the grave,Or Your faithfulness in [1]Abaddon?
Will Your wonders be made known in the darkness,Or Your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
But I, O Jehovah, have cried out to You;And in the morning my prayer comes before You.
Why, O Jehovah, have You rejected my soul?Why do You hide Your face from me?
I have been afflicted and about to die since my youth;I bear Your terrors; I am [1]overwhelmed.
Your fierce wrath has gone over me;Your terrors have cut me off.
They surrounded me like water all day long;They altogether encompassed me.
You have put far from meLover and friend;My acquaintances are in darkness.Psa 89:Title A [*]Maschil of Ethan the Ezrahite

89.text: [1]I will sing of the lovingkindness of Jehovah forever;From generation to generation I will make known Your faithfulness withmy mouth.
For I have said, Lovingkindness will be built up forever;In the heavens themselves You will establish Your faithfulness.
I have made a [a]covenant with My chosen one;I have [b]sworn to David My servant:
I will establish your seed [a]forever,And I will build up your throne from generation to generation. Selah
And the heavens will praise Your wonders, O Jehovah,Indeed Your faithfulness in the congregation of the holy ones.
For who in the skies can be compared to Jehovah?Who among the sons of the mighty is like Jehovah,
Like God, greatly feared in the council of the holy onesAnd awesome beyond all around Him?
O Jehovah God of hosts, who is [a]like You, the mighty [1]Jah?Your faithfulness also surrounds You.
You rule the swelling of the sea:When its waves rise up, You [a]still them.
You crushed [1]Rahab like one slain;You [a]scattered Your enemies with the arm of Your strength.
The [a]heavens are Yours; the [b]earth also is Yours;The [c]world and its fullness You have founded.
You created the north and the south;Tabor and Hermon shout for joy at Your name.
You have an arm with might;Your [a]hand is strong; Your right hand is high.
[a]Righteousness and justice are the [1]foundation of Your throne;Lovingkindness and truth go before Your face.
Blessed are the people who know the joyful shout;They walk, O Jehovah, in the [a]light of Your countenance.
In Your name they exult all day long,And in Your righteousness they are exalted.
For You are the [1]glory of their strength,And in Your favor our horn is exalted.
For our shield belongs to Jehovah,And our king, to the Holy One of Israel.
Then You spoke in visionOf [a]Your [1]Holy One, and You said,I have given help to One who is mighty;I have exalted One chosen from My people.
I have [a]found [1]David My Servant;With My holy oil I have [b]anointed Him,
With whom My hand will be established;My arm will also strengthen Him.
The enemy will not exact anything from Him,Nor will the son of wickedness afflict Him.
And I will crush His adversaries before Him,And I will strike those who hate Him.
And My faithfulness and My lovingkindness will be with Him,And in My name His horn will be exalted.
And I will set His hand on the [a]seaAnd His right hand on the rivers.
He will call upon Me, saying, You are My [a]Father,My God and the [b]rock of My salvation.
I will also make Him the [a]Firstborn,The [b]highest of the kings of the earth.
I will keep My lovingkindness for Him forever,And My covenant will stand firm with Him.
And I will establish His [a]seed forever,And His [b]throne as the days of heaven.
If His [a]children forsake My lawAnd do not walk in My ordinances,
If they profane My statutesAnd do not keep My commandments,
I will punish their transgression with a rodAnd their iniquity with stripes.
But My lovingkindness I will not utterly take away from Him,Nor will I be false to My own faithfulness.
I will not profane My covenant,Nor will I change what has gone forth from My lips.
Once I have sworn by My holiness;I will not lie to David.
His [a]seed shall endure forever,And His throne, like the sun before Me;
It shall be established forever like the moon;And the witness in the skies is firm. Selah
But You have [a]cast off and rejected;You have been angry with Your anointed.
You have abhorred the covenant of Your servant;You have profaned his crown by casting it to the ground.
You have broken down all his walls;You have brought his strongholds to ruin.
All who pass by on the way plunder him;He has become a reproach to his neighbors.
You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries;You have caused all his enemies to rejoice.
Indeed You have turned back the edge of his swordAnd have not made him stand in the battle.
You have made his brightness ceaseAnd have cast down his throne to the ground.
You have shortened the days of his youth;You have covered him with shame. Selah
How long, O Jehovah? Will You hide Yourself forever?How long will Your wrath burn like fire?
Remember how short my time is—For what vanity You have created all the sons of men!
What man will live and not see death?Will he deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah
Where are Your former acts of lovingkindness, O [1]Lord,Which You [a]swore to David in Your faithfulness?
Remember, O Lord, the reproach of Your servants,How I bear in my bosom the reproach of all the mighty peoples,
With which Your enemies have reproached, O Jehovah,With which they have reproached the footsteps of Your anointed.
[a]Blessed be Jehovah forever.[b]Amen and Amen.Psa 90:Title A Prayer of Moses, the [a]man of God

90.text: O Lord, You have been our [1a]dwelling placeIn all generations.
Before the [a]mountains were brought forth,And before You gave birth to the earth and the world,Indeed from [b]eternity to eternity, You are God.
You return man to [a]dustAnd say, Return, you sons of men.
For a [a]thousand years in Your sightAre like yesterday when it passes byAnd like a watch in the night.
You sweep them away as with a rain flood; they are as in a sleep:In the morning they are like [a]grass that comes up anew.
In the morning it flourishes and comes up anew;In the evening it is cut down, and it dries up.
For we have been consumed by Your anger,And by Your wrath we have been troubled.
You have set our iniquities before You,Our secret sins in the light of Your countenance.
For all our days have passed away in Your overflowing wrath;We bring our years to an end like a sigh.
The [1]days of our years are [a]seventy years,Or, if because of strength, [b]eighty years;But their pride is labor and sorrow,For it is soon [c]gone, and we fly away.
Who knows the power of Your anger,And Your overflowing wrath according to the fear that is due You?
Teach us then to [a]number our daysThat we may gain a heart of wisdom.
Return, O Jehovah! How long?And repent concerning Your servants.
Satisfy us in the [a]morning with Your lovingkindnessThat we may give a ringing shout and rejoice all our days.
Cause us to rejoice according to the days that You have afflicted us,According to the years that we have seen evil.
Let Your work appear to Your servants,And Your splendor, to their children.
And let the [1]favor of the Lord our God be upon us,And establish the work of our hands upon us;Indeed the work of our hands, establish it.

91.text: He who [1]dwells in the [a]secret place of the Most HighWill abide in the [b]shadow of the [2]Almighty.
I say of Jehovah,My [a]refuge and My fortress,My God in whom I trust!
For He will deliver YouFrom the snare of the fowler,From the deadly pestilence.
With His pinions He will cover You,And under His [a]wings You will [b]take refuge;His truth is a shield and a buckler.
You will not be afraid of the terror by night,Or of the arrow that flies by day,
Or of the pestilence that walks in darkness,Or of the destruction that lays waste at noon.
A thousand will fall at Your side,And ten thousand at Your right hand;But it will not come near to You.
You will only look on with Your eyesAnd see the recompense of the wicked.
For [1]You have made Jehovah, who is my [a]refuge,Even the Most High, [1]Your [b]habitation,
No evil will befall You,Nor will any plague come near Your tent.
[a]For He will give His [1b]angels charge concerning YouTo keep You in all Your ways.
They will bear You up in their hands,Lest You dash Your foot against a stone.
You will tread upon the [1]lion and the [1]cobra;You will trample the young lion and the [a]serpent.
Because [1]He has set His love upon Me, I will rescue Him;I will set Him on high, because He has known My name.
He will call upon Me, and I will answer Him.I will be with Him in distress;I will deliver Him and honor Him.
With an [a]extension of His days I will satisfy Him,And I will show Him My salvation.Psa 92:Title A Psalm; a Song for the Sabbath day

92.text: It is good to [1]give thanks to JehovahAnd to sing psalms to Your name, O Most High;
To [a]declare Your lovingkindness in the [b]morningAnd Your faithfulness in the nights,
Upon an instrument of ten strings and upon a harp,Upon the [1]higgaion of the lyre.
For You have made me rejoice, O Jehovah, because of what You have done;Because of the works of Your hands I will shout for joy.
How great are Your works, O Jehovah!So very deep are Your thoughts!
A senseless man does not know them,Nor does a fool understand [1]them.
When the wicked sprout like grass,And all the workers of iniquity blossom,It is so that they may be destroyed forever.
But You are on high forever, O Jehovah.
For behold, Your enemies, O Jehovah;For behold, Your enemies will perish;All the workers of iniquity will be scattered.
But You have exalted my [1]horn like that of a wild ox;I am [2a]anointed with fresh oil.
And my eye has seen its desire for my enemies;My ears have heard their desire for the evildoers who rise up against me.
The righteous man will [1]flourish like the [a]palm tree;He will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
[a]Planted in the house of Jehovah,They will flourish in the [b]courts of our God.
They will still bring forth fruit in old age;They will be [a]full of sap and green;
To declare that Jehovah is upright;He is my [a]rock, and there is [b]no unrighteousness in Him.

93.text: Jehovah [1a]reigns; He is [b]clothed with majesty.Jehovah is clothed, He has girded Himself, with strength.Indeed the world has been [2]established; it will not be moved.
Your [a]throne is established from of old;You are from eternity.
The floods have lifted up, O Jehovah;The floods have lifted up their voice;The floods lift up their roaring.
More than the voices of many waters,Than the mighty breakers of the sea,Jehovah on high is mighty!
Your testimonies are very sure;Holiness befits Your house,O Jehovah, forevermore.

94.text: O God of vengeance, O Jehovah,O God of vengeance, [1]shine forth!
Lift Yourself up, O [a]Judge of the earth;Render the reward upon the proud.
How long, O Jehovah, will the wicked,How long will the wicked exult?
They burst out; they speak arrogantly;All the workers of iniquity boast.
They crush Your people, O Jehovah,And afflict Your inheritance.
They slay the widow and the stranger,And they murder orphans.
And they say, [1]Jehovah does [a]not see,And the God of Jacob does not consider.
Consider, you senseless ones among the people;And you foolish ones, when will you become wise?
He who planted the [a]ear, does He not hear?And He who formed the eye, does He not see?
He who [1]disciplines the nations,He who teaches man knowledge, does He not reprove?
[a]Jehovah knows the thoughts of man,That they are vanity.
[a]Blessed is the manWhom You [b]discipline, O Jehovah,And teach out of Your law;
That You may give him rest from the days of evilUntil the pit is dug for the wicked.
For Jehovah will [a]not abandon His people,Nor will He forsake His inheritance.
For judgment will [1]return unto righteousness,And all the upright in heart will follow it.
Who will rise up for me against the evildoers?Who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?
Unless Jehovah had been my help,My soul would have soon dwelt in silence.
When I said, My foot has slipped,Your lovingkindness, O Jehovah, upheld me.
When my disquieting thoughts within me are many,Your comfortings delight my soul.
Shall the seat of destruction be united with You,Which devises trouble through its statutes?
They band together against the soul of the righteous manAnd condemn innocent blood.
But Jehovah has been my high tower,And my God the [a]rock of my [b]refuge;
And He will return their iniquity upon themAnd destroy them in their own evil;Jehovah our God will destroy them.

95.text: Come, let us sing for joy to Jehovah;Let us make a [a]joyful noise to the [b]rock of our salvation.
Let us [a]come before His presence with thanksgiving;Let us make a joyful noise to Him with psalms.
For Jehovah is a [a]great GodAnd a [1]great [b]King above all gods.
In His hand are the depths of the earth,The heights of the mountains are His also.
The sea is His, and He made it;And His hands formed the [a]dry land.
Come, let us worship and bow down;Let us kneel before Jehovah our [a]Maker.
For He is our God,And we are the people of His [a]pastureAnd the flock of His hand.[b]Today, if you hear His voice,
Do not [a]harden your heart as at [1b]Meribah,As in the day of [2]Massah in the wilderness;
[a]When your fathers [b]tested Me;They tried Me, even though they had seen My work.
For [a]forty years I loathed that [b]generation,And I said, They are a people who go astray in heart;And they do not know My [1c]ways;
Therefore I [a]swore in My anger:[b]They shall by no means enter into My [1]rest!

96.text: Sing to Jehovah a [a]new song;[b]Sing to Jehovah, all the earth.
Sing to Jehovah, bless His name;Proclaim His salvation from day to day.
Declare His glory among the [1]nations,His wondrous deeds among all the [2]peoples.
For Jehovah is [a]great and much to be praised;He is to be feared above all gods.
For all the [a]gods of the peoples are idols,But Jehovah made the heavens.
Majesty and splendor are before Him;Strength and beauty are in His sanctuary.
[a]Ascribe to Jehovah, O families of the peoples,Ascribe to Jehovah, glory and strength;
Ascribe to Jehovah the glory of His name;Bring an offering, and come into His courts.
Worship Jehovah in holy [1]splendor;Tremble before Him, all the earth.
Say among the nations, Jehovah [a]reigns;Indeed the world has been [1]established; it will not be moved;He will [b]judge the peoples with equity.
Let the heavens rejoice and the [a]earth be glad;[b]Let the sea and its fullness roar.
Let the field and all that is in it exult;Then all the trees of the forest will sing for joy
[a]Before Jehovah, for He is coming;For He is coming to judge the earth;He will [b]judge the world with righteousness,And the peoples with His truth.

97.text: Jehovah [1a]reigns! Let the earth be glad;Let the many islands rejoice.
Clouds and [a]deep darkness surround Him;[1b]Righteousness and justice are the foundation of His throne.
A [a]fire goes before HimAnd burns up His adversaries all around.
His [a]lightning illumines the world;The earth sees and trembles.
The mountains [a]melt like wax at the [b]presence of Jehovah,At the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.
The [a]heavens declare His righteousness,And all the peoples see His [b]glory.
Let all who serve a graven image be ashamed,Who make their boast in idols;[a]Worship Him, all you [1]gods.
Zion hears and [a]rejoices,And the daughters of Judah are glad,Because of Your judgments, O Jehovah.
For You, O Jehovah, are [a]most high over all the earth;You are exalted far above all gods.
O you who love Jehovah, hate evil!He preserves the souls of His faithful ones;He [a]delivers them from the hand of the wicked.
Light is sown for the righteous man,And rejoicing for the upright in heart.
Rejoice in Jehovah, O righteous ones;And [1a]praise [2]His holy memorial.Psa 98:Title A Psalm

98.text: Sing to Jehovah a [a]new song,For He has done wonderful things.His [b]right hand and His holy [c]armHave accomplished salvation for Him.
Jehovah has made His [a]salvation known;In the sight of the nationsHe has revealed His [b]righteousness.
He [a]remembered His lovingkindness and His faithfulnessToward the house of Israel;All the ends of the earth have seenThe [b]salvation of our God.
Make a [a]joyful noise to Jehovah, all the earth;Break forth, and sing for joy, and sing psalms.
Sing psalms to Jehovah with the lyre,With the lyre and the sound of melody.
With trumpets and the sound of the hornMake a [a]joyful noise before the [b]King, Jehovah.
[a]Let the sea and its fullness roar,The world and those who inhabit it.
Let the rivers clap their hands;Let the mountains sing for joy together
[a]Before Jehovah, for He is comingTo judge the earth.He will [b]judge the world with righteousnessAnd the peoples with equity.

99.text: Jehovah [1a]reigns; let the peoples tremble.He [b]is enthroned between the cherubim; let the earth shake.
Jehovah is [a]great in [b]Zion,And He is exalted over all the peoples.
Let them praise Your great and awesome name—It is holy—
And the strength of the King, who loves justice.You have established equity;You have executedJustice and righteousness in Jacob.
Exalt Jehovah our God,And worship at His [a]footstool;Holy is He.
[a]Moses and [b]Aaron among His priests,And [c]Samuel among those who [d]called on His name—They called out to Jehovah,And He answered them.
In the [a]pillar of cloud He spoke to them;They kept His testimonies and the statute that He gave them.
O Jehovah our God, You answered them;You were a forgiving God to them,Yet One avenging their deeds.
Exalt Jehovah our God,And worship on His [a]holy mountain;For holy is Jehovah our God.Psa 100:Title A Psalm [*]of thanksgiving

100.text: [1]Make a [a]joyful noise to Jehovah, all the earth.
Serve Jehovah with rejoicing;[a]Come before His presence with joyful singing.
Know that it is Jehovah who is God;It is He who has [a]made us and [1]not we ourselves.We are His people and the [b]sheep of His pasture.
Enter His gates with thanksgiving,His [a]courts with praise;Give thanks to Him; bless His name.
For Jehovah is [a]good; His lovingkindness is forever,And His faithfulness is to all generations.Psa 101:Title [a]Of David. A Psalm

101.text: [1]I will sing of [2a]lovingkindness and justice;To You, O Jehovah, I will sing psalms.
I will give careful heed to the perfect way.When will You come to me?I will walk in the perfectness of my heartWithin my house.
I will not set anything baseBefore my eyes;I hate what those who turn aside do;It will not cling to me.
A perverse heart will depart from me;No evil will I know.
He who secretly slanders his neighbor,Him I will destroy;He who is of a haughty look and a proud heart,Him I will not tolerate.
My eyes are on the faithful of the land,That they may abide with me;He who walks in the perfect way,He will minister to me.
He who practices deceitWill not dwell within my house;He who speaks lies will not be maintainedIn my sight.

Morning by morning I will destroyAll the wicked of the land,In order to cut off from the [a]city of JehovahAll the workers of iniquity.Psa 102:Title A Prayer of an afflicted one, when he is fainting and pours out his complaint before Jehovah

102.text: O Jehovah, [a]hear my [1]prayer;And let my cry come unto You.
Do not hide Your face from meOn the day of my distress;Incline Your ear to me;On the day when I call out, answer me quickly.
For my days are consumed in [a]smoke,And my bones burn like a hearth.
My heart is stricken like grass and dried up;Indeed I have even forgotten to eat my bread.
Because of the voice of my groaningMy bones cling to my flesh.
I am like a [a]pelican of the wilderness;I have become like an owl of the desolate places.
I [a]watch, and I am likeA lone [1b]sparrow on a housetop.
All day long my enemies reproach me;Those who deride me use me as a curse.
For I have eaten ashes like bread,And have mixed my drink with weeping,
Because of Your indignation and Your wrath;For You have taken me up and cast me away.
My days are like a [a]shadow that declines,And I am dried up like [b]grass.
[a]But You, O Jehovah, abide forever;And Your [b]memorial is from generation to generation.
You will arise and have compassion on [1a]Zion,For it is time to be gracious to her; the [b]appointed time has come.
For Your servants take pleasure in her [a]stones,And show favor to her [1]soil.
And the nations will fear the name of Jehovah,And all the kings of the earth, Your glory;
For Jehovah has [1a]built up [b]Zion;He has appeared in His [c]glory.
He has regarded the prayer of the destitute,And has not despised their prayer.
This will be written for a generation to come,And a people to be created will praise [1]Jehovah.
For He has [a]looked down from the height of His sanctuary;From heaven Jehovah beheld the earth,
To hear the groaning of the prisoner,To release those appointed to die;
That the [1a]name of Jehovah may be declared in [b]Zion,And His praise, in Jerusalem,
When the peoples are gathered together,And the [a]kingdoms, to serve Jehovah.
He has weakened my strength in the way;He has shortened my days.
I said, My God,Do not take me away in the middle of my days;Your years are throughout all generations.
[1a]Of old You laid the [b]foundation of the earth,And the heavens are the [c]work of Your hands.
