[a]They will perish, but You [b]endure;Indeed all of them will wear out like a [c]garment;Like clothing You will change them,And they will be changed.
But You are the [a]same,And Your years are without end.
The children of Your servants will continue,And their seed will be established before You.Psa 103:Title [a]Of David

103.text: [1a]Bless Jehovah, O my soul;And all that is within me, bless His holy name.
Bless Jehovah, O my soul;And do not forget all His benefits:
He [a]pardons all your iniquities;He [b]heals all your diseases;
He redeems your life from the pit;He crowns you with lovingkindness and compassions;
He satisfies the [1]prime of your life with good,So that your youth is renewed like the [a]eagle.
Jehovah executes righteous acts,And judgments for all who are oppressed.
He made His [1a]ways known to Moses;His [1b]acts, to the children of Israel.
[a]Jehovah is compassionate and gracious,Long-suffering and abundant in lovingkindness.
[a]He will not always contend with us,Nor will He keep His [b]anger forever.
He has not dealt with us according to our [a]sins,Nor has He recompensed us according to our iniquities.
For as high as the heavens are above the earth,So great is His lovingkindness upon those who fear Him.
As far as the east is from the west,So far has He removed our [a]transgressions from us.
As compassionate as a [a]father is toward his children,So compassionate is Jehovah toward those who fear Him.
For He knows our frame;He remembers that we are [a]dust.
Man—his days are like [a]grass;He flourishes like a field [b]flower:
For the wind passes over it, and it is no more;And its own place knows of it no longer.
But Jehovah’s lovingkindness is from eternityUnto eternity upon those who fear Him,And His righteousness is to the children’s children;
To those who keep His covenantAnd remember His precepts so as to do them.
Jehovah has established His [a]throne in the heavens,And His kingdom rules over all.
Bless Jehovah, you His angels,Who are mighty in strength to perform His word,By obeying the voice of His word.
Bless Jehovah, all you His hosts,You His ministers who execute His will.
Bless Jehovah, all you His works,In all places of His dominion.Bless Jehovah, O my soul.

104.text: [1a]Bless Jehovah, O my soul.O Jehovah my God, You are very great.You are clothed with majesty and splendor,
Wrapped with [a]light as with a garment;You [b]stretch out the heavens like a tent curtain.
He lays the beams of His upper chambers in the [1]waters;He makes the clouds His chariot;He walks upon the wings of the wind.
He makes [a]winds His messengers,Flames of fire His ministers.
He established the [a]earth upon its foundations,So that it cannot be moved forever and ever.
You covered it with the deep as with a garment;The waters stood above the [a]mountains.
At Your [a]rebuke they fled;At the voice of Your thunder they rushed away—
The [a]mountains rose, the valleys sank—To the place that You established for them.
You set a [a]border that they may not pass over,That they may not turn back to cover the earth.
He makes the [a]springs gush forth in the valleys;They run among the mountains;
They give drink to every beast of the field;The wild asses quench their thirst.
The birds of heaven dwell alongside them;They utter their voice among the branches.
He [a]waters the mountains from His upper chambers.The earth is satisfied with the fruit of Your works.
He causes the [a]grass to grow for the cattle,And [b]herbage for man’s use,That he may bring forth food from the earth:
Even [a]wine that cheers man’s heart,[b]Oil that makes his face shine,And bread that sustains man’s [c]heart.
The trees of Jehovah are [a]full of sap,The cedars of Lebanon, which He planted,
Where the birds build their nests;As for the stork, the cypresses are her house.
The high mountains are for the wild goats;The cliffs are a refuge to the rock badgers.
He made the moon to mark [a]seasons;The [b]sun knows the time of its going down.
You make darkness, and it is night,In which all the beasts of the forest creep about.
The young lions roar after their prey,Even to seek their food from God.
The sun rises: they retreatAnd lie down in their dens.
Man goes forth to his workAnd to his [a]labor until evening.
How many are Your works, O Jehovah!In wisdom You have made all of them;The earth is full of Your creatures.
Yonder is the sea, great and extensively wide;There the creeping things are, even without number,The small animals with the great.
There the ships go;Leviathan is there, whom You have formedTo play in it.
They all wait for You,That You may give them [a]food at the proper time.
You give to them; they gather.You open Your hand; they are satisfied with good.
You hide Your face; they are troubled.You take away their breath; they perish,And return to their [a]dust.
You send forth Your [a]Spirit; they are created,And You renew the surface of the ground.
May the glory of Jehovah endure forever!May Jehovah rejoice in His works,
He who looks on the earth, and it trembles;He who touches the mountains, and they [a]smoke!
[a]I will sing to Jehovah while I live;I will sing psalms to my God while I yet have being.
May my musing be sweet to Him;I will rejoice in Jehovah.
May the sinners be consumed from off the earth,And may the wicked be no more.Bless Jehovah, O my soul.[1a]Hallelujah.

105.text: [a]Give thanks to Jehovah; [b]call upon His name;Make known His deeds among the peoples.
Sing to Him; sing psalms to Him;Muse upon all His wondrous deeds.
Glory in His holy name;Let the heart of those who seek Jehovah rejoice.
[a]Seek Jehovah and His strength;[b]Seek His face continually.
Remember His wondrous deeds, which He has done,His signs and the judgments of His mouth,
O seed of Abraham His servant,O children of Jacob, His chosen ones.
He is Jehovah our God;His judgments are in all the earth.
He has remembered His [1]covenant forever,The word that He commanded to a thousand generations,
The covenant that He made with [a]Abraham,And His oath unto [b]Isaac.
And He confirmed it unto [a]Jacob as a statute,Unto Israel as an eternal covenant,
Saying, To you I will [a]give the land of Canaan,The portion of your inheritance;
When they were men few in number,Indeed very [a]few and [b]strangers in [1]the land.
And they went about from nation to nation,From one kingdom to another people.
He [a]allowed no man to oppress them;Indeed He [b]rebuked kings for their sake.
Saying, Do not touch My anointed ones,And do My prophets no harm.
And He called a [a]famine upon the land;He broke the whole staff of bread.
He sent a man [a]before them;Joseph was [b]sold as a servant.
They afflicted his feet with fetters;He himself was [a]placed in irons.
Until the time when his word [a]came to pass,The word of Jehovah tried him.
The king sent men and [a]freed him,Even the ruler of the peoples, and let him go.
He made him lord of his houseAnd [a]ruler over all his possessions,
To imprison his princes at will,And to teach his elders wisdom.
Then Israel came into [a]Egypt,And Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
And He made His people very [a]fruitful,And caused them to be mightier than their adversaries.
He turned their heart to hate His people,To deal [a]craftily with His servants.
He [a]sent Moses His servant,And Aaron, whom He had chosen.
[a]They set His signs among them,And wonders in the land of Ham.
He sent [a]darkness and made it dark;And they did not rebel against His words.
He turned their waters into [a]blood,And killed their fish.
Their land teemed with [a]frogs,Even in the chambers of their kings.
He spoke, and a swarm of [a]flies came;There were [b]gnats within all their borders.
He gave them [a]hail for rain,And flaming fire in their land.
He struck their vines and their fig trees,And He [a]broke the trees of their borders.
He spoke, and the swarming [a]locusts came,And the licking locusts, even without number.
And they ate every herb in their land,And they ate the fruit of their ground.
He [a]struck all the firstborn in their land,The firstfruits of all their vigor.
And He brought them out with silver and [a]gold,And there was none among His tribes who stumbled.
Egypt rejoiced at their [a]going forth,For a dread of them had fallen upon them.
He spread a [a]cloud for a covering,And a fire to give light by night.
They asked, and He brought [a]quails;And He satisfied them with the [b]bread of heaven.
He opened the [a]rock, and water gushed forth;It ran in the dry places like a river.
For He remembered His holy wordAnd Abraham His servant.
And He brought forth His people with gladness,His chosen ones with a ringing shout.
And He gave them the lands of the nations,And they took [a]possession of the labor of the peoples,
So that they might keep His statutesAnd observe His laws.[1a]Hallelujah.

106.text: [1a]Hallelujah.[b]Give thanks to Jehovah, for He is good,For His lovingkindness is forever.
Who can utter the mighty acts of Jehovah,Or declare all His praise?
Blessed are those who maintain justice,Who execute righteousness at all times.
Remember me, O Jehovah, with Your favor toward Your people;Visit me with Your salvation,
That I may see the prosperity of Your chosen ones,That I may rejoice in the joy of Your nation,That I may glory with Your inheritance.
We [a]have sinned with our fathers;We committed iniquity; we have acted wickedly.
Our fathers in EgyptDid not understand Your wonders;They did not remember the multitude of Your [a]acts of lovingkindness,But [b]rebelled by the sea, at the Red Sea.
But He saved them for His name’s sake,That He might make His power known.
Thus He [a]rebuked the Red Sea, and it [b]dried up;And He led them through the depths as through the wilderness.
And He [a]saved them from the hand of him who hated them,And redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
The water then [a]covered their adversaries;Not one of them was left.
Then they [a]believed His words;They [b]sang His praise.
Soon they forgot His works;They did not wait for His counsel,
But [a]lusted exceedingly in the wilderness,And [b]tempted God in the desert.
And He gave them their request,But He sent leanness into their soul.
And they were [a]jealous of Moses in the campAnd of Aaron, the holy one of Jehovah.
The earth opened, and it [a]swallowed up DathanAnd covered the company of Abiram.
And a [a]fire burned among their company;A flame engulfed the wicked.
They made a [a]calf in Horeb,And worshipped a molten image.
Thus they exchanged their [a]gloryFor the image of an ox that eats grass.
They forgot God their Savior,Who had done great things in Egypt,
Wondrous deeds in the land of Ham,Awesome things by the Red Sea.
Therefore He said that He would [a]destroy them,And would have, had not Moses His chosen oneStood in the breach before HimTo turn away His wrath from destroying them.
Then they despised the [a]pleasant land;They did [b]not believe in His word;
Rather they [a]murmured in their tents;They did not listen to the voice of Jehovah.
Therefore He lifted up His hand and swore to themThat He would make them [a]fall in the wilderness,
And that He would make their seed fall among the nations,And that He would [a]disperse them among the lands.
Then they joined themselves to [a]Baal-peor,And ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
Thus they provoked Him to anger by their actions,And the plague broke out among them.
Then [a]Phinehas stood up and intervened,And the plague was stayed;
And it has been accounted to him as [a]righteousnessFrom generation to generation unto eternity.
Then they angered Him at the waters of [a]Meribah,So that it went ill with Moses on account of them;
For they rebelled against His Spirit,And he [a]spoke rashly with his lips.
They did not [a]destroy the peoples,As Jehovah had commanded them;
But they [a]mingled themselves with the nationsAnd learned their practices.
And they [a]served their idols,Which became a snare to them.
They also sacrificed their sonsAnd their daughters to [a]demons,
And poured out innocent blood,The blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idolsof Canaan;And the land was [a]polluted with bloodshed.
Thus they were defiled by their practices,And went about as [a]harlots by their actions.
And the anger of Jehovah was kindled against His people,And He abhorred His inheritance.
And He [a]gave them over into the hand of the nations:And those who hated them ruled over them,
And their enemies [a]oppressed them,And they were subdued under their hand.
Many times He delivered them,But they were rebellious in their counsel,And they were brought low in their iniquity.
Yet He regarded their distressWhen He heard their cry,
And for their sake He remembered His [a]covenant,And repented according to the multitude of His acts of lovingkindness;
And He caused them to find [a]compassionWith all their captors.
[a]Save us, O Jehovah our God;And gather us out of the nations,That we may give thanks to Your holy name,That we may glory in Your praise.
[a]Blessed be Jehovah the God of Israel,From eternity to eternity.And let all the people say, [b]Amen.Hallelujah.

107.text: [a]Give thanks to Jehovah, for He is good,For His lovingkindness is forever.
Let the [1a]redeemed of Jehovah say so,Whom He redeemed from the hand of the adversary,
And [1a]gathered out of the lands,From the east and from the west,From the north and from the south.
They [1]wandered in the [a]wilderness, on the desert way;They found no city of habitation.
Hungry and thirsty,Their soul fainted within them.
Then they cried out to Jehovah in their trouble,And He delivered them from their distresses.
And He led them on a straight wayThat they might come to a city of habitation.
Let them give thanks to Jehovah for His lovingkindnessAnd for His wondrous deeds to the children of men.
For He [a]satisfies the thirsty soul,And the [b]famished soul He fills with good.
Some inhabited [a]darkness and the shadow of death,And were bound in affliction and irons,
Because they rebelled against the words of GodAnd spurned the counsel of the Most High;
Therefore He brought down their heart with labor;They stumbled, and there was no one to help them.
Then they cried out to Jehovah in their trouble,And He saved them from their distresses.
He brought them out of [a]darkness and the shadow of death,And He broke apart their bonds.
Let them give thanks to Jehovah for His lovingkindnessAnd for His wondrous deeds to the children of men.
For He breaks down the doors of bronzeAnd cuts down the bars of iron.
Some were fools, because of their way of transgression;And because of their iniquities, they were afflicted.
Their soul loathed all food,And they drew near to the gates of death.
Then they cried out to Jehovah in their trouble,And He saved them out of their distresses.
He sent forth His word and [a]healed them,And He delivered them from the pits of destruction.
Let them give thanks to Jehovah for His lovingkindnessAnd for His wondrous deeds to the children of men.
And let them offer sacrifices of thanksgivingAnd declare His doings in joyful song.
Those who go down to the sea in ships,Conducting business on the mighty waters,
These see Jehovah’s doingsAnd His wondrous deeds in the deep.
For He commanded and raised up a storm wind,And it lifted up [1]the waves of the sea.
[1]They mounted up to the heavens; they went down to the depths;Their soul melted in the calamity.
They reeled and staggered like a drunken man,And all their skill was swallowed up.
Then they cried out to Jehovah in their trouble,And He brought them out of their distresses.
He made the storm be still,And [1]the waves of the sea were [a]calm;
And they rejoiced that they were quiet,And He brought them to the haven they desired.
Let them give thanks to Jehovah for His lovingkindnessAnd for His wondrous deeds to the children of men.
And let them exalt Him in the congregation of the peopleAnd praise Him in the assembly of the elders.
He turns [a]rivers into a wilderness,And springs of water into thirsty ground;
Fruitful land into a [a]salt flat,Because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
He turns a wilderness into a pool of water,And a dry land into [a]springs of water.
And He makes the hungry dwell there,That they may prepare a city of habitation
And sow fields and plant vineyardsAnd get a fruitful yield.
He also blesses them, and they are multiplied greatly;And He does not let their cattle decrease.
When they are diminished and bowed downThrough oppression, trouble, and sorrow,
He pours contempt on princes,And makes them wander in a pathless waste.
But He raises the needy man high above afflictionAnd makes his families like flocks.
The upright see it and rejoice;And all iniquity stops its mouth.
Who is [a]wise? Then let him observe these thingsAnd consider the lovingkindness of Jehovah.Psa 108:Title A Song; a [a]Psalm of David

108.text: [a]My heart is fixed, O God;I will sing, indeed I will sing psalms,Even with my glory.
Awake, O harp and lyre!I will awaken the dawn.
I will give thanks to You among the peoples, O Jehovah;And I will sing psalms to You among the nations.
For Your lovingkindness is higher than the heavens,And Your [1]faithfulness reaches to the sky.
Be exalted above the heavens, O God;And let Your glory be above all the earth.
[a]That Your beloved ones may be delivered,[1]Save with Your right hand, and answer [2]us.
God has spoken in His [1]holiness: I will exult;I will divide ShechemAnd measure out the Valley of Succoth.
Gilead is Mine; Manasseh is Mine;And Ephraim is the helmet for My head;Judah is My [a]scepter.
Moab is My washbowl;Upon Edom I will cast My sandal;Over Philistia I will shout.
Who will bring me into the fortified city?Who will lead me to Edom?
Will not You, O God, who [a]rejected usAnd who did not go forth, O God, with our armies?
Give us help from the adversary,For salvation by man is vain.
In God we will do valiantly,And it is He who will tread down our adversaries.Psa 109:Title To the choir director. [a]Of David. A Psalm

109.text: [1]O God of my praise, do not be silent;
For the mouth of the wicked man and the mouth of deceit are openedagainst me;They speak to me with a lying tongue.
And with words of hatred they have surrounded meAnd have fought against me without cause.
In return for my love they have become my adversaries,But I am all prayer.
And they have set against me evil in return for goodAnd hatred in return for my love.
Appoint a wicked man over him,And let an adversary stand at his right hand.
When he is judged, let him come forth wicked;And let his prayer be counted as sin.
May his days be few;[a]May another take his office.
May his children be orphans,And his wife a widow.
And may his children wander all about and beg,And may they seek food far from their desolated ruins.
May the creditor seize all that he has,And may strangers plunder the fruit of his labor.
May there be no one to extend him mercy,Nor anyone to be kind to his orphans.
May his posterity be cut off;In the generation following may [1]his name be blotted out.
May his father’s iniquity be remembered before Jehovah,And may his mother’s sin not be blotted out.
May they be before Jehovah continually,So that He may cut off the memory of them from the earth;
Because he did not remember to show lovingkindness,But persecuted the poor and needy and brokenhearted,To put them to death.
Indeed he loved cursing, so it came on him;And he took no delight in blessing, so it was far from him.
And he clothed himself with cursing like his garment,So it came into his inward parts like waterAnd into his bones like oil.
Let it be to him like the cloak that he wraps around himself,And as a belt with which he constantly girds himself.
This is the reward from Jehovah for my adversariesAnd for those who speak evil against my soul.
But You, O Jehovah Lord, deal with me,For Your name’s sake;Because Your lovingkindness is good, deliver me.
For I am poor and needy,And my heart is wounded within me.
I am gone like a shadow that declines;I am shaken off like the locust.
My knees wobble through fasting,And my flesh has become lean, without fatness.
I have also become a [a]reproach to them:When they see me, they [b]shake their heads.
Help me, O Jehovah my God;Save me according to Your lovingkindness;
And they will know that this is Your hand,That it is You, O Jehovah, who have done it.
Let them curse, but You bless;When they arise, they will be put to shame,And Your servant will rejoice.
May my adversaries be clothed with humiliation,And may they wrap themselves with their own shame as with a mantle.
I will give much thanks to Jehovah with my mouth,And in the midst of the multitude I will praise Him;
For He stands at the right hand of the needyTo save him from those who judge his soul.Psa 110:Title [a]Of David. A Psalm

110.text: [1a]Jehovah declares to my Lord,Sit at My [2b]right handUntil I make Your enemiesYour [3]footstool.
Jehovah will send forthThe [a]scepter of Your strength from [1b]Zion:[c]Rule in the midst of Your enemies.
Your people will [1]offer themselves [a]willinglyIn the day of Your warfare,In the [2]splendor of their consecration.Your young men will be to YouLike the [3b]dew from the womb of the dawn.
[a]Jehovah has sworn,And He will [b]not change:[c]You are a [1d]Priest [e]foreverAccording to the [2]order of [3f]Melchizedek.
The [1]Lord is at Your [a]right hand;He will shatter kings in the day of His [b]anger.
He will execute [a]judgment among the nations;He will fill the place with corpses;He will shatter the headOver a [1]great land.
He will drink from the [1]brook by the way;Therefore He will [2]lift up His head.

111.text: [1a]Hallelujah![2]I will give thanks to Jehovah with all my heartIn the council of the upright and [b]in the assembly.
[a]Great are the [1]works of Jehovah,And sought out by all who delight in them.
His work is majesty and splendor,And His righteousness endures forever.
He has caused His wondrous deeds to be remembered;Jehovah is gracious and compassionate.
He gives food to those who fear Him;He remembers His covenant forever.
He has shown His people the power of His worksBy giving them the [a]heritage of the nations.
The works of His hands are truth and justice;All His precepts are sure:
They are maintained forever and ever,They are executed in truth and uprightness.
He has sent [a]redemption to His people;He has commanded His covenant forever;[b]Holy and awesome is His name.
The [a]fear of Jehovah is the beginning of wisdom;All who practice [1]His precepts have a good understanding.His praise endures forever.

112.text: [1a]Hallelujah![2]Blessed is the man who [b]fears Jehovah,Who delights greatly in His commandments.
His seed will be mighty on the earth;The generation of the upright will be blessed.
Wealth and riches are in his house,And his righteousness endures forever.
Light in the darkness arises to the upright;They are gracious and compassionate and righteous.
It is well with the man who is gracious and [a]lends;He will maintain his cause in judgment.
For he will never be moved;The righteous man will be remembered forever.
He will not be afraid of evil tidings;His heart is steadfast, trusting in Jehovah.
His heart is sustained, he is not afraid,Until he sees what should happen to his adversaries.
[a]He has scattered abroad; he has [b]given to the needy;His righteousness endures forever;His horn will be exalted with honor.
The wicked man will see it and be angry;He will gnash his teeth and melt away;The desire of the wicked will perish.

113.text: [1a]Hallelujah!Praise, you servants of Jehovah,Praise the name of Jehovah.
Let the name of Jehovah be [a]blessed,From now and to eternity.
From the [a]rising of the sun to its settingThe name of Jehovah is to be praised.
Jehovah is high above all the nations,And His [a]glory is above the heavens.
[a]Who is like Jehovah our God,Who has set His throne on high,
Who humbles Himself to beholdThe heavens and the earth?
[a]He raises the poor from the dust;From the ash heap He lifts the needy;
That He may seat them with princes,With the princes of His people.
He causes the [a]barren woman to keep houseAs a joyful mother of children.Hallelujah.

114.text: When Israel [a]went forth from Egypt,The house of Jacob from among a people of unintelligible speech,
The [a]sea saw and fled;The [b]Jordan turned back.
The mountains skipped like rams,The hills, like lambs.
What troubles you, O sea, that you flee?O Jordan, that you turn back?
O mountains, that you skip like rams?O hills, like lambs?
Tremble at the [a]presence of the Lord, O earth,At the presence of the God of Jacob,
Who turns the [a]rock into a pool of water,The flint into a fountain of water.

115.text: Not to us, O Jehovah, not to us,But to Your name give [a]glory,For the sake of Your lovingkindness and Your [1]truth.
[a]Why should the nations say,Where then is their God?
But our God is in the heavens;He does whatever He pleases.
[a]Their idols are mere silver and gold,The work of human [b]hands.
They have mouths, but they do [a]not speak;They have eyes, but they do [b]not see.
They have ears, but they do not hear;They have noses, but they do not smell.
They have hands, but they do not feel;They have feet, but they do not walk;They make no sound in their throat.
Those who make them,All who [a]trust in them,Are [1]like them.
O Israel, [a]trust in Jehovah.He is their help and their shield.
O house of Aaron, trust in Jehovah.He is their help and their shield.
O you who fear Jehovah, trust in Jehovah.He is their help and their shield.
Jehovah has been mindful of us; He will bless us;He will bless the house of Israel;He will bless the house of Aaron;
He will bless those who fear Jehovah,Both small and great.
May Jehovah give you increase,Both you and your children.
May you be blessed of Jehovah,[a]Maker of heaven and earth.
The heavens are the heavens of Jehovah,But He has given the [a]earth to the children of men.
The [a]dead do not praise [1]Jehovah,Nor do any that go down into silence.
But we will bless JehovahFrom now and to eternity.[1a]Hallelujah.

116.text: I love Jehovah because He hearsMy voice, my supplications,
Because He inclines His ear to me;Therefore I will call upon Him all my days.
The bonds of death encompassed me,And the distresses of Sheol fell upon me;I fell upon trouble and sorrow.
But I [a]called upon the name of Jehovah.O Jehovah, I pray, deliver my soul.
Jehovah is gracious and righteous,Indeed our God is compassionate.
Jehovah preserves the simple:I was brought low, and He saved me.
Return to your rest, O my soul;For Jehovah has dealt bountifully with you.
[a]For You have delivered my soul from death,My eyes from tears,My feet from stumbling.
I will walk before JehovahIn the [1]land of the living.
[a]I believed, [1]therefore I spoke;I was afflicted greatly.
I said in my haste,[a]All men are liars.
What shall I [a]return to JehovahFor all His benefits toward me?
I will take up the [1a]cup of salvationAnd [1b]call upon the name of Jehovah.
I will pay my vows to Jehovah,Oh, in the presence of all His people.
[a]Precious in the sight of JehovahIs the [b]death of His faithful ones.
O Jehovah!Surely I am Your [a]servant;I am Your servant, the son of Your female servant;You have loosed my bonds.
To You I will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving,And I will [a]call upon the name of Jehovah.
I will pay my vows to Jehovah,Oh, in the presence of all His people,
In the [a]courts of the house of Jehovah,In the midst of you, O Jerusalem.[b]Hallelujah.

117.text: [a]Praise Jehovah, all you nations;Laud Him, all you peoples.
For His lovingkindness is great toward us,And the faithfulness of Jehovah is forever.[1a]Hallelujah.

118.text: Give [1]thanks to Jehovah, for He is good,For His lovingkindness is forever.
Let Israel say,His lovingkindness is forever.
Let the house of Aaron say,His lovingkindness is forever.
Let those who fear Jehovah say,His lovingkindness is forever.
Out of my distress I [a]called upon [1]Jehovah;[1]Jehovah answered me and set me in a place [b]broad and free.
Jehovah is for me; [a]I will not be afraid.What can man do to me?
Jehovah is for me as my Helper,And I will see what should happen to those who hate me.
It is better to [a]take refuge in JehovahThan to trust in man.
It is better to take refuge in JehovahThan to trust in princes.
All nations surrounded me;In the name of Jehovah I indeed cut them down!
They surrounded me; yes, they surrounded me;In the name of Jehovah I indeed cut them down!
They surrounded me like bees;They were extinguished like a fire of thorns;In the name of Jehovah I indeed cut them down!
[1]I was pushed hard that I might fall;But Jehovah helped me.
[a]Jehovah is my strength and my song,And He has become my [1]salvation.
The voice of rejoicing and salvationIs in the tents of the righteous.The [a]right hand of Jehovah does valiantly;
The right hand of Jehovah is exalted;The right hand of Jehovah does valiantly.
I will not die, but live,And declare the works of Jehovah.
Jehovah has severely chastened me,But He has not given me over to [a]death.
Open to me the [a]gates of righteousness:I will enter in through them; I will give thanks to Jehovah.
This is the gate of Jehovah;The righteous shall [a]enter in through it.
I will give thanks to You, for You have answered meAnd have become my salvation.
[a]The [1]stone which the [1]builders rejectedHas become the [2]head of the [b]corner.
This is from Jehovah;It is wonderful in our sight.
This is the [1a]day that Jehovah has made;Let us exult and rejoice in it.
O Jehovah, [1]do [a]save, we pray!O Jehovah, do send [2]prosperity, we pray!
[a]Blessed is He who [1]comes in the name of Jehovah;We bless you from the house of Jehovah.
Jehovah is God,And He has given us light;Bind the sacrifice of the feast with cordsTo the [a]horns of the altar.
You are my God, and I give thanks to You.You are my God; I will exalt You.
Give thanks to Jehovah, for He is good,For His lovingkindness is forever.Psa 119:Title [*]a (Aleph)

119.text: Blessed are those whose way is perfect,Who [a]walk in the [1]law of Jehovah.
Blessed are those who keep His [1]testimonies,Who [2a]seek Him with all their heart.
Surely they do no wrong;They walk in His ways.
You have commanded that Your preceptsBe diligently kept.
Oh that my ways may be establishedSo as to keep Your [1]statutes!
Then I will not be put to shame,When I regard all Your commandments.
I will give thanks to You with uprightness of heartWhen I learn Your righteous ordinances.
I will keep Your statutes;Do not utterly forsake me.b (Beth)
With what should a young man keep his way pure?By guarding it according to Your [1]word.
With all my heart I have sought You;Do not let me wander from Your commandments.
In my [a]heart I have treasured up Your wordThat I might not sin against You.
Blessed are You, O Jehovah;Teach me Your statutes.
With my lips I have declaredAll the ordinances of Your mouth.
I have rejoiced in the way of Your testimoniesAs much as in all riches.
I will [1]muse upon Your preceptsAnd regard Your ways.
I will take delight in Your statutes;I will not forget Your word.g (Gimel)
Deal bountifully with Your servant that I may liveAnd keep Your word.
[a]Open my eyes that I may beholdWondrous things out of Your law.
I am a [a]sojourner in the earth;Do not hide Your commandments from me.
My soul is crushed with longingFor Your ordinances at all times.
You have rebuked the proud, the cursed,Who wander from Your commandments.
Roll away from me reproach and contempt,For I have kept Your testimonies.
Princes as well have sat and spoken against me,But Your servant [a]mused upon Your statutes.
Also Your testimonies are my delight;They are my counselors.d (Daleth)
My soul clings to the dust;[a]Enliven me according to Your word.
I have recounted my ways, and You have answered me;Teach me Your statutes.
Cause me to understand the way of Your precepts,And I will muse upon Your wondrous deeds.
My soul melts because of grief;Strengthen me according to Your word.
Remove from me the way of falsehood,And graciously grant me Your law.
I have chosen the way of faithfulness;Your ordinances I have set before me.
I cling to Your testimonies;O Jehovah, do not put me to shame.
I run the way of Your commandments,For You [a]enlarge my heart.h (He)
Teach me, O Jehovah, the way of Your statutes;And I will keep it to the end.
Give me understanding, and I will observe Your law;Indeed I will keep it with all my heart.
Cause me to go on the path of Your commandments,For in it I delight.
Incline my heart to Your testimoniesAnd not to unjust gain.
Turn my eyes from beholding vanity,And enliven me in Your ways.
Confirm to Your servant Your word,Which is for fearing You.
Turn away my reproach, which I fear;For Your ordinances are good.
Behold, I have longed after Your precepts;Enliven me in Your righteousness.w (Vav)
May Your lovingkindness also come to me, O Jehovah,Your salvation according to Your word.
Thus I will have an answer for him who reproaches me,For I trust in Your word.
And do not take the word of truth utterly from my mouth,For I have hoped in Your ordinances.
Thus I will keep Your law continually,Forever and ever.
And I will walk at liberty,For I have sought Your precepts.
I will also speak of Your testimonies before [a]kingsAnd not be ashamed.
And I will take delight in Your commandments,Which I love.
And I will [1]lift up my hand to Your commandments, which I love;And I will [a]muse upon Your statutes.z (Zayin)
Remember the word to Your servantIn which You have made me hope.
This is my comfort in my affliction,For Your word has [1a]enlivened me.
The proud have derided me greatly;I have not swerved from Your law.
I have remembered Your ordinances from long ago,O Jehovah, and have comforted myself.
Raging indignation seizes me because of the wicked,Who forsake Your law.
Your statutes have become my songs of praiseIn the house of my pilgrimage.
I have remembered Your nameIn the night, O Jehovah,And have kept Your law.
This has been mine,That I have kept Your precepts.j (Heth)
Jehovah is my [a]portion;I have promised to keep Your words.
I entreated Your [1]favor with my whole heart;Be gracious to me according to Your word.
I considered my waysAnd turned my feet toward Your testimonies.
I hurried and did not delayTo keep Your commandments.
The cords of the wicked surround me,But I have not forgotten Your law.
At [a]midnight I rise up to give thanks to YouBecause of Your righteous ordinances.
I am a companion to all who fear YouAnd to those who keep Your precepts.
Your lovingkindness, O Jehovah, [a]fills the earth;Teach me Your statutes.f (Teth)
You have dealt well with Your servant,O Jehovah, according to Your word.
Teach me proper discernment and knowledge,For I believe in Your commandments.
Before I was afflicted, I went astray;But now I keep Your word.
You are [a]good and One who does good;Teach me Your statutes.
The proud have plastered lies against me;With my whole heart I will keep Your precepts.
Their heart is heavy as with fat;But I delight in Your law.
It is good for me that I have been afflicted,That I might learn Your statutes.
The law of Your mouth is better to meThan thousands of pieces of [a]gold and silver.y (Yodh)
Your hands have [a]made me and fashioned me;Give me understanding that I may learn Your commandments.
Those who fear You will see me and rejoice,For I have hoped in Your word.
I know, O Jehovah, that Your judgments are righteousAnd that in faithfulness You afflicted me.
Let Your lovingkindness, I beseech You, be my comfort,According to Your word to Your servant.
Let Your compassions come to me, and I will live;For Your law is my delight.
Let the proud be put to shame, for they have subverted my cause with a lie;But I will muse upon Your precepts.
Let those who fear You turn to me,And they will know Your testimonies.
May my heart be perfect in Your statutes,That I may not be put to shame.k (Kaph)
My soul faints while longing for Your salvation;I hope in Your word.
My eyes faint while longing for Your word,As I say, When will You comfort me?
For I have become like a wineskin in the smoke;But I do not forget Your statutes.
How many are the days of Your servant?When will You execute justice upon my persecutors?
The proud have dug pits for me,Those who are not according to Your law.
All Your commandments are faithful;They persecute me falsely; help me.
They have almost consumed me upon the earth;But I have not forsaken Your precepts.
[a]Enliven me according to Your lovingkindness,And I will keep the testimony of Your mouth.l (Lamedh)
Forever, O Jehovah,Does Your [a]word stand firm in heaven.
Your faithfulness is from generation to generation;You established the earth, and it abides.
They abide to this day according to Your ordinances,For all things are Your servants.
Unless Your law had been my delight,I would have perished in my affliction.
I will never forget Your precepts,For by them You have enlivened me.
I [a]am Yours; save me,For I have sought Your precepts.
The wicked have waited for me in order to destroy me,But I consider Your testimonies.
I have seen an end to all perfection,But Your commandment is all-expansive.m (Mem)
Oh, how I love Your law!All day long it is my musing.
Your commandments make me [a]wiser than my enemies,For they are always with me.
I have more insight than all my teachers,For Your testimonies are my musing.
I understand more than the aged,For I have kept Your precepts.
I have withheld my feet from every evil way,That I might keep Your word.
I have not turned aside from Your ordinances,For You have taught me.
How sweet are Your words to my taste![a]Sweeter than honey to my mouth!
Through Your precepts I gain understanding;Therefore I hate every false way.n (Nun)
Your word is a [a]lamp to my feetAnd a [b]light to my path.
I have sworn and will confirm it,That I will keep Your righteous ordinances.
I have been greatly afflicted;O Jehovah, [a]enliven me according to Your word.
Accept, I pray, the freewill offerings of my mouth, O Jehovah;And teach me Your ordinances.
My soul is continually in my hand,But I do not forget Your law.
The wicked have laid a trap for me,But I do not stray from Your precepts.
Your testimonies are my heritage forever,For they are the gladness of my heart.
I have inclined my heart to perform Your statutesForever, to the end.s (Samekh)
I hate those who are double-minded,But I love Your law.
You are my hiding place and my shield;In Your word do I hope.
Depart from me, you evildoers,That I may keep the commandments of my God.
Uphold me according to Your word that I may live,And do not let me be ashamed of my hope.
Hold me up that I may be safe,And I will regard Your statutes continually.
You spurn all those who stray from Your statutes,For their deceitfulness is vain.
You do away with all the wicked ones of the earth like dross;Therefore I love Your testimonies.
My flesh trembles for fear of You,And I am afraid of Your judgments.u (Ayin)
I have performed justice and righteousness;Do not leave me to my oppressors.
Guarantee Your servant’s well-being;Do not let the proud oppress me.
My eyes faint while longing for Your salvationAnd for Your righteous word.
Deal with Your servant according to Your lovingkindness,And teach me Your statutes.
I am Your servant; give me understanding,That I may know Your testimonies.
It is time for Jehovah to act,For they have broken Your law.
Therefore I love Your commandmentsMore than [a]gold, indeed, more than fine gold;
Therefore I esteem all Your precepts concerning all things to be right,And I hate every false way.p (Pe)
Your testimonies are wonderful;Therefore my soul keeps them.
The [1]opening of Your words gives [a]light,Imparting understanding to the simple.
I opened my mouth wide and panted,For I longed for Your commandments.
Turn to me, and be gracious to me,As is Your custom with those who love Your name.
Establish my footsteps in Your word,And do not let any iniquity have dominion over me.
Redeem me from man’s oppression,That I may keep Your precepts.
Cause Your [a]face to shine on Your servant,And teach me Your statutes.
My eyes [a]shed streams of water,Because [1]men do not keep Your law.x (Tsadhe)
You are righteous, O Jehovah;And Your judgments are upright.
You have commanded Your testimonies in righteousnessAnd great faithfulness.
My [a]zeal has consumed me,For my adversaries have forgotten Your words.
Your word is very [a]pure,And Your servant loves it.
I am small and despised,But I do not forget Your precepts.
Your righteousness is an eternal righteousness,And Your law is truth.
Distress and anguish have come upon me,But Your commandments are my delight.
Your testimonies are righteous forever;Give me understanding, and I will live.q (Qoph)
I called with my whole heart; answer me, O Jehovah.I will keep Your statutes.
I called upon You; save me,And I will observe Your testimonies.
I anticipated the [a]dawn and cried out;I hoped in Your words.
My eyes anticipated the night watches,That I might [a]muse upon Your word.
Hear my voice according to Your lovingkindness;O Jehovah, according to Your ordinances enliven me.
Those who persecute me with evil intent draw near;They are far from Your law.
You are near, O Jehovah;And all Your commandments are truth.
Of old I have known from Your testimoniesThat You have founded them forever.r (Resh)
Look at my affliction, and deliver me;For I have not forgotten Your law.
Plead my case, and redeem me;[a]Enliven me according to Your word.
Salvation is far from the wicked,For they do not seek Your statutes.
Great are Your compassions, O Jehovah;Enliven me according to Your ordinances.
Many are my persecutors and my adversaries,But I have not swerved from Your testimonies.
I saw the treacherous and loathed them,Because they did not keep Your word.
See how I have loved Your precepts;O Jehovah, [a]enliven me according to Your lovingkindness.
The sum of Your [a]word is truth,And all Your righteous ordinances are forever.? (Shin)
Princes have persecuted me without cause,But my heart stands in awe of Your words.
I rejoice at Your word,Like one who finds great spoil.
I hate and abhor falsehood,But I love Your law.
[a]Seven times a day I praise YouFor Your righteous ordinances.
There is great peace for those who love Your law,And there is no occasion of stumbling for them.
I have hoped for Your salvation, O Jehovah;And I have done Your commandments.
My soul has kept Your testimonies,And I love them very much.
I have kept Your precepts and Your testimonies,For all my ways are before You.t (Tav)
Let my cry come before You, O Jehovah;Give me understanding according to Your word.
Let my supplication come before You;Deliver me according to Your word.
Let my lips pour forth praise,For You teach me Your statutes.
Let my tongue sing of Your word,For all Your commandments are righteousness.
Let Your hand be ready to help me,For I have chosen Your precepts.
I have longed for Your salvation, O Jehovah,And Your law is my delight.
Let my soul live, and it will praise You;And let Your ordinances help me.
I have gone astray like a [a]lost sheep; seek Your servant,For I have not forgotten Your commandments.Psa 120:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

120.text: [1]In my [2]distress I called out to Jehovah,And He answered me.
O Jehovah, deliver my soul from lying lips,From a [a]tongue of deceit.
What should be given to you,And what further shall be done to you,O tongue of deceit?
The sharp arrows of a mighty man,With the burning coals of the broom tree!
Woe is me, for I sojourn in [1]Meshech,I dwell among the tents of [1]Kedar.
Long has my soul had its dwellingWith him who hates peace.
I am for peace; but when I speak,They are for war.Psa 121:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

121.text: [1]I will lift up my eyes to the [2a]hills.From where will my help come?
My [a]help comes from Jehovah,Who made heaven and earth.
He will not let your foot slip;He who keeps you will not slumber.
Behold, Israel’s KeeperWill neither slumber nor sleep.
Jehovah is your Keeper;Jehovah is your [a]shade at your right hand.
The [a]sun will not strike you by day,Nor the moon by night.
Jehovah will keep you from all evil;He will keep your soul.
Jehovah will keep your going out and your coming inFrom now and to eternity.Psa 122:Title A [a]Song of Ascents. [b]Of David

122.text: I rejoiced when they said to me,Let us go to the [1a]house of Jehovah.
Our feet [1]are standingWithin your [a]gates, O Jerusalem.
Jerusalem has been [a]built as a cityThat has been [b]compacted together;
To which the tribes [1a]go up,The tribes of [2]Jehovah—A testimony for Israel—To give thanks to the name of Jehovah.
For there thrones are set for judgment,Thrones of the house of David.
Pray for the [a]peace of [b]Jerusalem.Those who love you will prosper.
Peace be within your [a]bulwarks,And prosperity within your citadels.
For my brothers’ and companions’ sake I will now say,Peace be within you.
For the sake of the [a]house of Jehovah our GodI will seek your good.Psa 123:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

123.text: To You do I lift up my [a]eyes,You who sit enthroned in heaven.
Behold, as the eyes of the servantsLook to the hand of their masters,As the eyes of a servant girlLook to the hand of her mistress,So our eyes look to Jehovah our God,Until He is [1]gracious to us.
Be gracious to us, O Jehovah, be gracious to us;For we are greatly filled with contempt.
Our soul is greatly filledWith the scorn of those who are at ease,With the contempt of the proud.Psa 124:Title A [a]Song of Ascents. [b]Of David

124.text: [1]If it had not been Jehovah who was on our side,Let Israel say,
If it had not been Jehovah who was on our sideWhen men rose up against us;
Then they would have swallowed us up aliveWhen their anger burned against us;
Then the waters would have washed us away;The stream would have passed over our soul;
Then the [1]proud watersWould have passed over our soul.
Blessed be Jehovah, who has not given usAs prey to their teeth.
Our soul has escaped like a bird,Out of the snare of the fowlers;The snare is broken,And we have escaped.
Our [a]help is in the name of Jehovah,Who made heaven and earth.Psa 125:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

125.text: Those who [a]trust in Jehovah are like Mount [b]Zion,Which cannot be moved but abides forever.
Jerusalem—mountains surround her;And Jehovah [1a]surrounds His peopleFrom now and to eternity.
For the scepter of wickedness will not restUpon the allotment of the righteous,That the righteous would not put forthTheir hands to iniquity.
Do good, O Jehovah, to the [1]goodAnd to those upright in their hearts.
But as for those who turn aside to their crooked ways,Jehovah will lead them off with the workers of iniquity.[a]Peace be upon Israel.Psa 126:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

126.text: When Jehovah [a]turned again the [1]captivity of Zion,We were like those who dream.
At that time our mouth was filled with laughterAnd our tongue with a ringing shout.At that time they said among the nations,Jehovah has done [1a]great things for them.
Jehovah has done great things for us;We are joyful.
Turn [1]again, O Jehovah, our captivityLike the streams in the south.
Those who sow in [a]tearsWill [b]reap with a ringing shout.
He who goes forth and weeps,Bearing [1]seed for scattering,Will no doubt come in with a ringing shout,Bearing his sheaves with him.Psa 127:Title A [a]Song of Ascents. [b]Of Solomon

127.text: [1]Unless Jehovah [a]builds the [2]house,Those who build it [b]labor in vain.Unless Jehovah [c]keeps the [2]city,The guard watches in vain.
It is [1]vain for you to rise up early,To stay up late,To eat the bread of toil;All the same, He gives to His beloved while they sleep.
Behold, [1a]children are the heritage of Jehovah,The fruit of the womb a reward.
Like arrows in the hand of a mighty man,So are the children of one’s youth.
Blessed is the manWho fills his quiver with them.[1]He will not be put to shameWhen [1]he speaks with enemies in the gate.Psa 128:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

128.text: [1]Blessed is everyone who fears Jehovah,Who walks in His ways.
You will indeed eat the labor of your hands;You will be blessed, and it will go well with you.
Your wife will be like a [a]fruitful vineIn the innermost parts of your house.Your children will be like [b]olive shootsRound about your table.
Thus will the man be blessedWho fears Jehovah.
Jehovah [1]bless you [2]from [a]Zion;And may you see the [1]prosperity of JerusalemAll the days of your life.
May you also see the children of your children.[a]Peace be upon Israel.Psa 129:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

129.text: Many times have they afflicted me since my youth,Let Israel say,
Many times have they afflicted me since my youth,Yet they did not prevail against me.
The plowers plowed on my back;They made their furrows long.
Jehovah is righteous;He has cut the cords of the wicked.
May all who [1]hate ZionBe put to shame and turned back.
May they be like grass on the housetops,Which withers before it grows up,
With which the reaper does not fill his hand,Or he who binds sheaves, his bosom.
Nor may those who pass by say,The blessing of Jehovah be upon you!We bless you in the name of Jehovah.Psa 130:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

130.text: Out of the depths I have [a]called upon You, O Jehovah.
O Lord, hear my voice;Let Your ears be attentiveTo the voice of my supplications.
If You, O [1]Jehovah, marked iniquities,Who, O Lord, would stand?
But with You there is [1a]forgiveness,That You would be [2]feared.
I wait for Jehovah; my soul waits,And in His word I hope.
My soul waits for the LordMore than watchmen for the morning,Yes, watchmen for the morning.
Hope in Jehovah, O Israel;For with Jehovah there is lovingkindness,And with Him abundantly there is redemption;
And it is He who will redeem Israel[a]From all its iniquities.Psa 131:Title A [a]Song of Ascents. [b]Of David

131.text: O Jehovah, my [1]heart is not proud,Nor are my eyes haughty;Nor do I go about in things too greatOr too wondrous for me.
Surely I have calmedAnd quieted my soul,Like a [1]weaned [a]child with its mother;Like a weaned child is my soul within me.
Hope in Jehovah, O Israel,From now and to eternity.Psa 132:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

132.text: Remember, O Jehovah, for [1]David’s sakeAll his [2]afflictions;
How [a]he swore to JehovahAnd vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
I shall not go into the tent of my house;I shall not go up onto the couch of my bed;
I shall not give sleep to my eyes,Slumber to my eyelids;
Until I find a place for Jehovah,A [a]tabernacle for the Mighty One of Jacob.
Behold, we heard of it in [a]Ephrathah;We found it in the [b]fields of [1]Jaar.
We will go into His [a]tabernacle;We will worship at His [b]footstool.
[a]Arise, O Jehovah, unto Your resting place,You and the Ark of Your strength.
Let Your priests be clothed with [a]righteousness,And let Your faithful ones give a ringing shout.
For the sake of David Your servant,Do not turn away the face of Your Anointed One.
Jehovah has sworn unto David in truth;He will not turn from it:Of the [1a]fruit of your body I will set One upon your throne.
[a]If your children keep My covenantAnd My testimonies that I will teach them,Their children also will sitUpon your throne forever.
For Jehovah has chosen [1a]Zion;He has desired it for His habitation.
[1]This is My [2a]resting place forever;Here will I dwell, for I have desired it.
I will abundantly bless its provision;I will satisfy its poor with bread.
And its priests I will [a]clothe with salvation,And its faithful ones will shout with a ringing shout.
There I will cause a [1a]horn of David to shoot forth;I have prepared a [b]lamp for My anointed one.
I will clothe his enemies with shame,But on him his crown will shine.Psa 133:Title A [a]Song of Ascents. [b]Of David

133.text: [1]Behold, [2]how good and [2]how pleasant it isFor brothers to dwell in [3]unity!
It is like the fine [1a]oil upon the headThat [2]ran down upon the beard,Upon [b]Aaron’s beard,That ran down upon the [c]hem of his garments;
Like the [1a]dew of [b]HermonThat came down upon the [2]mountains of [c]Zion.For there Jehovah commanded the [d]blessing:[3e]Life forever.Psa 134:Title A [a]Song of Ascents

134.text: [1]Bless Jehovah now,All you [a]servants of JehovahWho stand by night in the house of Jehovah.
[a]Lift up your hands in the sanctuary,And bless Jehovah.
May Jehovah, who made heaven and earth,[a]Bless you [1]from [b]Zion.

135.text: [1a]Hallelujah! Praise the name of Jehovah;Praise Him, you [2b]servants of Jehovah,
Who [a]stand in the house of Jehovah,In the courts of the house of our God.
Praise [1]Jehovah, for Jehovah is [a]good;Sing psalms to His name, for it is pleasant.
For Jehovah has [a]chosen Jacob for Himself,Israel for His [b]personal treasure.
For I know that Jehovah is [a]greatAnd that our Lord is above all gods.
Whatever Jehovah pleases He doesIn heaven and on earth,In the seas and all the depths.
[a]He causes the vapors to ascend from the ends of the earth;He makes lightning for the rain;He brings forth wind from His treasuries.
He [a]struck the firstborn of Egypt,Both of man and of beast.
He sent [a]signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt,Upon Pharaoh and all his servants.
He struck many nationsAnd [a]slew mighty kings:
[a]Sihon the king of the AmoritesAnd Og the king of BashanAnd all the kingdoms of Canaan.
And He gave their land as an [a]inheritance,An inheritance to Israel His people.
O Jehovah, Your name is forever;O Jehovah, Your memorial is from generation to generation.
[a]For Jehovah will [1]execute judgment for His people,And He will have compassion concerning His servants.
[a]The [b]idols of the nations are mere silver and gold,The work of human [c]hands.
They have mouths, but they do not speak;They have eyes, but they do not see.
They have ears, but they do not give ear;And there is no breath in their mouths.
Those who make them,All who trust in them,Will be like them.
O house of [a]Israel, [1]bless Jehovah.O house of Aaron, bless Jehovah.
O house of Levi, bless Jehovah.O you who fear Jehovah, bless Jehovah.
Blessed be Jehovah from [a]Zion,Who [b]dwells in Jerusalem.Hallelujah.

136.text: [1]Give thanks to Jehovah, for He is good;For His lovingkindness is forever.
Give thanks to the God of gods,For His lovingkindness is forever.
Give thanks to the [a]Lord of lords,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who alone does great wonders,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who by [a]understanding [b]made the heavens,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who spread forth the [a]earth over the waters,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who made the [a]great lights,For His lovingkindness is forever;
The sun for ruling the day,For His lovingkindness is forever;
The moon and the stars for ruling the night,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who [a]struck Egypt in their firstborn,For His lovingkindness is forever;
And [a]brought forth Israel from their midst,For His lovingkindness is forever;
With a mighty [a]hand and an outstretched arm,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who [a]divided the Red Sea apart,For His lovingkindness is forever;
And brought Israel over through the midst of it,For His lovingkindness is forever;
And shook off Pharaoh and his force into the [a]Red Sea,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who marched His people through the [a]wilderness,For His lovingkindness is forever;
To Him who [a]struck great kings,For His lovingkindness is forever;
And slew majestic kings,For His lovingkindness is forever;
Sihon the king of the Amorites,For His lovingkindness is forever;
And Og the king of Bashan,For His lovingkindness is forever;
And gave their land as an inheritance,For His lovingkindness is forever;
An inheritance to Israel His servant,For His lovingkindness is forever;
Who remembered us in our low estate,For His lovingkindness is forever;
And rescued us from our adversaries,For His lovingkindness is forever;
Who gives [a]food to all flesh,For His lovingkindness is forever.
Give thanks to the [a]God of heaven,For His lovingkindness is forever.

137.text: By the [a]rivers of Babylon,There we sat down; indeed, we [b]weptWhen we [1]remembered [c]Zion.
On the willows in the midst of itWe hung up our lyres,
For there our captors required of us songs,And those who tormented us required of us mirth, saying,Sing for usOne of the [a]songs of Zion.
How can we sing the song of JehovahIn a foreign land?
If I [a]forget you, O Jerusalem,Let my right hand forget its skill.
Let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth,If I do not remember you,If I do not prefer [a]JerusalemAbove my chief joy.
Remember, O Jehovah,[1]Against the children of [a]Edom,The day of Jerusalem,Who said, Lay it bare! Lay it bare,Down to its foundation!
O daughter of Babylon, who are to be devastated,Happy will he be who [a]repays youThe recompense of what you have rendered to us.
Happy will he be who seizes your little onesAnd dashes them against the rock.Psa 138:Title [a]Of David

138.text: I will [1]give You thanks with all my heart;Before the gods I will sing psalms to You.
I will bow toward Your holy [a]templeAnd give thanks to Your nameFor Your lovingkindness and Your truth,For You have magnified Your word above all Your name.
On the day that I called out, You answered me;You encouraged me with strength in my soul.
All the kings of the earth will give thanks to You, O Jehovah;For they have heard the words of Your mouth.
And they will sing of the ways of Jehovah,For great is the glory of Jehovah.
For though Jehovah is high, He [a]regards the lowly;And the [b]haughty He knows from afar.
Though I [a]walk in the midst of trouble,You will preserve me;Against the wrath of my enemies You will [b]stretch out Your hand,And Your right hand will save me.
Jehovah will complete that which concerns me.O Jehovah, Your lovingkindness is forever;Do not forsake the works of Your own hands.Psa 139:Title To the choir director. [a]Of David. A Psalm

139.text: O Jehovah, You have [1a]searched me and [b]known me.
You know when I [a]sit down and when I rise up;You understand my thoughts from afar.
You thoroughly [a]search my path and my lying down,And You are acquainted with all my ways.
For a word is not yet on my tongue,And already You, O Jehovah, know it completely.
You have closed in on me behind and beforeAnd have laid Your hand on me.
Oh, knowledge too wonderful for me!It is so high; I cannot attain to it.
Where shall I go, away from Your Spirit,And where shall I flee [a]from Your presence?
If I ascend into [a]heaven, You are there;If I make my bed in Sheol, there You are.
If I take the wings of the dawnAnd settle at the limits of the sea,
There also Your hand will [a]lead me,And Your right hand will take hold of me.
And if I say, Surely darkness will cover me,And the light around me will be night;
Even the [a]darkness is not dark to You,And night shines like day;The darkness is like the light.
For it was You who [a]formed my inward parts;You wove me together in my mother’s womb.
I will praise You, for I am awesomely and wonderfully made;Your [a]works are wonderful,And my soul knows it well.
My frame was not hidden from YouWhen I was made in secret,[a]Skillfully fashioned in the depths of the earth.
Your eyes saw my unformed substance;And in Your book all of them were written:The days that were ordained for me,When not one of them was yet.
And how precious are Your [a]thoughts to me, O God!How great is the sum of them!
If I should count them, they would be more than the sand.When I awake, I am still with You.
Oh that You, O God, would [1]slay the wicked—Depart from me therefore, you men of bloodshed—
Those who speak of You with evil intent,Who as Your enemies take Your name in vain.
Do I not hate those who hate You, O Jehovah?And do I not loathe those who rise up against You?
I hate them with a perfect hatred;They have become my own enemies.
[a]Search me, O God, and know my heart;Try me, and know my anxious thoughts;
And see if there is some [1]harmful way in me,And lead me on the eternal way.Psa 140:Title To the choir director. A [a]Psalm of David

140.text: [1]Deliver me, O Jehovah, from evil men;From violent men preserve me;
Who devise evil schemes in their heartsAnd continually stir up wars.
They have sharpened their tongues like that of a serpent;The [a]poison of adders is under their lips. Selah
Keep me, O Jehovah, from the hands of the wicked;From violent men preserve me;Who devise schemes to thrust me down.
The proud have hidden a trap for me and cords;They have spread a net by the wayside;They have set snares for me. Selah
I said to Jehovah, You are my God;Give ear, O Jehovah, to the voice of my supplications.
O Jehovah Lord, strength of my salvation,You have covered my head in the day of battle.
O Jehovah, do not grant the desires of the wicked man;Do not let his evil scheme advance,Lest he be exalted. Selah
As for the head of those who surround me,May the mischief of their own lips cover them.
May burning coals drop down upon them;May they be thrown down into the fire,Into deep pits, never to rise again.
Let no slanderer be established in the land;Let evil hunt down the violent man speedily.
I know that Jehovah will maintain the cause of the afflictedAnd justice for the needy.
Surely the righteous will give thanks to Your name;The upright will dwell in Your presence.Psa 141:Title A [a]Psalm of David

141.text: O Jehovah, I have called upon You; hasten to me.Give ear to my voice when I call out to You.
Let my [a]prayer be set before You as incense,The lifting up of my hands as the evening offering.
Set a watch, O Jehovah, before my mouth;Keep the door of my lips.
Do not let my heart turn to anything evil,To practice deeds of wickednessWith men who commit iniquity;And let me not eat of their delicacies.
Let the righteous strike me in kindness and reprove me;Let not my head refuse [a]oil upon the head,For still my prayer is against their evil deeds.
Their judges are thrown down by the sides of the rock,And they hear my words, that they are pleasant.
As when one plows and breaks up the earth,Our bones are scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
For my eyes are upon You, O Jehovah Lord;In You I [a]take refuge; do not pour out my soul.
Keep me from the trap they have set for meAnd from the snares of the workers of iniquity.
May the wicked [1]fall into their own netsWhile I alone pass by.Psa 142:Title A [*]Maschil [a]of David, when he was in the [b]cave. A Prayer

142.text: With my voice I cry to Jehovah;With my voice I make my supplication to Jehovah.
I pour out my complaint before Him;I declare my trouble before Him.
When my spirit fainted within me,You knew my path.On the way on which I walkedThey have hidden a trap for me.
Look on my right hand and see,For there is no one who regards me;Refuge has failed me;No one cares for me.
I cried to You, O Jehovah;I said, You are my [a]refuge,My [b]portion in the land of the living.
Give heed to my cry,For I have been brought very low;Deliver me from those who persecute me,For they are stronger than I.
Bring my soul out of prison,That I may give thanks to Your name.The righteous will surround me,For You will deal bountifully with me.Psa 143:Title A [a]Psalm of David

143.text: O Jehovah, hear my prayer;Give ear to my supplications;Answer me in Your faithfulness and in Your righteousness;
And do not enter into judgment with Your servant,For [a]no one alive is righteous in Your sight.
For the enemy has persecuted my soul;He has crushed my life down to the ground;He has caused me to dwell in dark places, like those who have been long dead.
Therefore my spirit faints within me;My heart is appalled in the midst of me.
[a]I remember the days of old;I meditate upon all that You have done;I muse upon the work of Your hands.
I spread out my hands to You;My soul [a]thirsts for You like a parched land. Selah
Hurry and answer me, O Jehovah;My spirit fails.Do not hide Your face from me,Lest I become like those who go down into the pit.
Cause me to hear Your [a]lovingkindness in the morning,For in You I trust.Make known to me the way on which I should walk,For to You I lift up my soul.
Deliver me from my enemies, O Jehovah;[1]I have fled to You for a [a]refuge.
Teach me to do Your will,For You are my God;Let Your [a]good Spirit lead meIn a plain country.
For Your name’s sake, O Jehovah, preserve me alive;In Your righteousness bring my soul out of trouble.
And in Your lovingkindness [1]cut off my enemiesAnd destroy all those who oppress my soul,For I am Your [a]servant.Psa 144:Title [a]Of David

144.text: Blessed be Jehovah my [a]rock,Who teaches my hands for warAnd my fingers for battle:
My Lord of lovingkindness and my fortress,My [a]high retreat and my Deliverer,My shield and He in whom I [b]take refuge,Who subdues my people under me.
O Jehovah, [a]what is man, that You take knowledge of him,And the son of mortal man, that You think of him?
Man is like a [a]breath;His days are like a passing shadow.
O Jehovah, bow Your heavens down and descend;Touch the mountains, and they will smoke.
Flash forth lightning, and scatter them;Send forth Your arrows, and discomfit them.
Stretch forth Your hand from on high;Rescue me and deliver meFrom the great waters,From the hand of foreigners;
Whose mouth speaks deceit,And whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
O God, I will sing a [a]new song to You;Upon a harp of ten strings I will sing psalms to You,
Who gives salvation to kings,Who [a]rescues David His servant from the hurtful sword.
Rescue me and deliver me from the hand of foreigners,Whose mouth speaks deceit,And whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
[1]May our sons be like [a]plantsGrown up in their youth;May our daughters be like corner pillarsHewn out in fashion for a palace;
May our barns be full,Furnishing produce of all kinds;May our sheep bring forth thousandsAnd ten thousands in our fields;
May our oxen be fully laden;May there be no breaking in to rob or going forth to fight,Or outcry in our streets.
Blessed are the people who are so;[a]Blessed are the people whose God is Jehovah.Psa 145:Title A Song of Praise. [a]Of David

145.text: [1]I will [2]extol You, O my God and [a]King;And I will bless Your name forever and ever.
Every day I will bless You,And I will praise Your name forever and ever.
[a]Great is Jehovah, and much to be praised;And His greatness is unsearchable.
One generation will laud Your works to another,And they will declare Your mighty acts.
Upon the glorious splendor of Your majestyAnd upon Your wondrous works I will muse.
And men will speak of the might of Your awesome deeds,And I will declare Your greatness.
They will utter the memory of Your great goodnessAnd will sing for joy of Your righteousness.
[a]Jehovah is gracious and compassionate,Long-suffering and great in lovingkindness.
Jehovah is good to all,And His compassions are upon all His works.
All Your works will praise You, O Jehovah;And Your faithful ones will bless You.
They will speak of the glory of Your kingdomAnd tell of Your power,
To make known to the sons of men [1]Your mighty actsAnd the glorious splendor of [1]Your kingdom.
Your kingdom is an [a]eternal kingdom,And Your dominion is throughout all generations.
Jehovah upholds all who are fallingAnd raises up all who are bowed down.
The eyes of everyone wait for You,And You give them their [a]food in due season.
You open up Your handAnd satisfy the desire of every living thing.
Jehovah is [a]righteous in all His ways,And faithfully kind in all His doings.
Jehovah is [a]near to all who [b]call upon Him,To all who call upon Him in truth.
He will fulfill the desire of those who fear Him,And He will hear their cry and save them.
Jehovah preserves all who love Him,But He will [1]destroy all the wicked.
My mouth will speak the praise of Jehovah,And all flesh will bless His holy name forever and ever.

146.text: [a]I will praise Jehovah while I live;I will sing psalms to my God while I yet have being.
Do not trust in [1a]princes,In a son of man, with whom there is no salvation.
His breath goes forth; he returns to his earth;On that day his plans perish.
Blessed is the man whose [a]help is the God of Jacob,Whose hope is in Jehovah his God,
Who [a]made heaven and earthAnd the sea, and all that is in them;Who keeps faith forever;
Who executes judgment for the oppressed;Who gives food to the famished.Jehovah [a]frees the prisoners;
Jehovah [a]opens the eyes of the blind;Jehovah raises up those who are bowed down;Jehovah loves the righteous;
Jehovah preserves the sojourners;He upholds the orphan and the widow;But He subverts the way of the wicked.
Jehovah will [a]reign forever,Your God, O Zion, [1]forever and ever.Hallelujah!

147.text: [1a]Hallelujah!For it is good to sing psalms to our God;For it is pleasant; praise is fitting.
Jehovah [a]builds up Jerusalem;He [b]gathers the [1]outcasts of Israel.
He heals the [a]brokenheartedAnd binds up their wounds.
He counts the number of the [a]stars;He calls them all by their names.
[a]Great is our Lord, and mighty in power;His understanding is infinite.
Jehovah [a]uplifts the lowly;He brings the [1]wicked down to the ground.
[a]Sing unto Jehovah with thanksgiving;Sing psalms to our God with the lyre;
Who covers the heavens with clouds,Who prepares rain for the earth,Who causes grass to grow on the mountains,
Who gives to the beast its food,And to the young [a]ravens who cry.
He takes no delight in the strength of horses;Nor pleasure in the legs of man.
Jehovah takes pleasure in those who fear Him,In those who hope in His lovingkindness.
[a]Laud Jehovah, O Jerusalem;Praise your God, O Zion;
For He has strengthened the bars of your gates;He has blessed your children within you.
He makes peace in your borders;He satisfies you with the finest wheat.
He sends forth His command to the earth;His word [a]runs very swiftly.
He gives snow like wool;He scatters frost like ashes;
He casts His hail like crumbs—Who can stand before His cold?
He sends forth His word and melts them;He causes His wind to blow; the waters flow.
He [a]declares His word to Jacob,His statutes and His ordinances to Israel.
He has not dealt so with any other nation;And as for His ordinances, they have not known them.Hallelujah!

148.text: [1a]Hallelujah!Praise Jehovah from the heavens;Praise Him in the [b]heights.
Praise Him, all His [a]angels;Praise Him, all His hosts.
Praise Him, [a]sun and moon;Praise Him, all stars of light.
Praise Him, you [a]heaven of heavens,And you [b]waters that are above the heavens.
Let them praise the name of Jehovah;For He [a]commanded, and they were created.
And He established them forever and ever;He gave the decree which shall not pass away.
Praise Jehovah from the earth,You [a]sea monsters and all the depths;
Fire and hail, snow and smoke;Stormy wind that executes His word;
Mountains and all hills;Fruit trees and all cedars;
Animals wild and tame;Creeping things and birds of flight;
Kings of the earth and all peoples;Princes and all judges of the earth;
Choice young men as well as virgins;Old men with young men.
Let them praise the name of Jehovah,For His name alone is exalted;His glory is above the earth and the heavens.
And He has raised up a [a]horn for His people,The praise of all His faithful ones,The children of Israel, a people [b]near to Him.Hallelujah!

149.text: [1a]Hallelujah!Sing a [b]new song to Jehovah;Sing His praise in the congregation of His faithful ones.
Let Israel rejoice in his Maker;Let the children of [a]Zion exult in their [b]King.
Let them praise His name with dancing;Let them sing psalms to Him with the tambourine and the lyre.
For Jehovah takes pleasure in His people;He [a]adorns the lowly with salvation.
Let the faithful ones exult in glory;Let them give a ringing shout upon their beds.
Let the high praises of God be in their throats,And a two-edged sword in their hand,
To execute vengeance on the nationsAnd punishment among the peoples;
To bind their kings with fettersAnd their nobles with chains of iron;
To execute upon them the judgment written.This honor is for all His faithful ones.Hallelujah!

150.text: [1a]Hallelujah!Praise God in His [b]sanctuary;Praise Him in the expanse that manifests His power.
Praise Him for His mighty acts;Praise Him according to His vast greatness.
Praise Him with the blast of the trumpet;Praise Him with the harp and lyre;
Praise Him with the tambourine and with dancing;Praise Him with the stringed instrument and the pipe.
Praise Him with the loud cymbals;Praise Him with the loud clanging cymbals.
Let [a]everything that has breath praise [1]Jehovah.Hallelujah!Book of PsalmsHome

paragraphs: Or, Happy. Psalms is not a book of doctrines or of any kind of teaching. It is a book of divine revelation composed of the expressions, sentiments, feelings, impressions, and experiences of godly men concerning (1) God and the way He is in His dealing with them; (2) the law of God as the holy Word with the divine revelation; (3) the house of God, the temple, and Mount Zion, on which the temple was seated, as the center of God’s dwelling place on earth; (4) the holy city of God, Jerusalem, as the encompassing protection of the house of God; (5) the holy people of God, Israel, as God’s beloved elect among the nations; (6) Israel’s love toward God, their fellowship with God, their blessings received of God, their sufferings under God’s dealings, and their environment; (7) their captivity; and (8) their thanksgivings and praises to Jehovah their God, whom they tasted and enjoyed. Through the godly expressions of the psalmists Christ is revealed and the church as God’s house and God’s city is typified.

The Psalms were written according to two kinds of concepts: the human concept of the holy writers and the divine concept of God. The human concept of the holy writers was produced out of their good nature created by God, formed with the traditions of their holy race, constituted with the teachings of the Holy Scriptures, promoted by their practice of a holy life, and uttered out of their holy sentiments and impressions. The divine concept of God as the divine revelation in the Psalms concerns three major items: (1) God’s eternal economy, of which Christ is the centrality and universality; (2) Christ in His divinity, humanity, human living, all-inclusive death, life-imparting and church-producing resurrection, glorification, ascension, appearing in glory, and reigning forever; and (3) God’s heart’s desire, His good pleasure, in Christ as His centrality and universality, in the church as His fullness for His expression, in the kingdom for His eternal administration, and in the recovery of the earth for His eternal kingdom in eternity. In the Psalms only what is written out of the divine concept of God, and not what is written out of the human concept of the psalmists, should be considered as part of the divine revelation from God concerning His divine economy.

According to the divine concept, the central thought of the book of Psalms is Christ, as revealed in plain words (Luke 24:44), and the church as the house of God and the city of God for His kingdom, as typified by the temple and by the city of Jerusalem. The spirit, the reality, the characteristic, of the divine revelation in the book of Psalms is Christ as the centrality and universality of the eternal economy of God. For this, Christ is first the embodiment of the Triune God (Col. 2:9), then the house, the habitation, of God (signified by the temple—John 2:19-21), the kingdom of God (signified by the city of Jerusalem—Luke 17:21; Rev. 22:3b), and the Ruler of the entire earth from the house of God and in the kingdom of God (Dan. 2:34-35). Thus, He is all in all in the entire universe (cf. Eph. 1:23; Col. 3:11). Such a divine revelation is the same as what is revealed in the entire Holy Scriptures. The only particular point related to the divine revelation in the book of Psalms is that such a high revelation, even the highest peak of the divine revelation, is prophesied in the expressions of the sentiments of the ancient godly saints. Thus, it is mixed with their comfort in sufferings and with the cultivation of godliness. The consummation of this highest divine revelation is the city of New Jerusalem as a sign of the habitation, the tabernacle, of God (Rev. 21:1-3), through which the processed and consummated Triune God will be manifested and expressed in the all-inclusive Christ and will reign on the new earth in the new universe for eternity.

Psalms 1 and 2 portray a contrast between the human concept and the divine revelation. The human concept exalts the law and the law-keeper as one blessed by God, whereas the divine revelation proclaims Christ as the One anointed of God in God’s economy. In Psa. 1 the psalmist, according to his human, ethical concept, appreciated and uplifted the law to the uttermost. This is contrary to the divine concept in God’s New Testament economy. In its nature the law is holy, righteous, good, and spiritual (Rom. 7:12, 14a), but in its position, the law is not in the main line of God’s economy; rather, it entered in alongside the main line of God’s economy (Rom. 5:20a). God created man in His image and according to His likeness (Gen. 1:26) with the intention that man would receive God as life in the tree of life (Gen. 2:8-9, 16-17) so that man could live God and express God. This is the main line of God’s economy. But while this main line was proceeding, Satan came in to deceive man and constitute man with sin (Gen. 3:1-6; Rom. 5:19). In order to continue His purpose with man after man’s fall, God added the law to the main line of His economy (Gal. 3:19 and notes 1 and 2). According to Gal. 4:24-25 (see note 245 there), the position of the law is that of a concubine. A concubine does not have an orthodox position. Thus, to appreciate and uplift the law is to bring a “concubine” into God’s economy. This is absolutely against God’s ordination.

The entire book of Psalms presents a contrast between the law and Christ. Only Christ Himself, not the law, can make us members of Christ and conform us to Christ that we may be His complement (Eph. 5:30-32; Rom. 8:29). The spirit of the Scriptures does not exalt anything or anyone other than Christ (Matt. 17:1-5; Phil. 3:7-8; Col. 1:15-19). Eventually, by the end of the Psalms the only thing that remains is Christ with His complement, which is His Body, His church, the house and kingdom of God in God’s economy to fulfill God’s eternal purpose.

In the book of Psalms, as in the entire Bible, there are two lines: the line of the tree of life, the line of life, and the line of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, the line of death (Gen. 2:8-9, 16-17 and note 93, par. 2). In addition to these two lines, the line of the law runs alongside the line of life. God’s purpose in giving the law was to lead His people to Christ (Gal. 3:23-24), i.e., to the line of life (see note 11 in Exo. 25). However, if the law as the side line is exalted improperly, it becomes part of the line of the tree of knowledge, the line of death (Rom. 7:7-11; 2 Cor. 3:6b). God has ordained Christ to be the centrality and universality of His economy to fulfill His good pleasure (Eph. 1:9-10; 3:8-11). The law has been ended by Christ (Rom. 10:4a), and the believers are no longer under the law (Rom. 6:14).

n1_3x1.text: Psa 1:31 prospers The human concept here is that the man who delights in the law of God prospers in everything, whereas the wicked man suffers loss (vv. 4-5). But the experience of the psalmist in Psa. 73 was the opposite (73:1-7, 12-14). This seeming contradiction troubled the psalmist until he was instructed, in the sanctuary of God, to take only God Himself as his portion, not anything other than God (73:16-17, 25-26). According to the Lord’s teaching in the New Testament, those who are persecuted for the sake of righteousness (not those who are prosperous) are blessed (Matt. 5:10).
